  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    server: https://  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    client-certificate  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    name: prod  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      prod:6443       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    client-key          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    cluster:    в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    certificate-      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      prod     в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      authority       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  - user: dev-admin     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    user:      в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    token: eyJhb...     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      prod-    в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  - cluster: dev      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      admin    в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    server: https://  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  - user: staging-      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    namespace: в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      dev:6443        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      readonly          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      default  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    certificate-      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    token: abc123...    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      authority       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  - context:   в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    name: dev  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  - cluster: staging  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    cluster:   в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    server: https://  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      dev     в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      staging:6443    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    user:     в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      dev-    в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ      admin  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ             в”Ӯ                           в”Ӯ              в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ             в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                     в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                         в–ј  в–ј                                  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  current-context: prod  в—Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                       в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
~/.kube/config 
apiVersion: v1
kind: Config

# The active context (used when you run kubectl)
current-context: production

# WHERE to connect
clusters:
- cluster:
    certificate-authority-data: LS0tLS1...   # Base64 CA cert
    server: https://192.168.56.10:6443
  name: production

- cluster:
    certificate-authority-data: LS0tLS1...
    server: https://10.0.0.5:6443
  name: development

# WHO to authenticate as
users:
- name: prod-admin
  user:
    client-certificate-data: LS0tLS1...   # Base64 client cert
    client-key-data: LS0tLS1...           # Base64 client key

- name: dev-admin
  user:
    token: eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIs...        # Bearer token

# WHICH cluster + user combination to use
contexts:
- context:
    cluster: production
    user: prod-admin
    namespace: default
  name: production

- context:
    cluster: development
    user: dev-admin
    namespace: dev-apps
  name: development

kubeconfig вҖ” Field-by-Field Breakdown

рҹ“Қ clusters[].server
API server URL. Format: https://<ip>:6443. Port 6443 is the default.
рҹ”җ clusters[].certificate-authority-data
Base64-encoded CA certificate. Kubectl uses this to verify the API server's TLS certificate.
рҹ‘Ө users[].client-certificate-data
Base64-encoded client cert. The API server verifies this to authenticate the user.
рҹ”— contexts[].cluster + user + namespace
A context links a cluster and user together, plus an optional default namespace.
вӯҗ current-context
Which context kubectl uses right now. Changed via kubectl config use-context <name>

10.2 Managing Multiple Clusters & Contexts

On the CKA exam, you'll work with multiple clusters вҖ” each question tells you which context to use. Switching contexts fast is critical.

context-commands.sh 
# View all contexts
kubectl config get-contexts
# CURRENT   NAME          CLUSTER       AUTHINFO      NAMESPACE
# *         production    production    prod-admin    default
#           development   development   dev-admin     dev-apps
#           staging       staging       stg-viewer    staging

# Switch to a different context
kubectl config use-context development
# Switched to context "development".

# View current context
kubectl config current-context
# development

# Set default namespace for current context
kubectl config set-context --current --namespace=kube-system
# Context "development" modified.

# View the full kubeconfig
kubectl config view
# (displays the merged kubeconfig YAML)

# View just one cluster's details
kubectl config view --minify
# (shows only the active context's config)
рҹҶ•
Create a New Context
create-context.sh 
# Add a new cluster
kubectl config set-cluster prod-cluster \
  --server=https://10.0.1.5:6443 \
  --certificate-authority=/path/to/ca.crt

# Add a new user
kubectl config set-credentials jane \
  --client-certificate=/path/to/jane.crt \
  --client-key=/path/to/jane.key

# Create a context linking them
kubectl config set-context jane@prod \
  --cluster=prod-cluster \
  --user=jane \
  --namespace=web-apps
рҹ“Ғ
KUBECONFIG Env Variable
kubeconfig-env.sh 
# Use a specific kubeconfig file
export KUBECONFIG=/path/to/custom-config

# Merge multiple kubeconfig files
export KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/config:\
  ~/.kube/prod-config:\
  ~/.kube/dev-config

# View merged result
kubectl config view --flatten

# One-time override (no env change)
kubectl --kubeconfig=/path/to/config \
  get nodes
рҹ’Ў CKA Shortcut: First Command on Every Question

Every CKA exam question starts with a context switch instruction. Make this muscle memory:
kubectl config use-context k8s-cluster-1
If you forget this step and work on the wrong cluster, you'll lose points for that question.

10.3 API Server Configuration

The kube-apiserver is the front door to the entire cluster. Understanding its flags helps you troubleshoot and is tested on the CKA.

API Server вҖ” Static Pod Configuration
  /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  apiVersion: v1                                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  kind: Pod                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  metadata:                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    name: kube-apiserver                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    namespace: kube-system                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  spec:                                                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    containers:                                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    - command:                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      - kube-apiserver                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      - --advertise-address=192.168.56.10                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      - --etcd-servers=https://127.0.0.1:2379                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      - --service-cluster-ip-range=10.96.0.0/12                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      - --client-ca-file=/etc/kubernetes/pki/ca.crt             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      - --tls-cert-file=/etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      - --tls-private-key-file=/etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.keyв”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      - --enable-admission-plugins=NodeRestriction              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      - --authorization-mode=Node,RBAC                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      - --enable-bootstrap-token-auth=true                      в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                          в”Ӯ
                  kubelet watches this file
                  and auto-restarts the pod
                  when it changes

Key API Server Flags вҖ” CKA Must-Know

рҹҸ  --etcd-servers
Where the API server stores all cluster data. Default: https://127.0.0.1:2379
рҹҢҗ --service-cluster-ip-range
CIDR for ClusterIP Services. Default: 10.96.0.0/12. Must not overlap pod CIDR.
рҹ”’ --authorization-mode
Node,RBAC is the default. Order matters вҖ” first match wins. Always include RBAC.
рҹӣЎпёҸ --enable-admission-plugins
Comma-separated list of admission controllers. NodeRestriction is critical for security.
рҹ”‘ --client-ca-file
CA certificate for verifying client certificates (mutual TLS). Located in /etc/kubernetes/pki/
вҡ пёҸ Static Pod = Auto-Restart on Change

The API server runs as a static pod. If you edit /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml, kubelet automatically restarts it. But a bad edit will crash the API server вҖ” always backup the manifest first: cp kube-apiserver.yaml kube-apiserver.yaml.bak

10.4 kubelet Configuration

The kubelet runs on every node. It's the agent that talks to the API server, manages pods, reports node status, and runs static pods. Unlike other control plane components, kubelet is NOT a pod вҖ” it's a systemd service.

kubelet вҖ” Configuration File Locations
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ kubelet Configuration Paths в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/10-kubeadm.conf          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җ Environment flags passed to kubelet binary                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      в””в”Җв”Җ --config=/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml        вҶҗ Main config file         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ clusterDNS: ["10.96.0.10"]                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ clusterDomain: cluster.local                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ staticPodPath: /etc/kubernetes/manifests   вҶҗ Static pods!  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ authentication:                                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ x509: clientCAFile: /etc/kubernetes/pki/ca.crt         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ authorization:                                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ mode: Webhook                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җ cgroupDriver: systemd          вҶҗ Must match containerd     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf        вҶҗ kubelet's kubeconfig     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җ server, client-certificate, client-key                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  /etc/kubernetes/manifests/          вҶҗ Static pod manifests     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ etcd.yaml                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ kube-apiserver.yaml                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ kube-controller-manager.yaml                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җ kube-scheduler.yaml                                        в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
kubelet-commands.sh 
# Check kubelet status
sudo systemctl status kubelet
# в—Ҹ kubelet.service - kubelet: The Kubernetes Node Agent
#    Active: active (running) since...

# View kubelet logs (troubleshooting)
sudo journalctl -u kubelet -f
# (live log stream вҖ” look for errors here)

# Restart kubelet (after config changes)
sudo systemctl restart kubelet

# View kubelet config
cat /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml

# Static pods: drop a manifest in /etc/kubernetes/manifests/
# kubelet will auto-create the pod (no API server needed!)
cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/kubernetes/manifests/static-nginx.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: static-nginx
  namespace: default
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
    image: nginx:1.25
    ports:
    - containerPort: 80
EOF

# Verify static pod exists
kubectl get pods | grep static-nginx
# static-nginx-control-plane   1/1   Running   0   10s
рҹ“– Static Pods vs Regular Pods

Static pods are managed directly by kubelet, not the API server. They are created from YAML files in /etc/kubernetes/manifests/. The API server sees a mirror copy but cannot control them. All control plane components (etcd, apiserver, scheduler, controller-manager) run as static pods.

10.5 Admission Controllers

Admission controllers sit in the API request pipeline after authentication and authorization but before the object is persisted. They can validate, mutate, or reject requests.

API Request Pipeline вҖ” Where Admission Controllers Sit
  kubectl apply -f pod.yaml
       в”Ӯ
       в–ј
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ 1. AUTHENTICATIONв”Ӯв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ 2. AUTHORIZATION  в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ 3. ADMISSION CONTROLLERS в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ WHO are you?     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ CAN you do this?  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ SHOULD we allow this?    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ вҖў x509 certs     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў RBAC            в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ вҖў Bearer tokens  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Node            в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ вҖў ServiceAccount в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Webhook         в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ в”Ӯ Mutating Webhooks  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ в”Ӯ (modify request)   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ
                                                 в”Ӯ           в–ј            в”Ӯ
                                                 в”Ӯ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җв”Ӯ
                                                 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ Validating Webhooks в”Ӯв”Ӯ
                                                 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ (accept or reject) в”Ӯв”Ӯ
                                                 в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳв”Ӯ
                                                 в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                                                              в–ј
                                                 в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                                                 в”Ӯ 4. PERSIST TO etcd   в”Ӯ
                                                 в”Ӯ (object is stored)   в”Ӯ
                                                 в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
Admission Controller Type What It Does CKA?
NamespaceLifecycle Validating Prevents creating objects in non-existent or terminating namespaces вң…
LimitRanger Mutating Enforces LimitRange defaults вҖ” auto-sets resource requests/limits вң…
ResourceQuota Validating Rejects requests that exceed namespace ResourceQuota вң…
NodeRestriction Validating Limits kubelet to only modify its own node and pods вң…
DefaultStorageClass Mutating Adds default StorageClass to PVCs that don't specify one рҹ”№
ServiceAccount Mutating Injects the default ServiceAccount into pods that don't specify one вң…
admission-controller-commands.sh 
# View enabled admission plugins
kubectl -n kube-system describe pod kube-apiserver-control-plane \
  | grep enable-admission
#   --enable-admission-plugins=NodeRestriction

# To enable additional plugins, edit the API server manifest:
sudo vi /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml
# Change:
#   --enable-admission-plugins=NodeRestriction
# To:
#   --enable-admission-plugins=NodeRestriction,NamespaceLifecycle,LimitRanger

# kubelet will auto-restart the API server
# Verify the API server comes back up
kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep apiserver
рҹ’Ў CKA Tip: Enabling Admission Controllers

If a CKA question asks you to enable an admission controller, you edit /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml, add it to the --enable-admission-plugins flag, save the file, and wait for the API server pod to restart automatically.

10.6 API Groups and Resource Paths

Every Kubernetes resource belongs to an API group. Understanding this helps you write RBAC rules, use kubectl api-resources, and debug API calls.

Kubernetes API Group Hierarchy
  /api/v1  (Core / Legacy group вҖ” no group name in apiVersion)
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ pods                  apiVersion: v1
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ services              apiVersion: v1
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ configmaps            apiVersion: v1
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ secrets               apiVersion: v1
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ namespaces            apiVersion: v1
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ nodes                 apiVersion: v1
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ persistentvolumes     apiVersion: v1
  в””в”Җв”Җ serviceaccounts       apiVersion: v1

  /apis/apps/v1  (Named group: "apps")
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ deployments           apiVersion: apps/v1
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ statefulsets           apiVersion: apps/v1
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ daemonsets             apiVersion: apps/v1
  в””в”Җв”Җ replicasets            apiVersion: apps/v1

  /apis/networking.k8s.io/v1
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ ingresses             apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
  в””в”Җв”Җ networkpolicies       apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1

  /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ roles                 apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ rolebindings          apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ clusterroles          apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
  в””в”Җв”Җ clusterrolebindings   apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1

  /apis/batch/v1
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ jobs                  apiVersion: batch/v1
  в””в”Җв”Җ cronjobs              apiVersion: batch/v1

  /apis/storage.k8s.io/v1
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ storageclasses        apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
  в””в”Җв”Җ persistentvolumeclaims apiVersion: v1  (core group!)
api-groups-commands.sh 
# List ALL available API resources
kubectl api-resources
# NAME                  SHORTNAMES   APIVERSION          NAMESPACED   KIND
# pods                  po           v1                  true         Pod
# services              svc          v1                  true         Service
# deployments           deploy       apps/v1             true         Deployment
# nodes                 no           v1                  false        Node

# Filter by API group
kubectl api-resources --api-group=apps
# NAME           SHORTNAMES   APIVERSION   NAMESPACED   KIND
# deployments    deploy       apps/v1      true         Deployment
# statefulsets   sts          apps/v1      true         StatefulSet

# List API versions
kubectl api-versions
# apps/v1
# batch/v1
# networking.k8s.io/v1
# rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# v1  вҶҗ core group

# Explain a resource (shows API group and version)
kubectl explain deployment
# KIND:     Deployment
# VERSION:  apps/v1
# DESCRIPTION: ...
рҹ“– Core Group vs Named Groups

Core group resources (Pod, Service, ConfigMap, Secret) use apiVersion: v1 вҖ” no group prefix. Named group resources use apiVersion: groupName/v1 вҖ” e.g., apps/v1 for Deployments. When writing RBAC rules, core group resources use apiGroups: [""] (empty string).

рҹ”Ң Bonus: Control Plane Component Communication Map

Understanding how control plane components talk to each other is essential for debugging and CKA troubleshooting. Every arrow below represents a real network connection.

Control Plane Architecture вҖ” Who Talks to Whom
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                        CONTROL PLANE NODE                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ        в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  kube-scheduler  в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ      kube-apiserver       в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ  HTTPS в”Ӯ  (Central communication   в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ "Where should    в”Ӯ  :6443 в”Ӯ   hub вҖ” ALL components    в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  this pod run?"  в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ   talk through me)        в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ        в”Ӯ                           в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ  Ports:                  в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ        в”Ӯ   6443 вҖ” HTTPS API       в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ kube-controller- в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ                           в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ manager          в”Ӯ  HTTPS в”Ӯ                           в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ  :6443 в”Ӯ                           в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ "Are actual and  в”Ӯ        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  desired state   в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  matching?"      в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ HTTPS :2379                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                  в–ј                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ      etcd         в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ  (All state storedв”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ   here вҖ” ONLY the в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ   API server talks в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ   to etcd!)        в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ  Ports:           в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ   2379 вҖ” client   в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ   2380 вҖ” peer     в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                      в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                                        в”Ӯ
                          в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                          в–ј             в–ј             в–ј
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                         WORKER NODES                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ     kubelet       в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ   kube-proxy     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   CNI Plugin   в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  (e.g. Calico) в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ Talks to API     в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ Watches API      в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ server to:       в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ server for       в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ Manages pod    в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Report status  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ Service/Endpoint в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ networking and в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Get pod specs  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ changes, updates в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ network policy в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Send events    в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ iptables/IPVS    в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ enforcement    в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ                                                                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      в–ј                                                                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   containerd     в”Ӯ  kubelet вҶ’ containerd via CRI (Container          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   (container     в”Ӯ  Runtime Interface) to create/stop containers     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ    runtime)      в”Ӯ                                                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                                   в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  KEY RULE: Only the API server talks to etcd. Everything else talks to the API server.

рҹ“Ӣ Bonus: KUBECONFIG Merge Priority Order

When multiple kubeconfig sources exist, kubectl merges them in a specific precedence order. Knowing this prevents "wrong cluster" mistakes.

kubeconfig Resolution вҖ” Priority Order (Highest вҶ’ Lowest)
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  PRIORITY 1 (Highest)                                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  --kubeconfig flag                                       в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  kubectl --kubeconfig=/path/to/config get pods           в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вҶ’ Uses ONLY this file, ignores everything else          в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ if not set                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в–ј                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  PRIORITY 2                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  $KUBECONFIG environment variable                        в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  export KUBECONFIG=/a/config:/b/config:/c/config         в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вҶ’ Merges ALL files (colon-separated on Linux)           в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вҶ’ First context/cluster/user wins on conflict           в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ if not set                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в–ј                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  PRIORITY 3 (Default)                                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  $HOME/.kube/config                                      в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вҶ’ The default kubeconfig file                           в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вҶ’ Created by kubeadm init (admin.conf copied here)      в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

вҡЎ Bonus: kubectl config вҖ” CKA Speed Reference

Key commands you'll use on exam day, organized for quick recall.

Task Command When to Use
Switch context kubectl config use-context <name> Start of every CKA question
See current context kubectl config current-context Verify you're on the right cluster
List all contexts kubectl config get-contexts Find available cluster names
Set default namespace kubectl config set-context --current --namespace=X Avoid typing -n X repeatedly
View active config kubectl config view --minify Debug connection issues
Flatten merged configs kubectl config view --flatten Export a merged config to a single file
Check API server URL kubectl cluster-info Verify cluster endpoint
Test connectivity kubectl get nodes Confirm kubectl can reach the cluster
рҹ’Ў Pro Tip: Alias for Speed

The CKA exam pre-configures alias k=kubectl. You can also add: alias kgc='kubectl config get-contexts' and alias kuc='kubectl config use-context' to shave seconds off every question.

вҡ пёҸ CKA Gotcha: kubeconfig & Configuration Traps

  • KUBECONFIG env var вҖ” The exam may set KUBECONFIG=/path/to/config. If your commands fail, check: echo $KUBECONFIG and kubectl config view
  • Multiple kubeconfig files вҖ” Merge with KUBECONFIG=file1:file2 kubectl config view --flatten. The exam may provide separate configs per cluster
  • Admission controllers вҖ” Enabled in kube-apiserver.yaml via --enable-admission-plugins. The exam may ask you to enable/disable one вҖ” edit the static pod manifest
  • API groups matter вҖ” v1 = core group (pods, services). apps/v1 = apps group (deployments). rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1 = RBAC. Wrong group = object won't create

CKA Speed Tip: Quickly check API server flags without opening the manifest:
kubectl -n kube-system get pod kube-apiserver-controlplane -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].command}' | tr ',' '\n'
This lists every flag the API server is running with.

вҡЎ Config & context commands: kubectl config view --minify вҖ” show only current context details
kubectl config set-credentials user --client-certificate=cert.crt --client-key=key.pem вҖ” add user
kubectl config set-context myctx --cluster=mycluster --user=myuser --namespace=default вҖ” create context
kubectl api-versions вҖ” list all available API versions

Chapter 11: etcd вҖ” The Cluster Database

etcd is the single source of truth for your entire Kubernetes cluster. Every object вҖ” every pod, service, configmap, secret вҖ” is stored in etcd. Lose etcd, lose your cluster. That's why etcd backup is one of the most important admin tasks and is guaranteed to appear on the CKA exam.

рҹҹЎ Intermediate вҸұпёҸ ~1.5 hours рҹ’ҫ etcd рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

рҹҺҜ What You'll Learn

  • вң… Understand etcd's role вҖ” what it stores and why it's critical
  • вң… Know etcd's architecture and the Raft consensus algorithm
  • вң… Backup etcd using etcdctl snapshot save (CKA guaranteed question)
  • вң… Restore etcd from a backup snapshot
  • вң… Secure etcd with TLS certificates and access control
рҹҺ“ CKA Relevance
Cluster Architecture, Installation & Configuration 25%
etcd Backup = GUARANTEED CKA Question

Almost every CKA exam includes an etcd backup and/or restore question. This is worth significant points. Memorize the backup command and the certificate paths.

  • Memorize: ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot save with all 3 cert flags
  • Know the cert paths: /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/
  • Practice restore blindfolded: stop etcd вҶ’ restore snapshot вҶ’ update data-dir вҶ’ restart

11.1 What is etcd?

etcd is a distributed, reliable key-value store that Kubernetes uses to store all cluster state. Every kubectl apply, every pod creation, every secret вҖ” all stored in etcd.

рҹҸӣпёҸ
Key-Value Store

Stores data as key-value pairs, similar to a dictionary. Example: /registry/pods/default/nginx вҶ’ pod YAML.

рҹ”„
Distributed

Runs on multiple nodes (3 or 5 for HA) and replicates data using the Raft consensus algorithm.

рҹ”’
Strongly Consistent

All reads return the latest write. No stale data ever вҖ” critical for cluster state correctness.

рҹ“Ҡ
Watch API

Supports efficient watchers. The API server subscribes to changes вҖ” this powers the entire controller pattern.

What etcd Stores вҖ” The Kubernetes Registry
  etcd Key Space (all under /registry/)
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  /registry/                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ pods/                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ default/                                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ nginx-deployment-abc123     вҶҗ Pod spec + status    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ jobpost-web-xyz789          вҶҗ Our anihpj app!      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ kube-system/                                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ coredns-7db6d8ff4d-xxx      вҶҗ System pods          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ calico-node-yyy                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ deployments/                                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ default/                                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ nginx-deployment            вҶҗ Deployment spec      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ services/                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ default/kubernetes              вҶҗ The kubernetes svc   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ default/jobpost-service         вҶҗ Our app service      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ secrets/                                                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ default/db-credentials          вҶҗ вҡ пёҸ Stored in etcd!   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ configmaps/                                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ namespaces/                                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ nodes/                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ clusterroles/                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җ ... (every K8s object type)                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ң Size: Typically 2-8 GB for a large production cluster       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ң Default port: 2379 (client) / 2380 (peer)                  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
рҹҡЁ etcd Contains Secrets in Plain Text

By default, Kubernetes Secrets are stored unencrypted in etcd. Anyone with direct access to etcd can read all secrets. Enable encryption at rest (covered in Section 11.5) to protect sensitive data.

11.2 etcd Architecture & Raft Consensus

In production/HA clusters, etcd runs on 3 or 5 nodes. They elect a leader using the Raft consensus algorithm, ensuring data consistency even if nodes fail.

Raft Consensus вҖ” Leader Election & Replication
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ etcd Cluster (3 Members) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                                                                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ   etcd Member 1  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   etcd Member 2  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   etcd Member 3  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ   вҳ… LEADER       в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   FOLLOWER       в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   FOLLOWER       в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Log: v101  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Log: v101  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Log: v101 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Log: v102  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Log: v102  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Log: v102 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Log: v103  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Log: v103  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Log: v103 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ   Port: 2379     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   Port: 2379     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   Port: 2379    в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ   Peer:  2380    в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   Peer:  2380    в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   Peer:  2380   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    вң… Write: Client вҶ’ Leader вҶ’ Replicate to majority вҶ’ ACK            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    вң… Read:  Client вҶ’ Any member (leader or follower)                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    вң… Quorum: 2 of 3 must agree (tolerates 1 failure)                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    Quorum formula: (N/2) + 1                                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ Members в”Ӯ   Quorum   в”Ӯ Fault Toleranceв”Ӯ    Use Case      в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ    1    в”Ӯ     1      в”Ӯ       0        в”Ӯ Development      в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ    3    в”Ӯ     2      в”Ӯ       1        в”Ӯ Small production в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ    5    в”Ӯ     3      в”Ӯ       2        в”Ӯ Large production в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ    7    в”Ӯ     4      в”Ӯ       3        в”Ӯ Rare (overhead)  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ       в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
вңҚпёҸClient Write
вҶ’
вҳ…Leader Receives
вҶ’
рҹ“ӨReplicate Log
вҶ’
вң…Majority ACK
вҶ’
рҹ’ҫCommit & Respond
рҹ“– Why Odd Numbers? (3 or 5, Never 4 or 6)

A 3-member cluster tolerates 1 failure (quorum = 2). A 4-member cluster also tolerates only 1 failure (quorum = 3). So 4 members gives no advantage over 3 but adds network overhead. Always use odd numbers.

11.3 Backing Up etcd (CKA Must-Know)

This is the #1 admin task for cluster reliability. If you can back up and restore etcd, you can recover from almost any disaster. The CKA exam will test this.

etcd-backup.sh 
# в•”в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•—
# в•‘  MEMORIZE THIS COMMAND вҖ” CKA GUARANTEED QUESTION      в•‘
# в•ҡв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•қ

ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot save /opt/etcd-backup.db \
  --endpoints=https://127.0.0.1:2379 \
  --cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt \
  --cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt \
  --key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key

# Expected output:
# {"level":"info","msg":"created temporary snapshot file",
#  "path":"/opt/etcd-backup.db.part"}
# {"level":"info","msg":"fetching snapshot","endpoint":"https://..."}
# Snapshot saved at /opt/etcd-backup.db

# Verify the backup
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot status /opt/etcd-backup.db \
  --write-out=table
# +----------+----------+------------+------------+
# |   HASH   | REVISION | TOTAL KEYS | TOTAL SIZE |
# +----------+----------+------------+------------+
# | fe01cf57 |    10945 |        771 |    2.1 MB  |
# +----------+----------+------------+------------+

etcdctl snapshot save вҖ” Flag Breakdown

рҹ’ҫ /opt/etcd-backup.db
Output file path. Use whatever path the exam question specifies.
рҹ“Ў --endpoints
etcd endpoint URL. Default: https://127.0.0.1:2379 (local etcd).
рҹ”җ --cacert
etcd CA certificate: /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt
рҹ“ң --cert
etcd server certificate: /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt
рҹ”‘ --key
etcd server key: /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key

рҹ§  How to Find the Certificate Paths on the Exam

If you forget the cert paths, extract them from the running etcd pod:

find-etcd-certs.sh 
# Method 1: From the etcd pod spec
kubectl -n kube-system describe pod etcd-control-plane | grep -A5 "cert\|key\|ca"
#   --cert-file=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt
#   --key-file=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key
#   --trusted-ca-file=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt
#   --peer-cert-file=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/peer.crt

# Method 2: From the static pod manifest
cat /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml | grep -E "cert|key|ca"

# Method 3: From the API server (etcd client certs)
cat /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml | grep etcd
рҹ’Ў CKA Tip: Always Set ETCDCTL_API=3

Some etcdctl versions default to API v2. Always prefix with ETCDCTL_API=3 or export it: export ETCDCTL_API=3. API v3 is what Kubernetes uses. If you forget this, the backup command may fail silently.

11.4 Restoring etcd from Backup

Restoring etcd is more complex than backup because you must stop etcd, restore to a new data directory, update the configuration to point to the new directory, and restart etcd.

1
Restore Snapshot

Use etcdctl snapshot restore to create a new data directory from the backup file.

2
Stop etcd

Move the etcd static pod manifest out of /etc/kubernetes/manifests/ to stop it.

3
Update data-dir

Edit the etcd manifest to point --data-dir to the restored directory.

4
Restart etcd

Move the manifest back. kubelet will auto-start etcd with the restored data.

5
Verify

Check that all previous resources are present in the restored cluster.

etcd-restore.sh 
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
#  STEP 1: Restore the snapshot to a NEW data directory
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot restore /opt/etcd-backup.db \
  --data-dir=/var/lib/etcd-restored

# Output:
# {"level":"info","msg":"restoring snapshot",
#  "path":"/opt/etcd-backup.db",
#  "wal-dir":"/var/lib/etcd-restored/member/wal",
#  "data-dir":"/var/lib/etcd-restored",
#  "snap-dir":"/var/lib/etcd-restored/member/snap"}

# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
#  STEP 2: Update the etcd static pod manifest
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
sudo vi /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml

# Change the --data-dir flag:
#   BEFORE: --data-dir=/var/lib/etcd
#   AFTER:  --data-dir=/var/lib/etcd-restored

# ALSO update the hostPath volume:
#   BEFORE: path: /var/lib/etcd
#   AFTER:  path: /var/lib/etcd-restored

# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
#  STEP 3: Wait for etcd to restart automatically
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
# kubelet detects the manifest change and restarts etcd

# Watch for the etcd pod to come back
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -w | grep etcd
# etcd-control-plane   1/1   Running   0   10s

# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
#  STEP 4: Verify the restore
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
kubectl get nodes
kubectl get pods --all-namespaces
kubectl get deployments
# All resources from the backup should be present
etcd Restore Flow вҖ” Visual Walkthrough
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  ETCD RESTORE PROCEDURE                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  /opt/etcd-backup.db                                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в–ј                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  etcdctl snapshot restore                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ  --data-dir=/var/lib/etcd-restored                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в–ј                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  /var/lib/etcd-restored/         вҶҗ New data directory        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ member/                                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ snap/                   вҶҗ Restored snapshots        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ wal/                    вҶҗ Write-ahead logs          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Now update etcd manifest:                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  --data-dir=/var/lib/etcd-restored    вҶҗ Update в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                                в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  volumes:                                      в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  - hostPath:                                   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      path: /var/lib/etcd-restored     вҶҗ Update в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җ kubelet detects manifest change вҶ’ restarts etcd pod     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вң… Cluster restored to the backup point in time              в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
рҹҡЁ Critical: Update BOTH data-dir AND hostPath

When restoring, you must update the --data-dir flag in the etcd command AND the hostPath.path in the volume section of the manifest. Missing either one will cause etcd to fail to start or use stale data.

вҡ пёҸ Post-Restore: Everything After the Backup is Lost

A restore rolls the cluster back to the point when the backup was taken. Any resources created, updated, or deleted after the backup will be lost (or reverted). This is why regular, scheduled backups are essential.

11.5 etcd Security & Encryption at Rest

etcd must be secured because it holds every secret, token, and credential in the cluster. Security comes from TLS encryption for communication and encryption at rest for stored data.

рҹ”’
TLS Encryption (In Transit)

Default with kubeadm. All communication between API server вҶ” etcd and etcd вҶ” etcd peers uses mutual TLS. Certificates are in /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/.

рҹ”җ
Encryption at Rest

Not enabled by default! Without this, secrets are stored as base64 (NOT encrypted) in etcd. You must configure an EncryptionConfiguration.

/etc/kubernetes/enc/enc.yaml 
# Encryption Configuration вҖ” encrypts Secrets at rest in etcd
apiVersion: apiserver.config.k8s.io/v1
kind: EncryptionConfiguration
resources:
  - resources:
      - secrets           # Only encrypt secrets (most common)
    providers:
      - aescbc:           # AES-CBC encryption (recommended)
          keys:
            - name: key1
              secret: c2VjcmV0LWtleS1oZXJlLTMyYnl0ZXM=  # Base64 of 32-byte key
      - identity: {}       # Fallback: read unencrypted (for existing secrets)
enable-encryption.sh 
# Step 1: Create the encryption config file
sudo mkdir -p /etc/kubernetes/enc
sudo vi /etc/kubernetes/enc/enc.yaml   # (paste YAML above)

# Step 2: Add flag to API server manifest
sudo vi /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml
# Add this flag to the command:
#   --encryption-provider-config=/etc/kubernetes/enc/enc.yaml
# Add volume mount for the config file:
#   volumeMounts:
#   - name: enc
#     mountPath: /etc/kubernetes/enc
#     readOnly: true
#   volumes:
#   - name: enc
#     hostPath:
#       path: /etc/kubernetes/enc

# Step 3: Wait for API server to restart
kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep apiserver

# Step 4: Re-encrypt ALL existing secrets
kubectl get secrets --all-namespaces -o json | \
  kubectl replace -f -

# Step 5: Verify encryption is working
# Read directly from etcd вҖ” should show encrypted data
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl get /registry/secrets/default/my-secret \
  --endpoints=https://127.0.0.1:2379 \
  --cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt \
  --cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt \
  --key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key | hexdump -C
# Should show encrypted binary data, NOT readable text
etcd Security вҖ” Defense In Depth
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ etcd Security Layers в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Layer 1: Network Isolation                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ etcd should ONLY be accessible from the control plane    в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Firewall: Allow port 2379 only from API server IP       в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Firewall: Allow port 2380 only from other etcd peers    в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Layer 2: TLS Encryption (In Transit)                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ API Server в”Җв”ҖTLSв”Җв”Җв–¶ etcd (client certs: server.crt/key) в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ etcd в”Җв”ҖTLSв”Җв”Җв–¶ etcd peers (peer certs: peer.crt/key)     в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ All verified against etcd CA: /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Layer 3: Encryption at Rest                                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ EncryptionConfiguration encrypts secrets before storage  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Provider: aescbc (AES-256-CBC) or secretbox               в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Without this: secrets stored as base64 (readable!)       в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Layer 4: RBAC + Authentication                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Only API server should have etcd client credentials      в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ No user should access etcd directly (always go via API)  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
рҹ’Ў CKA Tip: Encryption at Rest vs TLS

TLS encrypts data in transit (between components). Encryption at rest encrypts data on disk (inside etcd). Both are independent. kubeadm sets up TLS automatically but does NOT enable encryption at rest вҖ” you must configure it manually.

рҹҸҘ Bonus: etcd Health Check & Monitoring Commands

Knowing how to check etcd's health is essential for cluster troubleshooting. These commands tell you if etcd is running, healthy, and performing well.

etcd-health-check.sh 
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
#  ETCD HEALTH CHECK COMMANDS
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ

# Set cert variables (reuse across commands)
export ETCDCTL_API=3
CERTS="--endpoints=https://127.0.0.1:2379 \
  --cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt \
  --cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt \
  --key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key"

# Check cluster health
etcdctl endpoint health $CERTS
# https://127.0.0.1:2379 is healthy: successfully committed
#   proposal: took = 2.345ms

# Check endpoint status (shows leader, DB size, Raft index)
etcdctl endpoint status $CERTS --write-out=table
# +---------------------------+--------+---------+---------+-----------+
# |         ENDPOINT          |   ID   | VERSION | DB SIZE | IS LEADER |
# +---------------------------+--------+---------+---------+-----------+
# | https://127.0.0.1:2379    | abc123 |  3.5.12 |  4.2 MB |   true    |
# +---------------------------+--------+---------+---------+-----------+

# List all etcd cluster members
etcdctl member list $CERTS --write-out=table
# +--------+---------+-------+---------------------------+-----------+
# |   ID   | STATUS  | NAME  |       PEER ADDRS          | IS LEADER |
# +--------+---------+-------+---------------------------+-----------+
# | abc123 | started | etcd0 | https://192.168.56.10:2380|   true    |
# +--------+---------+-------+---------------------------+-----------+

# Check performance (write latency)
etcdctl check perf $CERTS
# 60/60 succeeded, took 1.234s
# PASS: Throughput is 150 writes/s

# Monitor real-time changes (debugging)
etcdctl watch /registry/pods --prefix $CERTS
# (streams all pod changes as they happen)
вң…
Healthy etcd Indicators

вҖў endpoint health says "is healthy"
вҖў DB size under 8 GB
вҖў Raft term stable (not incrementing)
вҖў All members show "started"
вҖў Write latency under 10ms
вҖў Leader is elected (one IS LEADER = true)

рҹҡЁ
Unhealthy etcd Red Flags

вҖў endpoint health timeout/errors
вҖў DB size over 8 GB (needs compaction)
вҖў "no leader" in member list
вҖў High Raft term (frequent elections)
вҖў "mvcc: database space exceeded"
вҖў Members showing "unstarted"

рҹ§№ Bonus: etcd Maintenance вҖ” Compaction & Defragmentation

etcd stores all historical revisions of keys. Over time, this grows the database. Compaction removes old revisions, and defragmentation reclaims disk space.

etcd-maintenance.sh 
# Get current revision number
REV=$(etcdctl endpoint status $CERTS --write-out="json" | \
  python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['Status']['header']['revision'])")

# Compact old revisions (keep only latest)
etcdctl compact $REV $CERTS
# compacted revision 12345

# Defragment (reclaim disk space after compaction)
etcdctl defrag $CERTS
# Finished defragmenting etcd member[https://127.0.0.1:2379]

# Check new DB size (should be smaller)
etcdctl endpoint status $CERTS --write-out=table
вҡ пёҸ Compaction is Irreversible

Once you compact, old revisions are permanently deleted. This means watchers using old revisions will get an error. Always take an etcd backup before compacting in production.

рҹҺҜ CKA etcd Quick Reference Card

Print this or memorize it. These are the exact commands and paths you need for the CKA exam.

Item Path / Value Why You Need It
etcd CA cert /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt --cacert flag for all etcdctl commands
etcd server cert /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt --cert flag for all etcdctl commands
etcd server key /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key --key flag for all etcdctl commands
Client port 2379 --endpoints HTTPS URL
Peer port 2380 etcd вҶ” etcd communication
Data directory /var/lib/etcd --data-dir for restore
Static pod manifest /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml Edit to change --data-dir after restore
API version env ETCDCTL_API=3 Always set this before etcdctl commands
CKA etcd вҖ” Complete Backup & Restore Cheat Sheet
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ   BACKUP:                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot save /opt/backup.db \             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     --endpoints=https://127.0.0.1:2379 \                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     --cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt \                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     --cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt \                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     --key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   VERIFY BACKUP:                                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot status /opt/backup.db -w table    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
  в”Ӯ   RESTORE:                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot restore /opt/backup.db \          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     --data-dir=/var/lib/etcd-restored                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   THEN EDIT: /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     1. --data-dir=/var/lib/etcd-restored                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     2. volumes.hostPath.path=/var/lib/etcd-restored                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   WAIT: kubelet auto-restarts etcd pod                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   VERIFY: kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep etcd              в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
  в”Ӯ   FIND CERT PATHS (if you forget):                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   kubectl -n kube-system describe pod etcd-<node> | grep cert     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   cat /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml | grep -E "cert|key|ca"  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
рҹ’Ў CKA Exam Strategy: etcd Questions

Step 1: Read the question carefully вҖ” does it ask for backup, restore, or both?
Step 2: If you forget cert paths, run: kubectl describe pod etcd-controlplane -n kube-system
Step 3: After restore, always verify with kubectl get nodes and kubectl get pods -A
Step 4: Don't panic if the API server takes 30-60 seconds to restart after a restore.

вҡ пёҸ CKA Gotcha: etcd Backup & Restore Traps

  • Certificate paths are REQUIRED вҖ” etcdctl won't work without --cacert, --cert, --key. Extract them from /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml
  • ETCDCTL_API=3 вҖ” Must be set. API v2 commands look similar but produce incompatible snapshots
  • Restore path вү  original path вҖ” etcdctl snapshot restore creates a NEW data directory. You must update --data-dir in the etcd manifest to point to it
  • After restore, wait вҖ” The API server, scheduler, and controller-manager restart automatically when kubelet detects the manifest change. Give it 30-60 seconds
  • Don't confuse endpoint вҖ” Default etcd endpoint is https://127.0.0.1:2379. External etcd uses the node's IP

CKA Speed Tip вҖ” etcd backup one-liner you MUST memorize:
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot save /tmp/etcd-backup.db \
  --cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt \
  --cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt \
  --key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key

рҹ“қ Chapter 11 Practice Drill

Timed challenge (target: 8 minutes):

  • 1. Find etcd cert paths: cat /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml | grep -E "cert|key|ca"
  • 2. Take a snapshot to /opt/etcd-backup.db
  • 3. Verify snapshot: ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot status /opt/etcd-backup.db --write-table
  • 4. Restore to /var/lib/etcd-restored
  • 5. Update /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml data-dir and hostPath volume
  • 6. Wait and verify: kubectl get pods -A

Key insight: This is a GUARANTEED exam question. Practice until you can do it blindfolded.

Chapter 12: Managing the Control Plane

Static pods, control plane health, API server config, scheduler, controller manager.

рҹҹЎ Intermediate вҸұпёҸ ~1 hour рҹ§  Control Plane рҹ“қ CKA EXAM
рҹ“Ӣ CKA Relevance: Cluster Architecture, Installation & Configuration вҖ” 25% of exam
рҹ“қ Exam Tip: Control plane troubleshooting appears in EVERY CKA exam. You must know where static pod manifests live, how to edit them, and what happens when you do.

вҡ пёҸ CKA Gotcha: Control Plane Management Traps

  • Static pods auto-restart вҖ” Edit a manifest in /etc/kubernetes/manifests/ and kubelet restarts the pod automatically. No kubectl apply needed. But if you make a syntax error, the component goes DOWN
  • API server down = kubectl dead вҖ” If you break kube-apiserver.yaml, you can't use kubectl. Use crictl ps and crictl logs to debug instead
  • Scheduler and Controller Manager вҖ” Both connect to the API server. If they crash, pods won't be scheduled or self-heal, but existing pods keep running
  • Static pod names include node suffix вҖ” A static pod manifest named myapp.yaml appears as myapp-<nodename> in kubectl get pods. Don't confuse it with a normal pod
  • kubelet config for static pods вҖ” The static pod path is configured in kubelet: staticPodPath in /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml. The exam may change it

CKA Speed Tip вҖ” Control plane health check in 10 seconds:
kubectl get pods -n kube-system вҖ” all 4 components should be Running (1/1)
kubectl get cs вҖ” (deprecated but quick) scheduler, controller-manager, etcd status
If a component is missing, check: ls /etc/kubernetes/manifests/ + crictl ps -a

вҡЎ Control plane inspection commands: kubectl -n kube-system logs kube-apiserver-<node> вҖ” API server logs
kubectl -n kube-system logs kube-scheduler-<node> вҖ” scheduler logs
crictl ps -a | grep kube вҖ” check containers when kubectl is down
crictl logs <container-id> вҖ” read logs without kubectl
systemctl restart kubelet вҖ” force kubelet to re-read manifests
рҹ“қ Chapter 12 Practice Drill

Timed challenge (target: 5 minutes):

  • 1. Create a static pod: place a pod manifest in /etc/kubernetes/manifests/test-static.yaml
  • 2. Verify it appears: kubectl get pods -A | grep test-static
  • 3. Break the API server: add an invalid flag to /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml
  • 4. Observe: kubectl stops working. Use crictl ps -a to see the crash-looping container
  • 5. Fix the manifest вҶ’ API server auto-recovers within 30 seconds
  • 6. Clean up: remove test-static.yaml from manifests dir

Key insight: When kubectl is dead, crictl + journalctl + direct manifest editing are your lifeline.

рҹҹЎ Part 4: Workloads & Scheduling Chapters 13-16

Chapter 13: Deployments, ReplicaSets, and Scaling

Deployments are the heart of Kubernetes workload management. In this chapter you'll master the complete lifecycle вҖ” creating, scaling, updating, and rolling back applications. We'll trace how a Deployment creates a ReplicaSet, which in turn creates Pods, and how the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) adds elastic scaling. Every example uses our anihpj/jobpost Django app so you see real-world patterns, not toy demos.

рҹҹЎ Intermediate вҸұпёҸ ~1.5 hours рҹҡҖ Workloads рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

рҹҺҜ What You'll Learn

  • вң… Create Deployments imperatively and declaratively вҖ” know the full YAML anatomy
  • вң… Perform rolling updates and instant rollbacks using kubectl rollout
  • вң… Understand how Deployments вҶ’ ReplicaSets вҶ’ Pods chain together under the hood
  • вң… Scale manually with kubectl scale and automatically with HPA
  • вң… Choose the right deployment strategy вҖ” RollingUpdate vs Recreate
  • вң… Control update pace with maxSurge and maxUnavailable
рҹҺ“ CKA Relevance
Workloads & Scheduling 15% Troubleshooting 30%
Deployments Are CKA Exam Bread & Butter

Nearly every CKA exam has at least 2-3 tasks involving Deployments. You must be fast at:

  • kubectl create deployment NAME --image=IMG --replicas=N вҖ” imperative creation
  • kubectl set image deployment/NAME container=IMG:TAG вҖ” trigger rolling update
  • kubectl rollout undo deployment/NAME --to-revision=N вҖ” rollback
  • kubectl scale deployment/NAME --replicas=N вҖ” manual scaling
  • kubectl rollout status deployment/NAME вҖ” verify update progress

13.1 Deployments вҖ” Complete Lifecycle

A Deployment is Kubernetes' recommended way to manage stateless applications. It provides declarative updates, self-healing, scaling, and version history вҖ” all in one resource.

рҹ“– Key Concept: Declarative vs Imperative

Kubernetes supports both styles. Imperative: kubectl create deployment anihpj --image=anihpj:v1. Declarative: kubectl apply -f deployment.yaml. On the CKA, use imperative commands to generate YAML fast, then edit if needed.

Deployment
рҹҺӣпёҸ Manages ReplicaSets
рҹ“Ұ Creates Pods via RS
рҹ”„ Rolling Updates
вҸӘ Rollback History
рҹ“Ҳ Scale In/Out
вң… Self-Healing

a) Creating a Deployment

Imperative вҖ” Fast Exam Creation 
# Create deployment (CKA exam speed)
kubectl create deployment anihpj --image=anihpj:v1 --replicas=3

# Generate YAML without creating (save to file, then edit)
kubectl create deployment anihpj --image=anihpj:v1 --replicas=3 \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml > anihpj-deploy.yaml

# Verify
kubectl get deployments
kubectl describe deployment anihpj
anihpj-deployment.yaml 
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    project: jobpost
spec:
  replicas: 3                    # Desired number of pods
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj                # Must match template labels
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1                # 1 extra pod during update
      maxUnavailable: 0          # Never fewer than 3 running
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj              # Must match selector
        version: v1
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: django
        image: anihpj:v1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "250m"
            memory: "256Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "500m"
            memory: "512Mi"
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 10

Deployment YAML Anatomy

рҹ”ў replicas
Desired pod count вҖ” the controller constantly ensures this number is running
рҹҸ·пёҸ selector.matchLabels
How the Deployment finds its pods вҖ” must match template labels exactly
рҹ”„ strategy
RollingUpdate (default) or Recreate вҖ” controls how pods are replaced
рҹ“Ұ template
The Pod spec вҖ” this is a "Pod blueprint" stamped out for each replica
рҹ“Ҳ maxSurge
How many extra pods beyond replicas are allowed during an update (number or %)
рҹ“ү maxUnavailable
How many pods can be unavailable during an update (number or %). 0 = zero downtime.

b) Scaling

Scaling changes the spec.replicas field. The Deployment's ReplicaSet controller immediately creates or deletes pods to match.

Manual Scaling Commands 
# Scale up вҖ” handle more traffic
kubectl scale deployment anihpj --replicas=5

# Scale down вҖ” save resources
kubectl scale deployment anihpj --replicas=2

# Verify scaling
kubectl get deployment anihpj
# NAME      READY   UP-TO-DATE   AVAILABLE   AGE
# anihpj   5/5     5            5           10m

# Watch pods appear/disappear in real-time
kubectl get pods -w -l app=anihpj
Manual Scaling вҖ” Pods Appear Instantly
  kubectl scale deployment anihpj --replicas=5

  BEFORE (3 replicas):             AFTER (5 replicas):
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯanihpj-1в”Ӯ в”Ӯanihpj-2в”Ӯ в”Ӯanihpj-3в”Ӯ    в”Ӯanihpj-1в”Ӯ в”Ӯanihpj-2в”Ӯ в”Ӯanihpj-3в”Ӯ в”Ӯanihpj-4в”Ӯ в”Ӯanihpj-5в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ v1 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ v1 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ v1 в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ v1 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ v1 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ v1 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ рҹҶ• v1  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ рҹҶ• v1  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                                                                          вҶ‘ NEW       вҶ‘ NEW

c) Rolling Updates

Rolling updates replace pods one at a time, ensuring your application stays available throughout the upgrade. This is the default strategy and is heavily tested on the CKA.

Triggering a Rolling Update 
# Update image вҖ” triggers rolling update
kubectl set image deployment/anihpj django=anihpj:v2

# Watch rollout progress (CKA exam: verify your work!)
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj
# Waiting for deployment "anihpj" rollout to finish:
#   1 out of 3 new replicas have been updated...
#   2 out of 3 new replicas have been updated...
#   deployment "anihpj" successfully rolled out

# Check rollout history
kubectl rollout history deployment/anihpj
# REVISION  CHANGE-CAUSE
# 1         <none>
# 2         <none>

# Add change cause for audit trail
kubectl annotate deployment/anihpj \
  kubernetes.io/change-cause="Update to v2 вҖ” new job search feature"
Rolling Update: anihpj v1 вҶ’ v2 (Zero Downtime)
  maxSurge: 1       (1 extra pod allowed during update)
  maxUnavailable: 0 (never fewer than 3 running)

  Time 0:  [рҹ“Ұv1] [рҹ“Ұv1] [рҹ“Ұv1]              вҶҗ All running v1
           в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
  Time 1:  [рҹ“Ұv1] [рҹ“Ұv1] [рҹ“Ұv1] [рҹҶ•v2]       вҶҗ v2 pod starting (surge=1)
           в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
  Time 2:  [рҹ“Ұv1] [рҹ“Ұv1] [рҹ—‘пёҸ  ] [вң…v2]       вҶҗ v2 ready вҶ’ old v1 terminating
           в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
  Time 3:  [рҹ“Ұv1] [рҹ“Ұv1] [вң…v2] [рҹҶ•v2]       вҶҗ Next v2 pod starting
           в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
  Time 4:  [рҹ“Ұv1] [рҹ—‘пёҸ  ] [вң…v2] [вң…v2]       вҶҗ Second v1 terminating
           в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
  Time 5:  [рҹ“Ұv1] [вң…v2] [вң…v2] [рҹҶ•v2]       вҶҗ Last v2 starting
           в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
  Time 6:  [вң…v2] [вң…v2] [вң…v2]              вҶҗ All v2 вң… Zero downtime! рҹҺү

  Key insight: At NO point were there fewer than 3 healthy pods serving traffic!

d) Rollbacks

If v2 is broken, you can instantly rollback. Kubernetes keeps a revision history of ReplicaSets.

вҡ пёҸ CKA Exam Alert: Rollback Commands

Rollback questions are extremely common. Know these cold:

  • kubectl rollout undo deployment/NAME вҖ” go back one revision
  • kubectl rollout undo deployment/NAME --to-revision=N вҖ” go to specific revision
  • kubectl rollout history deployment/NAME вҖ” see all revisions
Rollback Commands 
# Undo last update (go back to previous revision)
kubectl rollout undo deployment/anihpj

# Go to a specific revision
kubectl rollout undo deployment/anihpj --to-revision=2

# Check what happened
kubectl rollout history deployment/anihpj --revision=2

# Verify rollback succeeded
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj
kubectl get pods -l app=anihpj -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].image}'
# anihpj:v1 anihpj:v1 anihpj:v1  вҶҗ back to v1!
Rollback Flow: v2 Broken вҶ’ Instant Recovery to v1
  Deployment: anihpj
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Revision 1 (v1) в”Җв”Җв”Җ Revision 2 (v2) в”Җв”Җв”Җ Revision 3    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ        в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ        в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ӮReplicaSetв”Ӯ        в”ӮReplicaSetв”Ӯ        в”ӮReplicaSetв”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ (scaled  в”Ӯ  в”Җв”ҖвҶ’   в”Ӯ (scaled  в”Ӯ  в”Җв”ҖвҶ’   в”Ӯ = rev 1  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  to 0)   в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ  to 0)   в”Ӯ  undo  в”Ӯ (active) в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                        вҶ‘ рҹ’Җ broken          вҶ‘ вң… fixed  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  kubectl rollout undo deployment/anihpj                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вҶ’ K8s scales old RS back up, scales broken RS down     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вҶ’ Pods running v1 again within seconds! рҹҺү             в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
1
Create

kubectl create deployment or kubectl apply -f

2
Verify

kubectl get deploy,rs,pods

3
Scale

kubectl scale deploy --replicas=N

4
Update

kubectl set image deploy/NAME

5
Monitor

kubectl rollout status

6
Rollback

kubectl rollout undo if needed

e) Pause & Resume вҖ” Batch Multiple Changes

Sometimes you need to make multiple changes (image + resources + env vars) in one rollout instead of triggering separate updates for each. Pause the deployment, make all changes, then resume to trigger a single rollout.

Pause & Resume Workflow 
# Pause вҖ” no changes will trigger a rollout
kubectl rollout pause deployment/anihpj

# Make multiple changes (none trigger rollout yet)
kubectl set image deployment/anihpj django=anihpj:v3
kubectl set resources deployment/anihpj -c django \
  --requests=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi
kubectl set env deployment/anihpj DEBUG=false

# Resume вҖ” all changes deploy in ONE rollout
kubectl rollout resume deployment/anihpj

# Verify single rollout applied all changes
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj
Pause/Resume vs Normal вҖ” Rollout Count
  WITHOUT Pause/Resume:              WITH Pause/Resume:

  Change image вҶ’ Rollout #1  рҹ”„      kubectl rollout pause
  Change CPU   вҶ’ Rollout #2  рҹ”„      Change image  в”җ
  Change env   вҶ’ Rollout #3  рҹ”„      Change CPU    в”ңв”Җ no rollouts
                                      Change env    в”ҳ
  3 separate rollouts!               kubectl rollout resume
  3 ReplicaSets created!              вҶ’ Rollout #1 only  рҹ”„
  вҡ пёҸ Wasteful & slower               вң… Single clean rollout!

13.2 ReplicaSets вҖ” Under the Hood
