A ReplicaSet ensures a specified number of pod replicas are running at any time. You rarely create ReplicaSets directly вҖ” Deployments create and manage them for you. But understanding them is essential for troubleshooting.

рҹ’Ў Why You Need to Know This

When kubectl get pods shows a pod stuck in ImagePullBackOff, you need to trace: Pod вҶ’ ReplicaSet вҶ’ Deployment to find the root cause. The CKA tests this troubleshooting chain.

The Deployment вҶ’ ReplicaSet вҶ’ Pod Chain
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  DEPLOYMENT: anihpj                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ You manage this layer                                       в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ "I want 3 pods running anihpj:v1"                          в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                             в”Ӯ creates & manages                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                             в–ј                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  REPLICASET: anihpj-7d9f8b6c5d                           в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Kubernetes manages this layer automatically              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  selector: matchLabels: { app: anihpj }                  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  replicas: 3                                              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ owns             в”Ӯ owns             в”Ӯ owns           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в–ј                  в–ј                  в–ј                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ POD          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ POD          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ POD          в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ anihpj-..abc в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ anihpj-..def в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ anihpj-..ghi в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ рҹ“Ұ anihpj:v1в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ рҹ“Ұ anihpj:v1в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ рҹ“Ұ anihpj:v1в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ labels:      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ labels:      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ labels:      в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  app: anihpj в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  app: anihpj в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  app: anihpj в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Delete a pod? вҶ’ ReplicaSet auto-creates a replacement! рҹ”„     в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
replicaset-example.yaml (for understanding вҖ” rarely created directly) 
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ReplicaSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-replicaset
  labels:
    app: anihpj
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:                 # Which pods this RS owns
      app: anihpj
    # Advanced: matchExpressions
    # matchExpressions:
    #   - key: app
    #     operator: In           # In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist
    #     values: [anihpj, anihpj-staging]
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: django
        image: anihpj:v1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
рҹҸ·пёҸ
matchLabels

Simple equality: app: anihpj. All labels must match. Most common approach.

рҹ”Қ
matchExpressions

Operators: In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist. For complex multi-value selectors.

Inspecting the Chain 
# See all three layers at once
kubectl get deploy,rs,pods -l app=anihpj

# NAME                     READY   UP-TO-DATE   AVAILABLE   AGE
# deployment.apps/anihpj   3/3     3            3           5m
#
# NAME                                DESIRED   CURRENT   READY   AGE
# replicaset.apps/anihpj-7d9f8b6c5d  3         3         3       5m
#
# NAME                          READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
# pod/anihpj-7d9f8b6c5d-abc12  1/1     Running   0          5m
# pod/anihpj-7d9f8b6c5d-def34  1/1     Running   0          5m
# pod/anihpj-7d9f8b6c5d-ghi56  1/1     Running   0          5m

# See owner references (pod вҶ’ RS вҶ’ Deployment)
kubectl get pod anihpj-7d9f8b6c5d-abc12 -o jsonpath='{.metadata.ownerReferences[0].name}'
# anihpj-7d9f8b6c5d  вҶҗ owned by the ReplicaSet
вҡ пёҸ Never Delete ReplicaSets Directly

If you delete a ReplicaSet that belongs to a Deployment, the Deployment controller will immediately recreate it. To scale down, always modify the Deployment. Delete the Deployment to clean up all RS and Pods together.

рҹ’Ў revisionHistoryLimit вҖ” Control Revision History

By default, Kubernetes keeps 10 old ReplicaSets (revision history) for rollback. Each old RS consumes etcd storage. You can control this with spec.revisionHistoryLimit:

  • revisionHistoryLimit: 5 вҖ” keep last 5 revisions (good for production)
  • revisionHistoryLimit: 0 вҖ” keep none (вҡ пёҸ no rollback possible!)
  • revisionHistoryLimit: 10 вҖ” default

13.3 Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA)

The HPA automatically scales your Deployment based on observed CPU, memory, or custom metrics. When traffic spikes, HPA adds pods. When traffic drops, it removes them. This is critical for production and tested on the CKA.

рҹ“Ӣ Prerequisite: Metrics Server

HPA requires the Metrics Server to be installed in the cluster. Without it, HPA has no data to make scaling decisions. Verify: kubectl top nodes вҖ” if this works, Metrics Server is running.

HPA вҖ” Imperative (CKA Exam Speed) 
# Create HPA bound to deployment
kubectl autoscale deployment anihpj --min=3 --max=10 --cpu-percent=70

# Check HPA status
kubectl get hpa
# NAME     REFERENCE           TARGETS   MINPODS   MAXPODS   REPLICAS   AGE
# anihpj  Deployment/anihpj   45%/70%   3         10        3          2m

# Detailed view
kubectl describe hpa anihpj
anihpj-hpa.yaml 
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: anihpj-hpa
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: anihpj
  minReplicas: 3
  maxReplicas: 10
  metrics:
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: cpu
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 70    # Scale when avg CPU > 70%
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: memory
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 80    # Also watch memory
  behavior:
    scaleUp:
      stabilizationWindowSeconds: 30   # React quickly to spikes
    scaleDown:
      stabilizationWindowSeconds: 300  # Wait 5 min before scaling down
HPA Scaling Flow вҖ” Automatic Response to Load
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  HPA: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler for anihpj                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ҡ Metrics Server reports: CPU = 85%  (above target of 70%)   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в–ј                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹӨ– HPA evaluates every 15 seconds:                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     desiredReplicas = ceil(currentReplicas Г— (85% / 70%))       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     desiredReplicas = ceil(3 Г— 1.21) = 4                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в–ј                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ҳ HPA updates: deployment.spec.replicas = 4                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в–ј                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұ  4 pods running вҶ’ CPU drops to 60%                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  ... traffic keeps climbing ...                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ҡ CPU = 90%  вҶ’  HPA: replicas = 6                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұ  6 pods running вҶ’ CPU stabilizes at 55%           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  ... traffic decreases (night time) ...                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ҡ CPU = 25%  вҶ’  HPA waits 5 min (stabilization window)       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ               вҶ’  then scales down to minReplicas = 3            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұ  3 pods вҖ” cost efficient! рҹ’°                             в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
в¬ҶпёҸ
Scale Up (Fast)

Default stabilization: 0 seconds. HPA reacts immediately to load spikes to protect users.

в¬ҮпёҸ
Scale Down (Slow)

Default stabilization: 300 seconds. Avoids flapping вҖ” waits 5 min to confirm load is truly gone.

рҹ’Ў CKA Tip: HPA Requires Resource Requests

HPA can only measure utilization as a percentage if resources.requests is set on the container. Without requests, HPA cannot calculate CPU percentage and will report <unknown>/70%. Always set resource requests on Deployments that use HPA.

13.4 Deployment Strategies

Kubernetes supports two built-in deployment strategies. Choosing the right one depends on your application's ability to handle multiple versions running simultaneously.

Strategy How It Works Downtime When to Use anihpj Example
RollingUpdate Replace pods gradually, one (or few) at a time вқҢ None Default вҖ” use for most updates New Django feature release
Recreate Kill ALL old pods first, then start ALL new pods вң… Yes вҖ” brief outage Breaking changes, DB schema migrations, incompatible API versions Major Django version upgrade with DB migration
RollingUpdate vs Recreate вҖ” Visual Comparison
  в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
  ROLLINGUPDATE STRATEGY (default)          RECREATE STRATEGY
  в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ

  T0: [v1] [v1] [v1]                       [v1] [v1] [v1]
       в”Ӯ                                         в”Ӯ
  T1: [v1] [v1] [v1] [v2] вҶҗ surge              [рҹ’Җ] [рҹ’Җ] [рҹ’Җ] вҶҗ all killed
       в”Ӯ                                         в”Ӯ
  T2: [v1] [v1] [v2]      вҶҗ replace 1           [  ] [  ] [  ] вҶҗ downtime! вҡ пёҸ
       в”Ӯ                                         в”Ӯ
  T3: [v1] [v2] [v2]      вҶҗ replace 2           [v2] [v2] [v2] вҶҗ all new
       в”Ӯ
  T4: [v2] [v2] [v2]      вҶҗ complete вң…

  Availability:                             Availability:
  в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ 100%                   в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ ~70%
  Never less than minAvailable              Brief outage window

  в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ

RollingUpdate вҖ” Tuning Parameters

strategy-examples.yaml 
# Conservative вҖ” zero downtime, slow rollout
strategy:
  type: RollingUpdate
  rollingUpdate:
    maxSurge: 1          # Only 1 extra pod at a time
    maxUnavailable: 0    # Never fewer than desired replicas

# Aggressive вҖ” faster rollout, allows brief capacity dip
strategy:
  type: RollingUpdate
  rollingUpdate:
    maxSurge: "50%"      # Up to 50% extra pods
    maxUnavailable: "25%" # Allow 25% to be unavailable

# Recreate вҖ” kill all, then create all
strategy:
  type: Recreate         # No rollingUpdate params needed
RollingUpdate вҖ” Safety вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…
RollingUpdate вҖ” Speed вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳҶвҳҶ
Recreate вҖ” Safety вҳ…вҳ…вҳҶвҳҶвҳҶ
Recreate вҖ” Speed вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…
рҹ’Ў CKA Exam Strategy

On the exam, the default RollingUpdate is almost always what you want. Only use Recreate if the question explicitly says "all pods must be running the same version" or mentions database migrations that can't handle mixed versions.

рҹ“– Beyond Built-in: Blue-Green & Canary Deployments

Kubernetes only has RollingUpdate and Recreate built in. Two other common strategies require manual setup or tools like Argo Rollouts / Istio:

  • Blue-Green вҖ” Run two full Deployments (blue=current, green=new). Switch the Service selector from blue to green once verified. Instant rollback by switching back.
  • Canary вҖ” Deploy new version alongside old with fewer replicas (e.g., 1 of 10). Route a small percentage of traffic to canary. If healthy, gradually increase. Popular with service meshes.
Blue-Green vs Canary вҖ” Not Built-in but Important to Know
  BLUE-GREEN:                           CANARY:

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ Service в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ              в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ Service в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  selector: ver=blue  в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ  selector: app=web   в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ              в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
             в”Ӯ                                     в”Ӯ
    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    в–ј                 в–ј                   в–ј                 в–ј
  Blue Deploy      Green Deploy        v1 Deploy (9)    v2 Deploy (1)
  (current вң…)     (new, testing)      (90% traffic)    (10% canary)
  [v1][v1][v1]     [v2][v2][v2]        [v1]Г—9           [v2]Г—1

  Switch: change   Promote: scale      Promote: scale v2 up,
  selector to      green, delete       scale v1 down gradually
  ver=green        blue

рҹ”‘ Chapter 13 Quick Reference

Action Command
Create deploymentkubectl create deployment NAME --image=IMG --replicas=N
Scalekubectl scale deployment NAME --replicas=N
Update imagekubectl set image deployment/NAME container=IMG:TAG
Check rolloutkubectl rollout status deployment/NAME
View historykubectl rollout history deployment/NAME
Undo lastkubectl rollout undo deployment/NAME
Go to revisionkubectl rollout undo deployment/NAME --to-revision=N
Autoscalekubectl autoscale deployment NAME --min=M --max=X --cpu-percent=P
Pause rolloutkubectl rollout pause deployment/NAME
Resume rolloutkubectl rollout resume deployment/NAME

вҡ пёҸ CKA Gotcha: Deployment & Rollout Traps

  • Rolling update is the default вҖ” If the question doesn't specify a strategy, it's RollingUpdate. Only use Recreate when explicitly asked (kills all pods first)
  • maxUnavailable + maxSurge вҖ” Defaults are 25% each. The exam may ask you to set specific values. Remember: maxUnavailable: 0 + maxSurge: 1 = zero-downtime
  • Rollback requires history вҖ” kubectl rollout undo only works if there's a previous revision. Add --record (deprecated) or use --to-revision=N for precision
  • kubectl set image container name вҖ” You must specify the container name: kubectl set image deployment/web nginx=nginx:1.25. Get it from kubectl get deploy web -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[*].name}'

CKA Speed Tip вҖ” Deployment lifecycle in 60 seconds:
kubectl create deploy web --image=nginx:1.24 --replicas=3
kubectl set image deploy/web nginx=nginx:1.25
kubectl rollout status deploy/web
kubectl rollout history deploy/web
kubectl rollout undo deploy/web вҖ” instant rollback

рҹ“қ Chapter 13 Practice Drill

Timed challenge (target: 4 minutes):

  • 1. Create deployment webapp with nginx:1.23, 4 replicas
  • 2. Update to nginx:1.24, watch rollout: kubectl rollout status deploy/webapp
  • 3. Update to nginx:BROKEN (bad image) вҖ” observe the stuck rollout
  • 4. Rollback: kubectl rollout undo deploy/webapp
  • 5. Verify image is nginx:1.24 again

Chapter 14: Scheduling and Node Management

The Kubernetes scheduler decides where every pod runs. As a CKA administrator, you must master how to influence scheduling decisions вҖ” from simple node selectors to complex affinity rules, taints, tolerations, and node maintenance procedures. This chapter is one of the most heavily tested topics on the CKA exam.

рҹҹЎ Intermediate вҸұпёҸ ~2 hours рҹ“Қ Scheduling рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

рҹҺҜ What You'll Learn

  • вң… How the Kubernetes scheduler makes placement decisions (filtering вҶ’ scoring вҶ’ binding)
  • вң… Use nodeSelector for simple node placement
  • вң… Configure node affinity (required vs preferred) for flexible scheduling
  • вң… Use pod affinity/anti-affinity for co-location and spreading
  • вң… Apply taints and tolerations to control which pods can run on which nodes
  • вң… Perform safe node maintenance with cordon, drain, uncordon
  • вң… Spread pods evenly with topology spread constraints
  • вң… Use PriorityClasses for pod preemption
рҹҺ“ CKA Relevance
Workloads & Scheduling 15% Cluster Architecture 25%
Scheduling Is a CKA Exam Favourite

Expect at least 2-3 scheduling questions. The most common patterns:

  • Taints & Tolerations вҖ” taint a node, create a pod with/without toleration, verify placement
  • Node Affinity вҖ” label a node, create a pod with nodeAffinity targeting that label
  • Node Maintenance вҖ” cordon вҶ’ drain вҶ’ perform work вҶ’ uncordon
  • Static Pods вҖ” create a manifest in /etc/kubernetes/manifests/

14.1 How the Scheduler Works

The kube-scheduler runs as a control plane component. When a new pod is created without a nodeName, it enters the scheduling queue. The scheduler then executes a multi-phase pipeline to find the best node.

1
Filtering

Which nodes CAN run this pod? Checks: sufficient resources, node taints, node selectors, affinity rules, port availability.

2
Scoring

Which passing node is the BEST fit? Scores based on: resource balance, affinity preference, spread, image locality.

3
Binding

The highest-scoring node wins. Scheduler sets pod.spec.nodeName and the kubelet on that node starts the container.

Scheduler Decision Flowchart вҖ” anihpj Pod Placement
  NEW POD: anihpj (requests: 256Mi memory, 250m CPU)
       в”Ӯ
       в–ј
  в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ PHASE 1: FILTERING в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
  "Which nodes CAN run this pod?"

  Node 1 (512Mi free, 500m CPU free)   вҶ’ вң… PASSES вҖ” has enough resources
  Node 2 (128Mi free, 100m CPU free)   вҶ’ вқҢ FAILS  вҖ” insufficient memory
  Node 3 (1Gi free, 1000m CPU free)    вҶ’ вң… PASSES вҖ” has enough resources
  Node 4 (taint: gpu=true:NoSchedule)  вҶ’ вқҢ FAILS  вҖ” no matching toleration
       в”Ӯ
       в–ј (only passing nodes continue)
  в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ PHASE 2: SCORING в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
  "Which passing node is the BEST fit?"

  Node 1: Score 65  вҖ” good fit, resources balanced
  Node 3: Score 82  вҖ” better fit, more headroom,
                      topology spread bonus, affinity match
       в”Ӯ
       в–ј
  в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ PHASE 3: BINDING в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ

  рҹҸҶ Winner: Node 3 (highest score: 82)
  вҶ’ pod.spec.nodeName = "node-3"
  вҶ’ kubelet on Node 3 pulls image вҶ’ starts container
  вҶ’ Pod status: Running вң…
рҹҺҜ
Resource Fit

Does the node have enough CPU & memory to satisfy the pod's requests?

рҹҸ·пёҸ
Label Match

Does the node have the labels required by nodeSelector or nodeAffinity?

рҹҡ«
Taint Check

Does the pod tolerate the node's taints? No toleration = rejected.

рҹ“Ҡ
Spread Score

How well does this placement spread pods across nodes/zones?

Static Pods вҖ” Bypass the Scheduler

Static pods are managed directly by the kubelet on a specific node, not by the API server. They're used for control plane components (etcd, apiserver, scheduler itself). The CKA may ask you to create one.

Creating a Static Pod 
# Static pods live in the kubelet's manifest directory
# Default: /etc/kubernetes/manifests/

# Find the staticPodPath on any node
ssh worker-1 "cat /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml | grep staticPodPath"
# staticPodPath: /etc/kubernetes/manifests

# Create a static pod (just place a YAML file there)
ssh worker-1 "cat > /etc/kubernetes/manifests/static-nginx.yaml" <<'EOF'
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: static-nginx
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
    image: nginx:1.25
    ports:
    - containerPort: 80
EOF

# kubelet auto-starts it! Check from control plane:
kubectl get pods
# NAME                       READY   STATUS    AGE
# static-nginx-worker-1      1/1     Running   10s
#                  ^^^^^^^^^ node name appended!

# Delete: remove the file from /etc/kubernetes/manifests/
# kubectl delete won't work вҖ” kubelet will recreate it!
вҡ пёҸ CKA Exam: Static Pod Key Facts
  • Static pod names always end with -<nodename>
  • They can only be created/deleted by adding/removing YAML from the manifest directory
  • kubectl delete on a static pod will NOT permanently remove it вҖ” kubelet recreates it
  • Control plane components (apiserver, etcd, scheduler) are static pods on the control plane node

14.2 Node Selectors вҖ” Simple Placement

The simplest way to constrain a pod to specific nodes. Label a node, then reference that label in the pod spec. It's a hard requirement вҖ” the pod won't schedule if no node matches.

Label a Node, Then Select It 
# Step 1: Label the node
kubectl label node worker-1 disk=ssd

# Verify label was applied
kubectl get node worker-1 --show-labels | grep disk

# Step 2: Create pod with nodeSelector (see YAML below)
kubectl apply -f anihpj-ssd-pod.yaml

# Verify pod landed on the right node
kubectl get pod anihpj-ssd -o wide
# NAME         READY   STATUS    NODE       
# anihpj-ssd   1/1     Running   worker-1   вҶҗ correct!

# Clean up: remove label
kubectl label node worker-1 disk-
anihpj-ssd-pod.yaml 
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: anihpj-ssd
spec:
  nodeSelector:
    disk: ssd                    # Only schedule on nodes with disk=ssd
  containers:
  - name: django
    image: anihpj:v1
    ports:
    - containerPort: 8000
рҹ’Ў nodeSelector vs nodeAffinity

nodeSelector is simple but limited вҖ” it only supports exact equality matching. For "prefer but don't require" or "match any of these values" logic, use Node Affinity (see 14.3).

14.3 Node Affinity вҖ” Advanced Placement

Node affinity is the more powerful version of nodeSelector. It supports operators (In, NotIn, Exists, Gt, Lt) and offers two types: hard rules (required) and soft rules (preferred).

Type Behavior When Pod Can't Match Use Case
requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution Hard rule вҖ” MUST match Pod stays Pending forever GPU workloads, compliance zones
preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution Soft rule вҖ” TRY to match Scheduled anywhere available Prefer SSD, prefer same zone
anihpj-node-affinity.yaml 
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
    spec:
      affinity:
        nodeAffinity:
          # HARD: Must be on an SSD node
          requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
            nodeSelectorTerms:
            - matchExpressions:
              - key: disk
                operator: In
                values:
                - ssd
                - nvme
          # SOFT: Prefer us-east zone (but ok if not available)
          preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
          - weight: 80           # 1-100, higher = stronger preference
            preference:
              matchExpressions:
              - key: topology.kubernetes.io/zone
                operator: In
                values:
                - us-east-1a
                - us-east-1b
      containers:
      - name: django
        image: anihpj:v1
Node Affinity Decision Matrix
  Pod: anihpj (requires: disk вҲҲ {ssd, nvme}, prefers: zone вҲҲ {us-east-1a})

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  Node          disk    zone          Required  Preferred   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  worker-1      ssd     us-east-1a   вң… PASS   вң… Match    в”Ӯ вҶҗ Best (score 82)
  в”Ӯ  worker-2      ssd     us-west-1a   вң… PASS   вқҢ No match в”Ӯ вҶҗ OK (score 65)
  в”Ӯ  worker-3      hdd     us-east-1a   вқҢ FAIL   вҖ”           в”Ӯ вҶҗ Rejected
  в”Ӯ  worker-4      nvme    us-east-1b   вң… PASS   вң… Match    в”Ӯ вҶҗ Good (score 78)
  в”Ӯ  worker-5      вҖ”       us-east-1a   вқҢ FAIL   вҖ”           в”Ӯ вҶҗ Rejected
  в”Ӯ                                                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Result: Pod scheduled on worker-1 (highest combined score)в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
вҡ пёҸ "IgnoredDuringExecution" вҖ” What It Means

If you remove a node's label after a pod is already scheduled there, the pod keeps running. The affinity rule is only checked at scheduling time, not continuously. A future Kubernetes version may add RequiredDuringExecution to also evict running pods.

14.4 Pod Affinity and Anti-Affinity

While Node Affinity places pods relative to nodes, Pod Affinity/Anti-Affinity places pods relative to other pods. This is essential for performance (co-locate) and high availability (spread out).

рҹӨқ
Pod Affinity

"Place me near this pod." Put anihpj Django on the same node/zone as its Redis cache for low latency.

вҶ”пёҸ
Pod Anti-Affinity

"Keep me away from that pod." Spread anihpj replicas across different nodes so one node failure doesn't take all of them down.

anihpj-pod-antiaffinity.yaml вҖ” HA Deployment 
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
    spec:
      affinity:
        # Anti-Affinity: Spread replicas across nodes
        podAntiAffinity:
          requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
          - labelSelector:
              matchExpressions:
              - key: app
                operator: In
                values:
                - anihpj
            topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname  # "different nodes"

        # Affinity: Place near Redis cache
        podAffinity:
          preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
          - weight: 100
            podAffinityTerm:
              labelSelector:
                matchExpressions:
                - key: app
                  operator: In
                  values:
                  - anihpj-redis
              topologyKey: topology.kubernetes.io/zone  # "same zone"
      containers:
      - name: django
        image: anihpj:v1
Pod Anti-Affinity вҖ” anihpj Replicas Spread Across Nodes
  Deployment: anihpj (replicas: 3)
  podAntiAffinity: kubernetes.io/hostname (no 2 on same node)

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ   worker-1     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   worker-2     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   worker-3     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ anihpj-abc в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ anihpj-def в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ anihpj-ghi в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   рҹ“Ұ v1    в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   рҹ“Ұ v1    в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   рҹ“Ұ v1    в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ redis-xyz  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   рҹ”ҙ cache в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  вң… Each node has exactly 1 anihpj pod вҖ” survives any single node failure!
  вң… anihpj-abc co-located with redis (podAffinity) for low latency

14.5 Taints and Tolerations

Taints are the opposite of affinity вҖ” taints repel pods from a node, while tolerations let specific pods override that repulsion. This is a CKA must-know concept and appears on almost every exam.

рҹ“– The Analogy

Think of a node's taint as a "No Entry" sign. A toleration is the "VIP pass" that lets a pod in despite the sign. Without the pass, the pod is rejected.

Effect Behavior Running Pods Use Case
NoSchedule New pods WITHOUT tolerance are rejected Existing pods stay Dedicated GPU nodes, team isolation
PreferNoSchedule Try NOT to schedule here, but allow if no other option Existing pods stay Soft preference вҖ” avoid but don't block
NoExecute Reject new pods AND evict running pods without tolerance Evicted! Node going into maintenance, toxic node
Taint & Toleration Commands 
# Add a taint to a node
kubectl taint node worker-1 gpu=true:NoSchedule

# Verify taint
kubectl describe node worker-1 | grep -A5 Taints
# Taints:  gpu=true:NoSchedule

# Remove a taint (add dash at the end)
kubectl taint node worker-1 gpu=true:NoSchedule-

# See control plane taints (this is why your pods don't run on master!)
kubectl describe node control-plane | grep Taints
# Taints:  node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane:NoSchedule
pod-with-toleration.yaml 
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: ml-training-job
spec:
  tolerations:
  - key: "gpu"
    operator: "Equal"        # Match exact value
    value: "true"
    effect: "NoSchedule"
  # Alternative: tolerate ANY value for the key
  # - key: "gpu"
  #   operator: "Exists"    # Don't care about value
  #   effect: "NoSchedule"
  containers:
  - name: trainer
    image: ml-trainer:v1
    resources:
      limits:
        nvidia.com/gpu: 1
Taints & Tolerations вҖ” Access Control
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ Node: gpu-node в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ Taint: gpu=true:NoSchedule   рҹҡ«                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  anihpj pod (no toleration)                  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вҶ’ вқҢ REJECTED вҖ” "You don't have a VIP pass" в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  ml-job pod (toleration: gpu=true)           в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вҶ’ вң… SCHEDULED вҖ” "VIP pass accepted!"      в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ Control Plane Node в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ Taint: node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane:      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ        NoSchedule                                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  That's why your pods DON'T run on control plane!  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Only system pods (with tolerations) run here:     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ kube-apiserver                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ kube-scheduler                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ kube-controller-manager                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җ etcd                                           в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
вҡ пёҸ CKA Trap: Taint Removal Syntax

To remove a taint, append a - (minus sign) at the end. Many candidates forget this on the exam:

  • Add: kubectl taint node worker-1 key=value:NoSchedule
  • Remove: kubectl taint node worker-1 key=value:NoSchedule-

14.6 Node Management for Admins

Cluster administrators regularly need to perform node maintenance вҖ” OS upgrades, kernel patches, hardware replacement. Kubernetes provides three commands for safe node management. This is a very common CKA exam scenario.

1
Cordon

Mark node as unschedulable. Existing pods stay, but no new pods can be scheduled here.

2
Drain

Safely evict all pods to other nodes. Respects PDB, grace periods, and pod lifecycle.

3
Maintenance

Perform the actual work вҖ” OS upgrade, kernel patch, reboot, hardware swap.

4
Uncordon

Mark node as schedulable again. New pods can now be placed here.

Node Maintenance Workflow 
# Step 1: Cordon вҖ” stop new pods from scheduling
kubectl cordon worker-1
# node/worker-1 cordoned

# Verify: node shows SchedulingDisabled
kubectl get nodes
# NAME        STATUS                     ROLES    AGE
# worker-1    Ready,SchedulingDisabled   worker   30d

# Step 2: Drain вҖ” safely evict all pods
kubectl drain worker-1 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data
# evicting pod default/anihpj-7d9f8b6c5d-abc12
# evicting pod default/anihpj-7d9f8b6c5d-def34
# node/worker-1 drained

# Step 3: Perform maintenance (OS upgrade, reboot, etc.)
# ... work happens here ...

# Step 4: Uncordon вҖ” allow pods again
kubectl uncordon worker-1
# node/worker-1 uncordoned

# Verify: node is Ready again (no SchedulingDisabled)
kubectl get nodes
# NAME        STATUS   ROLES    AGE
# worker-1    Ready    worker   30d
Node Maintenance Flow вҖ” Safe Pod Migration
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ worker-1 (BEFORE) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ worker-2 в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                                  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ anihpj-abc    рҹ“Ұ redis-xyz  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ anihpj-ghi          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ anihpj-def                  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  Step 1: kubectl cordon worker-1
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җ worker-1 (рҹҡ« SchedulingDisabled) в”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ anihpj-abc  рҹ“Ұ redis-xyz  (stay)  в”Ӯ  вҶҗ existing pods remain
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ anihpj-def                (stay)  в”Ӯ  вҶҗ new pods blocked
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  Step 2: kubectl drain worker-1 --ignore-daemonsets
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җ worker-1 (EMPTY) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ worker-2 в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ anihpj-ghi (existing)   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  (all pods evicted)      в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ anihpj-abc (migrated)   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ anihpj-def (migrated)   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ”§ Maintenance time!   в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ redis-xyz  (migrated)   в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  Step 3-4: Maintenance вҶ’ uncordon
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җ worker-1 (вң… Ready) в”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  Now accepts new pods!    в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
рҹҡЁ Drain Gotchas
  • --ignore-daemonsets is required if DaemonSet pods exist (they can't be evicted)
  • --delete-emptydir-data is needed if pods use emptyDir volumes
  • --force only if there are standalone pods (not managed by RS/Deployment) вҖ” data will be lost!
  • Without these flags, drain will refuse and show an error вҖ” know which flag to add!

14.7 Topology Spread Constraints

Topology spread constraints give you fine-grained control over how pods are distributed across topology domains (zones, nodes, regions). Unlike pod anti-affinity which is "avoid same node", topology spread says "distribute evenly".

anihpj-topology-spread.yaml 
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj
spec:
  replicas: 6
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
    spec:
      topologySpreadConstraints:
      - maxSkew: 1                           # Max diff in pod count between zones
        topologyKey: topology.kubernetes.io/zone  # Spread across zones
        whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule     # Hard constraint
        labelSelector:
          matchLabels:
            app: anihpj
      - maxSkew: 1
        topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname   # Also spread across nodes
        whenUnsatisfiable: ScheduleAnyway    # Soft constraint
        labelSelector:
          matchLabels:
            app: anihpj
      containers:
      - name: django
        image: anihpj:v1
Topology Spread вҖ” Even Distribution Across Zones
  6 replicas, maxSkew: 1, topologyKey: zone

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ us-east-1a в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ us-east-1b в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ us-east-1c в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ anihpj-1           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ anihpj-3           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ anihpj-5           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ anihpj-2           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ anihpj-4           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұ anihpj-6           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Count: 2              в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Count: 2              в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Count: 2              в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  maxSkew = max(count) - min(count) = 2 - 2 = 0  вң… (within maxSkew of 1)

  вқҢ Without topology spread (all could land on one zone):
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ us-east-1a в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ us-east-1b в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ us-east-1c в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұрҹ“Ұ           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  (empty)               в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  (empty)               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Count: 6   вҶҗ BAD!    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Count: 0              в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Count: 0              в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  Zone failure = total outage! рҹ’Җ

Topology Spread Parameters

рҹ“Ҹ maxSkew
Maximum allowed difference in pod counts between topology domains. Lower = more even.
рҹ—әпёҸ topologyKey
Node label to group by: kubernetes.io/hostname (node), topology.kubernetes.io/zone (zone)
рҹҡ« whenUnsatisfiable
DoNotSchedule (hard вҖ” pod stays Pending) or ScheduleAnyway (soft вҖ” best effort)

14.8 Priority and Preemption

PriorityClasses let you assign importance levels to pods. When the cluster is full, a high-priority pod can preempt (evict) a lower-priority pod to make room.

priority-classes.yaml 
# High priority вҖ” critical production workloads
apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityClass
metadata:
  name: high-priority
value: 1000000
globalDefault: false
preemptionPolicy: PreemptLowerPriority
description: "Critical production workloads like anihpj"
---
# Low priority вҖ” batch jobs, can be evicted
apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityClass
metadata:
  name: low-priority
value: 100
globalDefault: false
preemptionPolicy: Never          # Don't preempt others
description: "Batch jobs and background tasks"
---
# Usage in Deployment
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
    spec:
      priorityClassName: high-priority   # вҶҗ assign priority
      containers:
      - name: django
        image: anihpj:v1
Preemption вҖ” High Priority Pod Evicts Low Priority
  Cluster is FULL вҖ” no free capacity

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ worker-1 в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ worker-2 в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ рҹ“Ұ batch-job-1     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ рҹ“Ұ batch-job-3     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ (low-priority:100) в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ (low-priority:100) в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ рҹ“Ұ batch-job-2     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ рҹ“Ұ batch-job-4     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ (low-priority:100) в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ (low-priority:100) в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  NEW POD: anihpj (high-priority: 1000000) needs scheduling!

  Scheduler: "No room, but anihpj has higher priority..."
  вҶ’ PREEMPTS batch-job-1 on worker-1

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ worker-1 в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ worker-2 в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ рҹҢҹ anihpj          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ рҹ“Ұ batch-job-3     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ (high-priority!)   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ (low-priority:100) в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ рҹ“Ұ batch-job-2     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ рҹ“Ұ batch-job-4     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ (low-priority:100) в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ (low-priority:100) в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  batch-job-1 was evicted to make room for anihpj  вҡЎ
рҹ’Ў When to Use PriorityClasses

Use high priority for production-critical workloads (web servers, APIs). Use low priority for batch jobs, CI/CD runners, dev environments. This ensures production always gets resources first.

рҹ§© Scheduling Mechanisms Comparison вҖ” When to Use What

Mechanism Controls Direction Best For
nodeSelector Pod вҶ’ Node рҹҹў Attract (hard) Simple label matching
Node Affinity Pod вҶ’ Node рҹҹў Attract (hard/soft) Complex rules, preferred placement
Pod Affinity Pod вҶ’ Pod рҹҹў Attract (co-locate) Low latency (app near cache)
Pod Anti-Affinity Pod вҶ’ Pod рҹ”ҙ Repel (spread) HA (replicas on different nodes)
Taints/Tolerations Node вҶ’ Pod рҹ”ҙ Repel + allow Dedicated nodes, maintenance
Topology Spread Pod distribution вҡ–пёҸ Even spread Balance across zones/nodes
Static Pods Fixed to node рҹ“Ң Pinned Control plane components
PriorityClass Pod importance в¬ҶпёҸ Preempt lower Critical vs batch workloads

14.9 Practice Exercises

These exercises mirror actual CKA exam questions. Practice each one until you can complete it in under 5 minutes.

рҹҸӢпёҸ
Exercise 14.1: Taints & Tolerations

1. Taint node01 with env=prod:NoSchedule
2. Create an nginx pod вҖ” verify it does NOT schedule on node01
3. Add a toleration вҖ” verify it now schedules there
4. Remove the taint

рҹҸӢпёҸ
Exercise 14.2: Node Affinity

1. Label node01 with disktype=ssd
2. Create pod with required nodeAffinity for ssd nodes
3. Create pod with preferred nodeAffinity for ssd
4. Verify with kubectl get pods -o wide

рҹҸӢпёҸ
Exercise 14.3: Pod Anti-Affinity

1. Create anihpj deployment (replicas: 3)
2. Add podAntiAffinity вҖ” no 2 pods on same node
3. Verify: kubectl get pods -o wide (all on different nodes)
4. Scale to 4 вҖ” what happens if only 3 nodes?

рҹҸӢпёҸ
Exercise 14.4: Node Maintenance

1. Cordon worker-1
2. Deploy nginx (replicas: 3) вҖ” verify none land on worker-1
3. Drain worker-1 safely
4. Uncordon and verify

Exercise 14.1 вҖ” Solution (try it yourself first!) 
# 1. Taint the node
kubectl taint node node01 env=prod:NoSchedule

# 2. Create nginx pod (no toleration)
kubectl run nginx-test --image=nginx
kubectl get pod nginx-test -o wide
# вҶ’ Should be on a different node (not node01)

# 3. Create pod WITH toleration
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: nginx-tolerant
spec:
  tolerations:
  - key: "env"
    operator: "Equal"
    value: "prod"
    effect: "NoSchedule"
  containers:
  - name: nginx
    image: nginx
EOF
kubectl get pod nginx-tolerant -o wide
# вҶ’ Can schedule on node01

# 4. Remove taint
kubectl taint node node01 env=prod:NoSchedule-

# Clean up
kubectl delete pod nginx-test nginx-tolerant

рҹ”‘ Chapter 14 Quick Reference

Action Command
Label a nodekubectl label node NAME key=value
Remove labelkubectl label node NAME key-
Add taintkubectl taint node NAME key=val:Effect
Remove taintkubectl taint node NAME key=val:Effect-
Cordon nodekubectl cordon NAME
Drain nodekubectl drain NAME --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data
Uncordon nodekubectl uncordon NAME
Check node taintskubectl describe node NAME | grep Taints
Show node labelskubectl get nodes --show-labels
Check pod placementkubectl get pods -o wide

вҡ пёҸ CKA Gotcha: Scheduling & Node Management Traps

  • nodeSelector vs nodeAffinity вҖ” nodeSelector is simple key=value matching. nodeAffinity supports In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist. The exam tests both
  • Taint effect matters вҖ” NoSchedule = new pods blocked. NoExecute = existing pods EVICTED. PreferNoSchedule = soft preference. Mixing them up = wrong answer
  • kubectl drain flags вҖ” Without --ignore-daemonsets it fails on DaemonSet pods. Without --delete-emptydir-data it fails on emptyDir volumes. Always use both
  • Cordon вү  Drain вҖ” cordon only prevents NEW pods. drain evicts existing pods AND cordons. After maintenance: uncordon
  • Toleration must EXACTLY match taint вҖ” key, value, AND effect must match. A toleration with the wrong effect won't work

CKA Speed Tip вҖ” Node maintenance in 30 seconds:
kubectl drain node01 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data
вҖҰ perform maintenance (upgrade, reboot) вҖҰ
kubectl uncordon node01
Done. Pods will be rescheduled back automatically.

рҹ“қ Chapter 14 Practice Drill

Timed challenge (target: 6 minutes):

  • 1. Label node node01 with disk=ssd
  • 2. Create a pod with nodeSelector: disk: ssd вҖ” verify it lands on node01
  • 3. Taint node01: kubectl taint node node01 maintenance=true:NoSchedule
  • 4. Create another pod WITHOUT toleration вҖ” it should go to a different node
  • 5. Drain node01, uncordon, verify pods reschedule

Chapter 15: Resource Management

Every container in Kubernetes can request a minimum amount of CPU and memory, and be limited to a maximum. Understanding requests, limits, QoS classes, LimitRanges, and ResourceQuotas is critical for cluster stability вҖ” and prominently tested on the CKA exam. A misconfigured resource setting can crash an entire node.

рҹҹЎ Intermediate вҸұпёҸ ~1 hour рҹ“Ҡ Resources рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

рҹҺҜ What You'll Learn

  • вң… Set CPU and memory requests (scheduling guarantee) and limits (hard ceiling)
  • вң… Understand QoS classes вҖ” Guaranteed, Burstable, BestEffort
  • вң… Configure LimitRange for namespace-wide default limits
  • вң… Configure ResourceQuota to cap total namespace consumption
  • вң… Know what happens when CPU is exceeded (throttle) vs memory (OOMKilled)
рҹҺ“ CKA Relevance
Workloads & Scheduling 15% Cluster Architecture 25%
Resource Questions on the CKA

You'll likely be asked to:

  • Create pods with specific resource requests and limits
  • Create LimitRange or ResourceQuota for a namespace
  • Identify why a pod is OOMKilled or stuck in Pending
  • Know: kubectl top pods and kubectl top nodes

15.1 Resource Requests and Limits

Every container can specify requests (minimum guaranteed) and limits (maximum allowed). The scheduler uses requests to decide where to place pods. The kubelet enforces limits at runtime.

рҹ“Ӣ
Requests

"I need at least this much." Used by the scheduler for placement. The node must have this amount free or the pod won't be scheduled.

рҹҡ§
Limits

"Don't use more than this." Enforced by the kubelet. CPU exceeds limit вҶ’ throttled. Memory exceeds limit вҶ’ OOMKilled.

anihpj-resources.yaml 
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: anihpj
spec:
  containers:
  - name: django
    image: anihpj:v1
    resources:
      requests:                  # Scheduling guarantee
        cpu: "250m"              # 0.25 CPU cores (250 millicores)
        memory: "256Mi"          # 256 MiB RAM
      limits:                    # Hard ceiling
        cpu: "500m"              # 0.5 CPU cores max
        memory: "512Mi"          # 512 MiB RAM max

CPU & Memory Units

вҡҷпёҸ CPU: 1000m = 1 core
100m = 0.1 core, 250m = 0.25 core, 1 = 1 full core, 2 = 2 cores
рҹ’ҫ Memory: Mi, Gi
128Mi = 128 MiB, 1Gi = 1 GiB. Use Mi/Gi (binary) not MB/GB (decimal).
вҸі CPU exceeded
Throttled вҖ” process slows down but keeps running. Not killed.
рҹ’Җ Memory exceeded
OOMKilled вҖ” container is killed immediately. Restarts per restartPolicy.
Requests vs Limits вҖ” Visual Explanation
  Node Capacity: 4 CPU, 8Gi Memory
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ anihpj container в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                            в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  CPU:    [====request 250m====|...limit 500m....]             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ          в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җ guaranteed в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Өв”ңв”Җв”Җ can burst в”Җв”Җв”Ө               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                            в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Memory: [====request 256Mi===|...limit 512Mi...]             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ          в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җ guaranteed в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Өв”ңв”Җв”Җ can use в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                   вҶ‘ exceed = OOMKill рҹ’Җ      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  What happens:                                                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ Actual CPU  = 300m  вҶ’ вң… Within limit, no throttle         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ Actual CPU  = 600m  вҶ’ вҸі Throttled to 500m                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ Actual Mem  = 400Mi вҶ’ вң… Within limit, fine                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җ Actual Mem  = 600Mi вҶ’ рҹ’Җ OOMKilled! Container restarts    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
рҹҡЁ Never Skip Resource Settings in Production
  • No requests: Scheduler doesn't know what the pod needs вҶ’ random placement вҶ’ resource contention
  • No limits: Pod can consume ALL available node memory вҶ’ OOMs other pods вҶ’ cascading failure
  • Always set both on every production container!
Monitoring Resource Usage 
# Check actual usage vs requests/limits
kubectl top pods
# NAME      CPU(cores)   MEMORY(bytes)
# anihpj   120m         180Mi

kubectl top nodes
# NAME       CPU(cores)   CPU%   MEMORY(bytes)   MEMORY%
# worker-1   850m         42%    3200Mi           40%

# Find pods with no resource limits (dangerous!)
kubectl get pods -A -o json | jq '.items[] | 
  select(.spec.containers[].resources.limits == null) | 
  .metadata.name'

Troubleshooting OOMKilled Pods

One of the most common issues on the CKA and in production. Here's a systematic approach:

OOMKilled Diagnosis Steps 
# Step 1: Find OOMKilled pods
kubectl get pods -A | grep OOMKilled
kubectl get pods -A | grep CrashLoopBackOff   # often caused by OOM

# Step 2: Check the pod's last state
kubectl describe pod anihpj-abc12 | grep -A5 "Last State"
#   Last State:  Terminated
#     Reason:    OOMKilled
#     Exit Code: 137       вҶҗ 137 = SIGKILL (memory limit hit)

# Step 3: Check current resource config
kubectl get pod anihpj-abc12 -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].resources}'
# {"limits":{"memory":"256Mi"},"requests":{"memory":"128Mi"}}

# Step 4: Check actual usage (if pod is running)
kubectl top pod anihpj-abc12
# CPU: 100m    MEMORY: 245Mi   вҶҗ close to 256Mi limit!

# Step 5: Fix вҖ” increase memory limit
kubectl set resources deployment/anihpj -c django \
  --limits=memory=512Mi --requests=memory=256Mi
OOMKill Decision Tree вҖ” Is It the Container or the Node?
  Pod terminated with Exit Code 137?
       в”Ӯ
       в”ңв”Җв”Җ YES вҶ’ OOMKilled!
       в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ
       в”Ӯ   Was the pod using more memory
       в”Ӯ   than its spec.resources.limits.memory?
       в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ
       в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
       в”Ӯ   YES        NO
       в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
       в”Ӯ   Container  Node-level OOM
       в”Ӯ   limit hit  (node ran out of memory)
       в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
       в”Ӯ   Fix:       Fix: add more nodes,
       в”Ӯ   Increase   reduce other workloads,
       в”Ӯ   memory     or fix memory leaks
       в”Ӯ   limits
       в”Ӯ
       в””в”Җв”Җ Exit Code 1 = app error (not OOM)

15.2 QoS Classes вҖ” Quality of Service

Kubernetes automatically assigns a QoS class to every pod based on its resource configuration. This determines the eviction priority when a node runs out of resources.

QoS Class Criteria Eviction Priority anihpj Use Case
Guaranteed Every container has requests == limits (both CPU & memory set and equal) Last to be evicted (safest) рҹӣЎпёҸ вң… Production anihpj
Burstable At least one container has requests < limits (or only requests set) Evicted after BestEffort вҡ пёҸ вҡ пёҸ Dev/staging anihpj
BestEffort No requests or limits set on any container First to be evicted (riskiest) рҹ’Җ вқҢ Never in production
QoS Eviction Order вҖ” Node Under Memory Pressure
  Node memory: 95% used вҖ” kubelet starts evicting pods!

  Eviction Order:
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                                                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ’Җ FIRST: BestEffort pods                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Pod: test-pod (no requests, no limits)      в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҶ’ EVICTED immediately                       в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в–ј (still need more memory?)              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вҡ пёҸ SECOND: Burstable pods                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Pod: dev-anihpj (requests < limits)         в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҶ’ EVICTED if using more than requests       в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в–ј (extreme pressure only)                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹӣЎпёҸ LAST: Guaranteed pods                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Pod: prod-anihpj (requests == limits)       в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҶ’ Only evicted as absolute last resort      в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
QoS Examples 
# Guaranteed QoS вҖ” requests EQUAL limits
resources:
  requests:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"          # same as request
    memory: "512Mi"      # same as request
# вҶ’ QoS: Guaranteed вң…

---
# Burstable QoS вҖ” requests LESS THAN limits
resources:
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"          # more than request
    memory: "512Mi"      # more than request
# вҶ’ QoS: Burstable вҡ пёҸ

---
# BestEffort QoS вҖ” NO resources at all
# (just don't include the resources section)
# вҶ’ QoS: BestEffort рҹ’Җ
Check Pod QoS Class 
# Check QoS class of a pod
kubectl get pod anihpj -o jsonpath='{.status.qosClass}'
# Guaranteed

# Check QoS of all pods
kubectl get pods -o custom-columns='NAME:.metadata.name,QOS:.status.qosClass'
# NAME              QOS
# anihpj-abc12      Guaranteed
# dev-app-xyz       Burstable
# test-pod          BestEffort
рҹ’Ў Production QoS Strategy for anihpj

For your production stack, aim for these QoS classes:

  • Guaranteed: anihpj Django (production), PostgreSQL вҖ” set requests == limits
  • Burstable: anihpj staging, Redis cache вҖ” allow bursting for efficiency
  • BestEffort: Only for throwaway test pods in dev. Never in production!

Pro tip: Use Guaranteed for anything facing users. Use Burstable for internal services that benefit from occasional burst capacity.

15.3 LimitRange вҖ” Namespace Default Limits

A LimitRange sets default resource requests and limits for containers in a namespace. It also enforces min/max boundaries. This prevents pods from running without any resource configuration.

limitrange.yaml 
apiVersion: v1
kind: LimitRange
metadata:
  name: anihpj-limits
  namespace: anihpj-dev
spec:
  limits:
  - type: Container
    default:                     # Default LIMITS (if not specified)
      cpu: "500m"
      memory: "512Mi"
    defaultRequest:              # Default REQUESTS (if not specified)
      cpu: "250m"
      memory: "256Mi"
    max:                         # Maximum allowed
      cpu: "2"
      memory: "2Gi"
    min:                         # Minimum allowed
      cpu: "50m"
      memory: "32Mi"
  - type: Pod
    max:
      cpu: "4"
      memory: "4Gi"
LimitRange in Action 
# Create namespace and LimitRange
kubectl create namespace anihpj-dev
kubectl apply -f limitrange.yaml

# Create pod WITHOUT specifying resources
kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx -n anihpj-dev

# Check вҖ” LimitRange injected default values!
kubectl get pod test-pod -n anihpj-dev -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].resources}'
# {"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"512Mi"},"requests":{"cpu":"250m","memory":"256Mi"}}

# Try to create pod exceeding max
# вҶ’ Error: maximum cpu usage per Container is 2, but limit is 4
LimitRange вҖ” Automatic Defaults
  Namespace: anihpj-dev (with LimitRange)

  Pod created WITHOUT resources:        Pod received DEFAULTS:
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ spec:                    в”Ӯ   вҶ’     в”Ӯ spec:                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   containers:            в”Ӯ   вҶ’     в”Ӯ   containers:                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   - name: nginx          в”Ӯ   вҶ’     в”Ӯ   - name: nginx              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     image: nginx         в”Ӯ   вҶ’     в”Ӯ     image: nginx             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     # no resources!      в”Ӯ   вҶ’     в”Ӯ     resources:               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ   вҶ’     в”Ӯ       requests:              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ   вҶ’     в”Ӯ         cpu: "250m"    вҶҗ injected! в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ   вҶ’     в”Ӯ         memory: "256Mi" вҶҗ injected!в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ   вҶ’     в”Ӯ       limits:               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ   вҶ’     в”Ӯ         cpu: "500m"    вҶҗ injected! в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ   вҶ’     в”Ӯ         memory: "512Mi" вҶҗ injected!в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

15.4 ResourceQuota вҖ” Namespace Capacity Control

A ResourceQuota limits the total resources consumed across all pods in a namespace. This prevents one team from using all cluster resources and is a key tool for multi-tenant clusters.

resourcequota.yaml 
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: anihpj-quota
  namespace: anihpj-dev
spec:
  hard:
    # Compute resources
    requests.cpu: "4"            # Total CPU requests across all pods
    requests.memory: "8Gi"       # Total memory requests
    limits.cpu: "8"              # Total CPU limits
    limits.memory: "16Gi"        # Total memory limits

    # Object count limits
    pods: "10"                   # Max 10 pods in this namespace
    services: "5"                # Max 5 services
    persistentvolumeclaims: "5"  # Max 5 PVCs
    configmaps: "20"
    secrets: "20"
ResourceQuota Commands 
# Apply quota
kubectl apply -f resourcequota.yaml

# Check quota usage
kubectl get resourcequota -n anihpj-dev
# NAME           AGE   REQUEST                                              LIMIT
# anihpj-quota   5m    requests.cpu: 750m/4, requests.memory: 768Mi/8Gi    limits.cpu: 1500m/8, limits.memory: 1536Mi/16Gi

# Detailed view
kubectl describe resourcequota anihpj-quota -n anihpj-dev
# Name:                   anihpj-quota
# Namespace:              anihpj-dev
# Resource                Used    Hard
# --------                ----    ----
# limits.cpu              1500m   8
# limits.memory           1536Mi  16Gi
# pods                    3       10
# requests.cpu            750m    4
# requests.memory         768Mi   8Gi
Resource Control Hierarchy вҖ” 3 Levels
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  LEVEL 1: ResourceQuota (namespace total budget)               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ "anihpj-dev can use max 4 CPUs, 8Gi memory total"       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Used: 750m / 4 CPU    Used: 768Mi / 8Gi memory         в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ                                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в–ј                                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  LEVEL 2: LimitRange (per-container defaults & bounds)       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ "Each container: default 250m req, 500m limit"           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ "Min: 50m CPU, Max: 2 CPU per container"                 в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ                                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в–ј                                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  LEVEL 3: Pod spec (individual container settings)            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ anihpj-1   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ anihpj-2   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ anihpj-3   в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ req: 250m  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ req: 250m  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ req: 250m  в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ lim: 500m  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ lim: 500m  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ lim: 500m  в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Total: 750m request, 1500m limit (within quota вң…)           в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
вҡ пёҸ ResourceQuota Requires Resource Specs

When a ResourceQuota is active in a namespace, every pod must specify resource requests and limits. Without them, the pod creation will be rejected. Use a LimitRange to auto-inject defaults so developers don't have to specify them manually.

рҹ”‘ Chapter 15 Quick Reference

Action Command
Check pod resource usagekubectl top pods
Check node resource usagekubectl top nodes
View pod QoS classkubectl get pod NAME -o jsonpath='{.status.qosClass}'
View LimitRangekubectl describe limitrange -n NAMESPACE
View ResourceQuotakubectl describe resourcequota -n NAMESPACE
Check OOMKilled podskubectl get pods | grep OOMKilled
View container resourceskubectl get pod NAME -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[*].resources}'

вҡ пёҸ CKA Gotcha: Resource Management Traps

  • Requests vs Limits вҖ” requests = guaranteed minimum (used for scheduling). limits = maximum allowed (enforced at runtime). Scheduler uses requests, NOT limits
  • CPU units вҖ” 1 = 1 vCPU. 500m = 0.5 CPU. 100m = 0.1 CPU. The exam loves asking you to convert between these
  • Memory units вҖ” Mi (mebibytes) вү  M (megabytes). Use Mi for consistency. 128Mi = 134MB
  • QoS classes вҖ” Guaranteed (requests=limits for all containers), Burstable (some requests set), BestEffort (no requests/limits). OOM killer targets BestEffort first
  • LimitRange applies to NEW pods only вҖ” Existing pods aren't affected. ResourceQuota blocks creation when the namespace total is exceeded

CKA Speed Tip: Find which pods are hogging resources:
kubectl top pods -A --sort-by=cpu вҖ” highest CPU consumers
kubectl top pods -A --sort-by=memory вҖ” highest memory consumers
kubectl describe node <name> | grep -A5 "Allocated resources" вҖ” node-level usage summary

вҡЎ Resource management commands: kubectl set resources deployment/web -c=nginx --requests=cpu=100m,memory=128Mi --limits=cpu=500m,memory=256Mi
kubectl create quota my-quota --hard=pods=10,requests.cpu=4,requests.memory=8Gi -n dev
kubectl describe resourcequota -n dev вҖ” see used vs hard limits

Chapter 16: Jobs, CronJobs, DaemonSets, and StatefulSets

Deployments aren't the only workload type. Kubernetes provides specialized controllers for run-to-completion tasks (Jobs), scheduled tasks (CronJobs), one-pod-per-node daemons (DaemonSets), and stateful applications (StatefulSets). Mastering when to use each is critical for the CKA exam.

рҹҹЎ Intermediate вҸұпёҸ ~1.5 hours вҡҷпёҸ Workload Types рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

рҹҺҜ What You'll Learn

  • вң… Create Jobs for one-off tasks (database migrations, batch processing)
  • вң… Schedule recurring tasks with CronJobs (backups, reports)
  • вң… Deploy DaemonSets for per-node system services (logging, monitoring)
  • вң… Understand StatefulSets for databases and stateful applications
  • вң… Choose the right workload type for any scenario
рҹҺ“ CKA Relevance
Workloads & Scheduling 15% Troubleshooting 30%
Workload Type Questions on the CKA

The CKA often asks you to create Jobs and CronJobs. Know these imperative shortcuts:

  • kubectl create job NAME --image=IMG -- COMMAND
  • kubectl create cronjob NAME --image=IMG --schedule="*/5 * * * *" -- COMMAND
  • DaemonSets require YAML (no imperative shortcut)

16.1 Jobs вҖ” Run to Completion

A Job creates one or more pods and ensures they run to successful completion. Unlike Deployments (which run forever), Jobs stop once the task is done. Perfect for batch processing, database migrations, and one-off scripts.

Imperative Job Creation 
# Create a simple job
kubectl create job db-migrate --image=anihpj:v1 -- python manage.py migrate

# Generate YAML first (exam tip: always verify before applying)
kubectl create job db-migrate --image=anihpj:v1 \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml -- python manage.py migrate > job.yaml

# Check job status
kubectl get jobs
# NAME         COMPLETIONS   DURATION   AGE
# db-migrate   1/1           12s        30s

# View job pods
kubectl get pods --selector=job-name=db-migrate
# NAME               READY   STATUS      RESTARTS   AGE
# db-migrate-abc12   0/1     Completed   0          45s

# Check logs
kubectl logs job/db-migrate
anihpj-migrate-job.yaml 
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: anihpj-db-migrate
spec:
  completions: 1              # How many pods must complete successfully
  parallelism: 1              # How many pods run simultaneously
  backoffLimit: 3             # Retry up to 3 times before marking failed
  activeDeadlineSeconds: 300  # Timeout: kill after 5 minutes
  ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 60 # Auto-delete finished job after 60s
  template:
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never    # Jobs: Never or OnFailure (NOT Always!)
      containers:
      - name: migrate
        image: anihpj:v1
        command: ["python", "manage.py", "migrate"]
        env:
        - name: DATABASE_URL
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: anihpj-db-secret
              key: url

Job Configuration Fields

вң… completions
How many pods must succeed. Default: 1. Set to N for batch: process N items.
вҡЎ parallelism
How many pods run at once. Default: 1. Increase for faster batch processing.
рҹ”„ backoffLimit
Retry count before job is marked Failed. Default: 6. Set lower for fast failure.
вҸұпёҸ activeDeadlineSeconds
Hard timeout. Job is killed if it runs longer, regardless of retries.
рҹ—‘пёҸ ttlSecondsAfterFinished
Auto-cleanup. Completed/failed job is deleted after this many seconds.
рҹ”Ғ restartPolicy
Never (creates new pod on failure) or OnFailure (restarts container in same pod). Never Always!
Job Lifecycle вҖ” Run, Complete, Stop
  Job: anihpj-db-migrate (completions: 1, backoffLimit: 3)

  Attempt 1:
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ Pod: migrate в”Ӯ вҶ’ Runs python manage.py migrate
  в”Ӯ   рҹ”„ Running в”Ӯ вҶ’ ...
  в”Ӯ   вң… Success в”Ӯ вҶ’ Exit code 0
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  Job Status: Complete вң…  (1/1 completions)

  в”Җв”Җв”Җ What if it fails? в”Җв”Җв”Җ

  Attempt 1:
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ Pod: migrate в”Ӯ вҶ’ DB connection refused
  в”Ӯ   вқҢ Failed  в”Ӯ вҶ’ Exit code 1
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  Attempt 2:  (backoff: 10s wait)
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ Pod: migrate в”Ӯ вҶ’ DB now available
  в”Ӯ   вң… Success в”Ӯ вҶ’ Exit code 0
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  Job Status: Complete вң…  (1/1 completions, 1 retry)
вҡ пёҸ CKA Trap: restartPolicy

Jobs CANNOT use restartPolicy: Always (that's for Deployments). If you forget this, the Job will be rejected. Use Never or OnFailure.

Batch Processing with Parallelism

Jobs can process multiple items in parallel. Set completions for how many must finish and parallelism for how many run at once.

batch-processing-job.yaml 
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: anihpj-image-processor
spec:
  completions: 10       # 10 total items to process
  parallelism: 3        # Run 3 pods at a time
  backoffLimit: 5
  template:
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
      - name: processor
        image: anihpj:v1
        command: ["python", "manage.py", "process_images"]
Batch Job: 10 Completions, 3 Parallel
  Job: anihpj-image-processor (completions: 10, parallelism: 3)

  Wave 1: [рҹ“Ұ Pod-1] [рҹ“Ұ Pod-2] [рҹ“Ұ Pod-3]   вҶҗ 3 running
               вң…         вң…         вң…
  Wave 2: [рҹ“Ұ Pod-4] [рҹ“Ұ Pod-5] [рҹ“Ұ Pod-6]   вҶҗ 3 running
               вң…         вң…         вң…
  Wave 3: [рҹ“Ұ Pod-7] [рҹ“Ұ Pod-8] [рҹ“Ұ Pod-9]   вҶҗ 3 running
               вң…         вң…         вң…
  Wave 4: [рҹ“Ұ Pod-10]                        вҶҗ 1 running (last item)
               вң…
  Job Status: Complete вң…  (10/10 completions)

  Total time: ~4 waves instead of 10 sequential runs!
  Parallelism gives ~3x speedup вҡЎ

16.2 CronJobs вҖ” Scheduled Tasks

CronJobs are like Unix cron but in Kubernetes. They create Jobs on a schedule вҖ” perfect for backups, reports, cleanup tasks, and periodic data processing.

Imperative CronJob Creation 
# Create a CronJob вҖ” daily backup at 2 AM
kubectl create cronjob anihpj-backup \
  --image=anihpj:v1 \
  --schedule="0 2 * * *" \
  -- python manage.py dumpdata --output=/backup/db.json

# Check CronJob
kubectl get cronjobs
# NAME             SCHEDULE      SUSPEND   ACTIVE   LAST SCHEDULE   AGE
# anihpj-backup    0 2 * * *     False     0        30s             5m

# View recently created Jobs
kubectl get jobs --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
anihpj-backup-cronjob.yaml 
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: anihpj-backup
spec:
  schedule: "0 2 * * *"          # Daily at 2:00 AM
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid       # Don't start new if previous still running
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 3   # Keep last 3 successful job records
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 1       # Keep last 1 failed job record
  startingDeadlineSeconds: 200    # If job can't start in 200s, skip this run
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      backoffLimit: 2
      template:
        spec:
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
          containers:
          - name: backup
            image: anihpj:v1
            command:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - "python manage.py dumpdata | gzip > /backup/$(date +%Y%m%d).json.gz"
            volumeMounts:
            - name: backup-volume
              mountPath: /backup
          volumes:
          - name: backup-volume
            persistentVolumeClaim:
              claimName: backup-pvc
Schedule Meaning Use Case
*/5 * * * *Every 5 minutesHealth checks, cache refresh
0 2 * * *Daily at 2:00 AMDatabase backup
0 0 * * 0Every Sunday at midnightWeekly report generation
0 */6 * * *Every 6 hoursData sync, cleanup
30 8 1 * *1st of each month at 8:30 AMMonthly invoice generation
Cron Expression Format
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ minute (0-59)
  в”Ӯ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ hour (0-23)
  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ day of month (1-31)
  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ month (1-12)
  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ day of week (0-6, Sun=0)
  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  * * * * *

  Examples:
  0 2 * * *     = "At 02:00 every day"
  */5 * * * *   = "Every 5 minutes"
  0 0 * * 0     = "At midnight every Sunday"
  30 8 1 * *    = "At 08:30 on the 1st of every month"
рҹ”’
concurrencyPolicy: Forbid

Don't start new job if previous is still running. Prevents overlap. Safest for DB backups.

рҹ”„
concurrencyPolicy: Replace

Kill running job and start a new one. Good for "latest data only" scenarios.

рҹ“– Third Option: concurrencyPolicy: Allow (default)

Allow lets multiple jobs run simultaneously. If CronJob fires every 5 minutes but a job takes 7 minutes, you'll have overlapping runs. This is fine for independent tasks (e.g., sending reports) but dangerous for database operations where concurrent writes could corrupt data. Always set Forbid for DB backups.

16.3 DaemonSets вҖ” One Pod Per Node

A DaemonSet ensures that exactly one copy of a pod runs on every node (or every selected node). When a new node joins the cluster, the DaemonSet automatically schedules a pod there. When a node is removed, the pod is garbage-collected.

рҹ“– Common DaemonSet Use Cases
  • рҹ“Ӣ Log collectors вҖ” Fluentd, Filebeat, Promtail
  • рҹ“Ҡ Monitoring agents вҖ” Node Exporter, Datadog Agent
  • рҹҢҗ Network plugins вҖ” Calico, Cilium, Flannel (CNI)
  • рҹ’ҫ Storage daemons вҖ” Ceph, GlusterFS
  • рҹ”җ Security agents вҖ” Falco, Twistlock
fluentd-daemonset.yaml 
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: fluentd
  namespace: kube-system
  labels:
    app: fluentd
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: fluentd
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: fluentd
    spec:
      tolerations:
      # Tolerate control plane taint to run on ALL nodes
      - key: node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane
        operator: Exists
        effect: NoSchedule
      containers:
      - name: fluentd
        image: fluentd:v1.16
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "100m"
            memory: "200Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "200m"
            memory: "400Mi"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: varlog
          mountPath: /var/log
        - name: varlibdockercontainers
          mountPath: /var/lib/docker/containers
          readOnly: true
      volumes:
      - name: varlog
        hostPath:
          path: /var/log
      - name: varlibdockercontainers
        hostPath:
          path: /var/lib/docker/containers
DaemonSet вҖ” Automatic Pod Per Node
  DaemonSet: "fluentd"   (replicas: N/A вҖ” auto-managed)

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ    worker-1      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    worker-2      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    worker-3      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  fluentd   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  fluentd   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  fluentd   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ӣ logs   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ӣ logs   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ӣ logs   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  + anihpj pods   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  + anihpj pods   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  + anihpj pods   в”Ӯ
