Get signed certkubectl get csr dev-user -o jsonpath='{.status.certificate}' | base64 -d > user.crt
Check expirykubeadm certs check-expiration
Renew all certskubeadm certs renew all
Inspect certopenssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt -text -noout

CertificateSigningRequest вҖ” Exam Skeleton

CSR creation is a common CKA task. Know this pattern cold.

# Step 1: Generate key + CSR (on command line)
openssl genrsa -out dev-user.key 2048
openssl req -new -key dev-user.key \
  -subj "/CN=dev-user/O=dev-team" \
  -out dev-user.csr

# Step 2: Create Kubernetes CSR object
apiVersion: certificates.k8s.io/v1
kind: CertificateSigningRequest
metadata:
  name: dev-user                         # в—Җв”Җв”Җ CSR name
spec:
  request: $(cat dev-user.csr | base64 | tr -d '\n')  # в—Җв”Җв”Җ base64-encoded CSR
  signerName: kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client       # в—Җв”Җв”Җ REQUIRED signer
  usages:
  - client auth                          # в—Җв”Җв”Җ for user authentication
  expirationSeconds: 86400               # в—Җв”Җв”Җ optional: 24 hours

# Step 3: Approve and extract
# kubectl certificate approve dev-user
# kubectl get csr dev-user -o jsonpath='{.status.certificate}' | base64 -d > dev-user.crt

Certificate Exam Traps

  • вқҢ CSR request field takes the raw CSR file content
  • вң… The request field requires base64-encoded CSR content
  • signerName is required since K8s 1.22 вҖ” use kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client for user certs
  • Certificates expire after 1 year by default вҖ” kubeadm certs check-expiration shows all dates
  • After renewing certs, you MUST restart control plane pods (they load certs at startup)
  • The CN (Common Name) in the CSR becomes the username in K8s. The O (Organization) becomes the group
  • /etc/kubernetes/pki/ is the default cert location вҖ” memorize this path

Find Certificate Fields Fast

kubectl explain csr.spec                       # CSR spec
kubectl explain csr.spec.request               # base64-encoded CSR
kubectl explain csr.spec.signerName            # required signer
kubectl explain csr.spec.usages                # client auth, server auth, etc.
kubectl explain csr.spec.expirationSeconds     # cert lifetime
6+ Component Certificates
1 yr Default Cert Lifetime
/etc/kubernetes/pki/ Default Cert Location
1 Root CA Signs Everything

26.1 Kubernetes PKI Overview

PKI (Public Key Infrastructure) is the system of certificates that secure all communication in a Kubernetes cluster. At the top is a Root Certificate Authority (CA) that signs certificates for every cluster component.

Kubernetes PKI Architecture вҖ” The Certificate Tree
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                      KUBERNETES PKI HIERARCHY                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ   рҹҸӣпёҸ ROOT CA          в”Ӯ                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ   /etc/kubernetes/    в”Ӯ                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ   pki/ca.crt          в”Ӯ                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ       ca.key          в”Ӯ                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ signs                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в–ј                    в–ј                    в–ј                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ рҹҢҗ API Server в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ рҹ’ҫ etcd       в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ рҹ“Ў kubelet    в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ apiserver.crt в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ server.crt   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ kubelet.crt   в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ apiserver.key в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ server.key   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ kubelet.key   в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Also needs:   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ peer.crt     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ Authenticates в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ kubelet-clientв”Ӯ   в”Ӯ peer.key     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ to API server в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ .crt/.key     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ                                                                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ also serves as client to:                                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в–ј                                                                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ рҹ“Ӣ scheduler  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ рҹ”„ controller в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ рҹҢҗ front-proxyв”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   manager     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Uses kubeconf в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ Uses kubeconf в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ front-proxy-  в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ with embedded в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ with embedded в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ ca.crt/key    в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ client cert   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ client cert   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ (aggregation) в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ғ All certificates live under: /etc/kubernetes/pki/                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ғ etcd certs specifically: /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/                       в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
рҹ”’
Encryption

All component communication uses TLS вҖ” no plaintext data ever crosses the network.

рҹӘӘ
Identity

Certificates prove component identity. The API server verifies kubelet is who it claims to be.

рҹӨқ
Mutual TLS

Both sides present certificates. API server authenticates kubelet AND kubelet authenticates API server.

26.2 Certificate Locations and Purpose

Every certificate in the Kubernetes cluster has a specific purpose. For the CKA, you must know where they live and what they do.

File Used By Purpose
ca.crt / ca.keyCluster CARoot CA вҖ” signs all other certificates
apiserver.crt / .keyAPI ServerServe HTTPS on port 6443
apiserver-kubelet-client.crtAPI ServerAPI server authenticating to kubelets
apiserver-etcd-client.crtAPI ServerAPI server authenticating to etcd
etcd/server.crt / .keyetcdetcd serving TLS to API server
etcd/peer.crt / .keyetcdetcd-to-etcd peer communication (HA)
front-proxy-ca.crtAggregationAPI aggregation layer (metrics server)
sa.pub / sa.keySA tokensSigning/verifying ServiceAccount tokens
inspect-certificates.sh 
# List all certificate files
ls -la /etc/kubernetes/pki/
# ca.crt  ca.key  apiserver.crt  apiserver.key  ...
ls -la /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/
# ca.crt  ca.key  server.crt  server.key  peer.crt  peer.key

# Inspect API server certificate details
openssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt -text -noout

# Key fields to check:
# - Issuer: kubernetes (the CA that signed it)
# - Subject: CN=kube-apiserver
# - Not After: expiry date
# - Subject Alternative Names (SANs): all valid hostnames/IPs

# Check who signed a certificate
openssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt -issuer -noout
# issuer=CN = kubernetes

# Verify cert is signed by the CA
openssl verify -CAfile /etc/kubernetes/pki/ca.crt \
  /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt
# /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt: OK
Certificate File Layout on Disk
  /etc/kubernetes/
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ pki/                                 в—Җв”Җв”Җ Main PKI directory
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ ca.crt                           в—Җв”Җв”Җ Root CA certificate
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ ca.key                           в—Җв”Җв”Җ Root CA private key рҹ”’
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ apiserver.crt                    в—Җв”Җв”Җ API server TLS cert
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ apiserver.key                    в—Җв”Җв”Җ API server private key рҹ”’
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ apiserver-kubelet-client.crt     в—Җв”Җв”Җ APIвҶ’kubelet client cert
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ apiserver-kubelet-client.key     в—Җв”Җв”Җ APIвҶ’kubelet private key рҹ”’
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ apiserver-etcd-client.crt        в—Җв”Җв”Җ APIвҶ’etcd client cert
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ apiserver-etcd-client.key        в—Җв”Җв”Җ APIвҶ’etcd private key рҹ”’
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ front-proxy-ca.crt              в—Җв”Җв”Җ Aggregation CA
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ front-proxy-ca.key              в—Җв”Җв”Җ Aggregation CA key рҹ”’
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ front-proxy-client.crt          в—Җв”Җв”Җ Aggregation client cert
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ front-proxy-client.key          в—Җв”Җв”Җ Aggregation client key рҹ”’
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ sa.pub                           в—Җв”Җв”Җ SA token verification key
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ sa.key                           в—Җв”Җв”Җ SA token signing key рҹ”’
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ etcd/                            в—Җв”Җв”Җ etcd-specific certs
  в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ ca.crt                       в—Җв”Җв”Җ etcd CA (may be separate)
  в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ ca.key
  в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ server.crt                   в—Җв”Җв”Җ etcd server TLS
  в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ server.key
  в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ peer.crt                     в—Җв”Җв”Җ etcd peer communication
  в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ peer.key
  в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ healthcheck-client.crt       в—Җв”Җв”Җ etcd health checks
  в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ healthcheck-client.key
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ admin.conf                           в—Җв”Җв”Җ cluster-admin kubeconfig
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ controller-manager.conf              в—Җв”Җв”Җ controller-manager kubeconfig
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ scheduler.conf                       в—Җв”Җв”Җ scheduler kubeconfig
  в””в”Җв”Җ kubelet.conf                         в—Җв”Җв”Җ kubelet kubeconfig
вҡ пёҸ Private Keys Are Secrets

The .key files are private keys вҖ” anyone with access can impersonate that component. In production, restrict access to /etc/kubernetes/pki/ to root only. On the CKA exam, you'll SSH into the control plane node to read these files.

26.3 Certificate Signing Requests (CSR)

To add a new user to the cluster, you create a CSR and have the K8s CA sign it. This process is heavily tested on the CKA exam.

1
Generate Key

openssl genrsa -out user.key 2048

2
Create CSR

openssl req -new -key user.key -subj "/CN=dev-user/O=dev-team"

3
Submit to K8s

Create CertificateSigningRequest YAML with base64-encoded CSR

4
Approve

kubectl certificate approve dev-user

5
Extract Cert

Get the signed certificate from the CSR's .status.certificate

csr-complete-workflow.sh 
# в”Җв”Җ COMPLETE CSR WORKFLOW вҖ” CKA EXAM PATTERN в”Җв”Җ

# Step 1: Generate a private key
openssl genrsa -out dev-user.key 2048

# Step 2: Create a Certificate Signing Request
# CN = username, O = group (for RBAC)
openssl req -new -key dev-user.key \
  -subj "/CN=dev-user/O=dev-team" \
  -out dev-user.csr

# Step 3: Encode the CSR in base64 (single line, no wrapping)
CSR_BASE64=$(cat dev-user.csr | base64 | tr -d '\n')

# Step 4: Create the K8s CSR YAML
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: certificates.k8s.io/v1
kind: CertificateSigningRequest
metadata:
  name: dev-user
spec:
  request: ${CSR_BASE64}
  signerName: kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client
  usages:
  - client auth
EOF

# Step 5: Check CSR status
kubectl get csr
# NAME       AGE   SIGNERNAME                          CONDITION
# dev-user   5s    kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client  Pending

# Step 6: Approve the CSR
kubectl certificate approve dev-user

# Step 7: Extract the signed certificate
kubectl get csr dev-user -o jsonpath='{.status.certificate}' \
  | base64 -d > dev-user.crt

# Step 8: Verify the certificate
openssl x509 -in dev-user.crt -text -noout
# Subject: CN=dev-user, O=dev-team

# Step 9: Add user to kubeconfig
kubectl config set-credentials dev-user \
  --client-certificate=dev-user.crt \
  --client-key=dev-user.key

kubectl config set-context dev-user@kubernetes \
  --cluster=kubernetes \
  --user=dev-user \
  --namespace=anihpj
рҹ’Ў CKA Shortcut: CSR Encoding

Use cat dev-user.csr | base64 | tr -d '\n' to get the single-line base64 string for the CSR YAML. The tr -d '\n' removes line breaks that would break the YAML.

26.4 Checking Certificate Expiry

Kubernetes certificates expire after 1 year by default. If they expire, cluster components stop communicating and the cluster goes down. Monitoring expiry is critical.

check-expiry.sh 
# в”Җв”Җ METHOD 1: kubeadm (EASIEST вҖ” use on CKA exam) в”Җв”Җ
kubeadm certs check-expiration
# CERTIFICATE                EXPIRES                  RESIDUAL TIME
# admin.conf                 Apr 01, 2027 00:00 UTC   364d
# apiserver                  Apr 01, 2027 00:00 UTC   364d
# apiserver-etcd-client      Apr 01, 2027 00:00 UTC   364d
# apiserver-kubelet-client   Apr 01, 2027 00:00 UTC   364d
# controller-manager.conf    Apr 01, 2027 00:00 UTC   364d
# etcd-healthcheck-client    Apr 01, 2027 00:00 UTC   364d
# etcd-peer                  Apr 01, 2027 00:00 UTC   364d
# etcd-server                Apr 01, 2027 00:00 UTC   364d
# front-proxy-client         Apr 01, 2027 00:00 UTC   364d
# scheduler.conf             Apr 01, 2027 00:00 UTC   364d

# в”Җв”Җ METHOD 2: openssl (manual вҖ” for specific cert) в”Җв”Җ
openssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt \
  -noout -enddate
# notAfter=Apr  1 00:00:00 2027 GMT

# в”Җв”Җ METHOD 3: Check specific fields в”Җв”Җ
openssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt \
  -noout -subject -issuer -dates
# subject=CN = kube-apiserver
# issuer=CN = kubernetes
# notBefore=Apr  1 00:00:00 2026 GMT
# notAfter=Apr  1 00:00:00 2027 GMT
рҹҡЁ Expired Certificates = Cluster Down

If the API server certificate expires, kubectl stops working entirely. If etcd certificates expire, the API server can't read/write state. Always check expiry at least monthly in production. On the CKA exam, kubeadm certs check-expiration is the answer.

26.5 Renewing Certificates

Certificate renewal is a critical cluster maintenance task and a common CKA exam topic.

renew-certificates.sh 
# в”Җв”Җ Renew ALL certificates at once в”Җв”Җ
kubeadm certs renew all
# [renew] Reading configuration from the cluster...
# certificate embedded in admin.conf renewed
# certificate apiserver renewed
# certificate apiserver-kubelet-client renewed
# ...

# в”Җв”Җ Renew a SPECIFIC certificate в”Җв”Җ
kubeadm certs renew apiserver
kubeadm certs renew apiserver-kubelet-client
kubeadm certs renew etcd-server

# в”Җв”Җ CRITICAL: Restart control plane after renewal в”Җв”Җ
# Control plane pods load certs at startup вҖ” they need restart!

# Option A: If using static pods (kubeadm default)
# Move and restore manifests to trigger restart:
cd /etc/kubernetes/manifests/
mv kube-apiserver.yaml /tmp/
# Wait for pod to stop...
mv /tmp/kube-apiserver.yaml .
# Repeat for controller-manager, scheduler, etcd

# Option B: Force restart via crictl
crictl pods --name kube-apiserver -q | xargs crictl stopp
crictl pods --name kube-controller-manager -q | xargs crictl stopp
crictl pods --name kube-scheduler -q | xargs crictl stopp

# в”Җв”Җ Verify renewal worked в”Җв”Җ
kubeadm certs check-expiration
# All dates should now show ~1 year from today
Certificate Renewal Workflow
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ              CERTIFICATE RENEWAL PROCEDURE                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ 1. Check Expiry в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җ kubeadm certs check-expiration          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в–ј                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ 2. Renew Certs  в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җ kubeadm certs renew all                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в–ј                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ 3. Restart      в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җ Move/restore manifests or crictl       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    Components   в”Ӯ    (API server, controller, scheduler)     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в–ј                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ 4. Verify       в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җ kubeadm certs check-expiration          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    New Dates    в”Ӯ    (should show ~1 year from now)          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в–ј                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ 5. Update       в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җ Copy new admin.conf to ~/.kube/config  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    kubeconfig   в”Ӯ    cp /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    ~/.kube/config                          в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
вҡ пёҸ Don't Forget kubeconfig!

After renewing certs, the kubeconfig files (admin.conf, etc.) also get updated. Copy the new admin.conf to ~/.kube/config or kubectl will stop working with "certificate expired" errors!

рҹ’Ў Automatic Renewal

kubeadm upgrade automatically renews all certificates during a cluster upgrade. If you upgrade regularly (recommended), you may never need manual renewal.

Certificate Troubleshooting вҖ” Common Errors & Fixes
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ            CERTIFICATE TROUBLESHOOTING QUICK REFERENCE                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вқҢ ERROR: "x509: certificate has expired or is not yet valid"              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вң… FIX:   kubeadm certs renew all вҶ’ restart control plane pods            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вқҢ ERROR: "unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ            signed by unknown authority"                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вң… FIX:   Copy updated admin.conf: cp /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ            ~/.kube/config                                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вқҢ ERROR: "error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)"      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вң… FIX:   Check certificate CN matches expected user. Verify CSR was       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ            approved. Check RBAC bindings.                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вқҢ ERROR: API server won't start after cert renewal                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вң… FIX:   Check /var/log/containers/ for kube-apiserver logs.              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ            Verify cert files exist in /etc/kubernetes/pki/.                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ            Check file permissions (certs should be 644, keys 600).          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вқҢ ERROR: "tls: bad certificate" on etcd                                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вң… FIX:   Renew etcd-specific certs: kubeadm certs renew etcd-server      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ            kubeadm certs renew etcd-peer                                    в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

вң… Certificate DO

  • Check expiry monthly: kubeadm certs check-expiration
  • Always restart control plane after renewal
  • Update ~/.kube/config after renewing admin.conf
  • Use kubeadm upgrade вҖ” it renews certs automatically
  • Set correct CN/O in CSRs (CN=username, O=group)

вқҢ Certificate DON'T

  • Ignore certificate expiry warnings
  • Share private keys (.key files) across teams
  • Skip the signerName field on CSR objects (required since 1.22)
  • Forget to base64-encode the CSR request field
  • Assume certs renew themselves вҖ” only kubeadm upgrade auto-renews

TLS Certificate Verification Checklist

  • openssl x509 -in CERT -text -noout | grep -A1 Subject: вҶ’ verify CN and O fields
  • openssl x509 -in CERT -text -noout | grep "Not After" вҶ’ check expiry date
  • openssl verify -CAfile /etc/kubernetes/pki/ca.crt CERT вҶ’ confirm signed by cluster CA
  • kubeadm certs check-expiration вҶ’ view all certificate expiry dates at once
  • kubectl get csr вҶ’ check pending/approved CSR objects
  • kubectl describe csr NAME вҶ’ verify signerName, status, and conditions

рҹҸӢпёҸ Chapter 26 Practice Exercises

These exercises simulate real CKA certificate questions.

вҸұпёҸ Target: 8 minutes
Exercise 26.1 вҖ” Create User Certificate via CSR

Generate a private key, create a CSR, submit it to Kubernetes, approve it, and extract the signed certificate.

  1. Generate key: openssl genrsa -out exam-user.key 2048
  2. Create CSR: openssl req -new -key exam-user.key -subj "/CN=exam-user/O=exam-team"
  3. Submit K8s CSR object with signerName: kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client
  4. Approve: kubectl certificate approve exam-user
  5. Extract signed cert and verify CN with openssl x509 -text
рҹ’Ў Hint: The CSR request field must be base64-encoded with no line wrapping вҖ” use base64 -w 0.
рҹ“қ View Solution 
# Step 1: Generate private key
openssl genrsa -out exam-user.key 2048

# Step 2: Create CSR (PEM format)
openssl req -new -key exam-user.key \
  -subj "/CN=exam-user/O=exam-team" \
  -out exam-user.csr

# Step 3: Base64-encode the CSR (no line wrapping!)
CSR_B64=$(cat exam-user.csr | base64 -w 0)

# Step 4: Create K8s CSR object
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: certificates.k8s.io/v1
kind: CertificateSigningRequest
metadata:
  name: exam-user
spec:
  request: ${CSR_B64}
  signerName: kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client
  usages:
  - client auth
EOF

# Step 5: Approve the CSR
kubectl certificate approve exam-user

# Step 6: Extract the signed certificate
kubectl get csr exam-user -o jsonpath='{.status.certificate}' \
  | base64 -d > exam-user.crt

# Step 7: Verify the certificate
openssl x509 -in exam-user.crt -text -noout | grep Subject
# Subject: O = exam-team, CN = exam-user
вҸұпёҸ Target: 3 minutes
Exercise 26.2 вҖ” Inspect API Server Certificate

Examine the API server's TLS certificate to identify key fields tested on the CKA exam.

  1. SSH to the control plane node
  2. Run: openssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt -text -noout
  3. Identify: Subject (CN), Issuer, expiry date, and Subject Alternative Names (SANs)
  4. Verify it was signed by the cluster CA: openssl verify -CAfile /etc/kubernetes/pki/ca.crt /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt
рҹ’Ў Hint: SANs are in the X509v3 Subject Alternative Name section of the certificate output.
рҹ“қ View Solution 
# SSH to control plane
ssh controlplane

# Inspect the API server certificate
openssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt -text -noout

# Key fields to identify:
# Subject: CN = kube-apiserver
# Issuer:  CN = kubernetes   (signed by cluster CA)
# Not After: (expiry date вҖ” typically 1 year)
# X509v3 Subject Alternative Name:
#   DNS:kubernetes, DNS:kubernetes.default,
#   DNS:kubernetes.default.svc,
#   DNS:kubernetes.default.svc.cluster.local,
#   IP Address:10.96.0.1, IP Address:192.168.x.x

# Verify CA signature
openssl verify -CAfile /etc/kubernetes/pki/ca.crt \
  /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt
# apiserver.crt: OK
вҸұпёҸ Target: 5 minutes
Exercise 26.3 вҖ” Check and Renew Certificates

Check certificate expiry dates and renew the API server certificate.

  1. Check all certificate expiry dates: kubeadm certs check-expiration
  2. Renew the API server certificate only: kubeadm certs renew apiserver
  3. Restart the API server (move/restore static pod manifest)
  4. Verify the new expiry date matches today + 1 year
рҹ’Ў Hint: Moving the manifest out of /etc/kubernetes/manifests/ stops the pod. Moving it back triggers a restart with the new cert.
рҹ“қ View Solution 
# Step 1: Check all certificate expiry dates
kubeadm certs check-expiration
# CERTIFICATE                EXPIRES                  RESIDUAL TIME
# admin.conf                 Jan 01, 2026 00:00 UTC   364d
# apiserver                  Jan 01, 2026 00:00 UTC   364d
# apiserver-kubelet-client   Jan 01, 2026 00:00 UTC   364d
# ...

# Step 2: Renew API server certificate only
kubeadm certs renew apiserver

# Step 3: Restart API server (static pod)
mv /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml /tmp/
# Wait for pod to stop (~10 seconds)
sleep 10
mv /tmp/kube-apiserver.yaml /etc/kubernetes/manifests/
# Wait for restart
sleep 30

# Step 4: Verify new expiry
openssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt -text -noout \
  | grep "Not After"
# Not After : (should be ~1 year from today)

# Verify API server is running
kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep apiserver

вҡ пёҸ CKA Gotcha: TLS & Certificate Traps

  • Certificate locations вҖ” All certs live under /etc/kubernetes/pki/. API server: apiserver.crt, apiserver-key.crt. CA: ca.crt, ca.key. etcd has its own directory: /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/
  • CSR approval вҖ” kubectl certificate approve <csr-name>. The exam may ask you to create a CSR for a new user and approve it. Don't forget: the CSR object needs the right signerName (kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client)
  • Certificate expiry вҖ” kubeadm certs check-expiration shows all cert expiry dates. Certs default to 1 year. CA cert is 10 years. If a cert expires, that component stops working
  • After renewing certs, restart the component вҖ” For static pods (apiserver, controller-manager, scheduler): move the manifest out of /etc/kubernetes/manifests/, wait, move it back. This is the tested pattern
  • openssl x509 -in cert.crt -text -noout вҖ” Memorize this for inspecting any cert. Look at Subject, Issuer, Not After, and SANs (Subject Alternative Names)

CKA Speed Tip вҖ” Certificate inspection one-liners:
openssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt -text -noout | grep -A2 "Validity" вҖ” check expiry
openssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt -text -noout | grep -A1 "Alternative" вҖ” check SANs
kubeadm certs check-expiration вҖ” all certs at once
kubeadm certs renew all вҖ” renew everything (then restart control plane)

вҡЎ TLS/PKI commands: kubeadm certs check-expiration вҖ” show all cert expiry dates
kubeadm certs renew apiserver вҖ” renew a specific cert
kubectl get csr вҖ” list pending certificate signing requests
kubectl certificate approve <csr-name> вҖ” approve a CSR
kubectl certificate deny <csr-name> вҖ” deny a CSR
рҹ“қ Chapter 26 Practice Drill

Timed challenge (target: 8 minutes):

  • 1. Check all certificate expiration dates: kubeadm certs check-expiration
  • 2. Inspect the API server cert's SANs: openssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt -text -noout | grep -A1 "Alternative"
  • 3. Generate a private key and CSR for user "devuser": openssl genrsa -out devuser.key 2048 then openssl req -new -key devuser.key -subj "/CN=devuser/O=dev" -out devuser.csr
  • 4. Create and approve a Kubernetes CSR object for devuser
  • 5. Extract the signed certificate and create a kubeconfig for devuser

Chapter 27: Pod Security and Admission Control

Kubernetes provides multiple layers of pod-level security. Pod Security Standards (PSS) define three security profiles (Privileged, Baseline, Restricted). Pod Security Admission (PSA) enforces these standards at the namespace level using labels. Security Contexts let you set user IDs, capabilities, and read-only filesystems per pod/container. Admission Controllers intercept API requests to validate and mutate objects before they hit etcd. Mastering these is essential for CKA and real-world cluster hardening.

рҹҹЎ Intermediate вҸұпёҸ ~1 hour рҹӣЎпёҸ Pod Security рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

рҹҺҜ What You'll Learn

  • вң… Pod Security Standards вҖ” Privileged, Baseline, and Restricted profiles
  • вң… Pod Security Admission вҖ” enforce/audit/warn modes via namespace labels
  • вң… Security Contexts вҖ” runAsNonRoot, capabilities, readOnlyRootFilesystem
  • вң… Admission Controllers вҖ” built-in and webhook-based controllers
рҹҺ“ CKA Relevance
Cluster Architecture, Installation & Configuration 25% Workloads & Scheduling 15%
Pod Security on the CKA Exam

Expect questions that require you to:

  • Apply PSA labels to namespaces to enforce security standards
  • Configure SecurityContext fields (runAsNonRoot, runAsUser, capabilities)
  • Know which admission controllers are enabled by default
  • Troubleshoot pods rejected by admission policies

Pod Security Commands

ActionCommand
Label namespace (enforce restricted)kubectl label ns prod pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted
Label namespace (warn baseline)kubectl label ns dev pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn=baseline
Label namespace (audit baseline)kubectl label ns staging pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit=baseline
Check namespace labelskubectl get ns prod --show-labels
Remove PSA labelkubectl label ns prod pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-
Dry-run pod against PSAkubectl apply --dry-run=server -f pod.yaml
List enabled admission controllerskube-apiserver -h | grep enable-admission-plugins

SecurityContext вҖ” Exam Skeleton

Know both pod-level and container-level SecurityContext fields.

apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: secure-pod
spec:
  securityContext:                    # в—Җв”Җв”Җ POD-level (applies to all containers)
    runAsUser: 1000                   # в—Җв”Җв”Җ UID for all containers
    runAsGroup: 3000                  # в—Җв”Җв”Җ GID for all containers
    fsGroup: 2000                     # в—Җв”Җв”Җ supplemental group for volume mounts
    runAsNonRoot: true                # в—Җв”Җв”Җ REJECT if image runs as root
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: nginx
    securityContext:                  # в—Җв”Җв”Җ CONTAINER-level (overrides pod-level)
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false # в—Җв”Җв”Җ block setuid binaries
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true    # в—Җв”Җв”Җ immutable container filesystem
      capabilities:
        drop: ["ALL"]                 # в—Җв”Җв”Җ drop ALL Linux capabilities
        add: ["NET_BIND_SERVICE"]     # в—Җв”Җв”Җ add back only what's needed

Pod Security Exam Traps

  • вқҢ Container SecurityContext inherits all pod-level settings
  • вң… Container SecurityContext overrides pod-level for overlapping fields
  • PSA labels use the format pod-security.kubernetes.io/MODE=LEVEL вҖ” three modes (enforce/warn/audit) Г— three levels (privileged/baseline/restricted)
  • PSA enforce mode rejects pods. warn shows a warning but allows. audit logs only
  • runAsNonRoot: true checks the image USER directive вҖ” if the image has no USER set, the pod is rejected even if the process would run as non-root
  • readOnlyRootFilesystem blocks writes to the container filesystem вҖ” use emptyDir volumes for writable directories
  • capabilities.drop: ["ALL"] then add specific ones = principle of least privilege
  • The restricted PSS requires: runAsNonRoot, no privilege escalation, drop ALL capabilities, seccomp profile вҖ” missing ANY of these causes rejection

Find Security Fields Fast

kubectl explain pod.spec.securityContext             # pod-level security
kubectl explain pod.spec.containers.securityContext  # container-level security
kubectl explain pod.spec.securityContext.runAsUser    # UID field
kubectl explain pod.spec.containers.securityContext.capabilities  # Linux caps
3 Security Levels (PSS)
3 Enforcement Modes
9 PSA Label Combinations
30+ Built-in Admission Controllers

27.1 Pod Security Standards (PSS)

Pod Security Standards define three progressively restrictive security profiles that cover the most common pod security needs.

Three Pod Security Standards вҖ” Increasing Restriction
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ          POD SECURITY STANDARDS (PSS) вҖ” THREE LEVELS                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   рҹ”“ PRIVILEGED в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   рҹ”’ BASELINE   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   рҹ”җ RESTRICTED в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў No restrict.  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Blocks known  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Strictest     в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў Full access   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   escalations   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Hardened pods в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў Host PID/Net  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў No hostPID    в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Non-root only в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў Privileged    в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў No hostNet    в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Drop ALL caps в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   containers OK в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў No privileged в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў No privilege  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   containers    в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   escalation    в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Use: system     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў No hostPath   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Read-only FS  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ components,     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҖў Seccomp       в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ kube-system     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ Use: general    в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ workloads       в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ Use: sensitive  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ workloads       в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в—Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ OPEN в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯв”Җв”Җ в”ңв”Җв”Җ MODERATE в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯв”Җв”Җ в”ңв”Җв”Җ STRICT в”Җв”Җв–¶   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ’Ў Choose based on workload needs:                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     kube-system вҶ’ Privileged | apps вҶ’ Baseline | PII/finance вҶ’ Restric в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
Check Privileged Baseline Restricted
Privileged containersвң… AllowedвқҢ BlockedвқҢ Blocked
Host namespaces (PID/IPC/Net)вң… AllowedвқҢ BlockedвқҢ Blocked
hostPath volumesвң… AllowedвқҢ BlockedвқҢ Blocked
Host portsвң… AllowedRestrictedвқҢ Blocked
runAsNonRootNot requiredNot requiredвң… Required
Privilege escalationвң… Allowedвң… AllowedвқҢ Must be false
CapabilitiesAnyRestricted setDrop ALL, add specific only
Seccomp profileNot requiredNot requiredвң… Required (RuntimeDefault/Localhost)
рҹ“қ PSS vs PodSecurityPolicy

PodSecurityPolicy (PSP) was deprecated in K8s 1.21 and removed in 1.25. Pod Security Standards + Pod Security Admission (PSA) replaced it. The CKA tests PSS/PSA, NOT PSP.

27.2 Pod Security Admission (PSA)

Pod Security Admission is the built-in admission controller that enforces Pod Security Standards. It's configured entirely through namespace labels вҖ” no YAML files needed.

PSA Enforcement via Namespace Labels
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ           POD SECURITY ADMISSION вҖ” HOW IT WORKS                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   kubectl apply -f pod.yaml                                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в–ј                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  API Server   в”Ӯ                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ intercepts                                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в–ј                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  PSA Admission Controller                 в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                                           в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  Checks namespace labels:                 в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=X      в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn=X         в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit=X        в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      ENFORCE       WARN      AUDIT                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     вқҢ REJECT   вҡ пёҸ Warning   рҹ“қ Log                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     the pod     but ALLOW    but ALLOW                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   рҹ’Ў You can combine modes: enforce=baseline + warn=restricted          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      вҶ’ Blocks baseline violations, warns about restricted violations    в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
psa-namespace-labels.sh 
# в”Җв”Җ Apply PSA labels to namespaces в”Җв”Җ

# Production: enforce restricted (reject non-compliant pods)
kubectl label ns production \
  pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted \
  pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-version=latest

# Staging: enforce baseline, warn about restricted violations
kubectl label ns staging \
  pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=baseline \
  pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn=restricted

# Development: warn only (don't block anything)
kubectl label ns development \
  pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn=baseline \
  pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit=baseline

# Verify labels
kubectl get ns production --show-labels
# NAME         STATUS   AGE   LABELS
# production   Active   5d    pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted,...

# в”Җв”Җ Test: What happens when you deploy a non-compliant pod? в”Җв”Җ

# This pod runs as root вҖ” violates "restricted"
kubectl run test --image=nginx -n production
# Error: pods "test" is forbidden: violates PodSecurity "restricted:latest"

# Same pod in dev namespace вҖ” just a warning
kubectl run test --image=nginx -n development
# Warning: would violate PodSecurity "baseline:latest"
# pod/test created  вҶҗ allowed!
вқҢ
enforce

Rejects pods that violate the standard. Use in production to prevent insecure workloads from deploying.

вҡ пёҸ
warn

Warns the user but allows the pod. Perfect for migration вҖ” see what would break before enforcing.

рҹ“қ
audit

Logs the violation to the audit log silently. Pods still run. Use for monitoring compliance.

27.3 Security Contexts

SecurityContext lets you set fine-grained security settings at the pod and container level. These are the fields that PSA actually checks against the Pod Security Standards.

Field Level What It Does
runAsUserPod / ContainerSet the UID the process runs as
runAsGroupPod / ContainerSet the primary GID
runAsNonRootPod / ContainerReject if image would run as root (UID 0)
fsGroupPod onlySupplemental group for mounted volumes
readOnlyRootFilesystemContainer onlyMake container filesystem read-only
allowPrivilegeEscalationContainer onlyBlock setuid/setgid binaries
capabilities.dropContainer onlyRemove Linux capabilities
capabilities.addContainer onlyAdd back specific capabilities
privilegedContainer onlyFull host access (avoid!)
seccompProfilePod / ContainerSystem call filtering profile
restricted-compliant-pod.yaml 
# This pod passes the "restricted" PSS вҖ” use this as template
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: secure-app
  namespace: production     # namespace with enforce=restricted
spec:
  securityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true      # в—Җв”Җв”Җ Required by restricted
    runAsUser: 1000
    runAsGroup: 3000
    fsGroup: 2000
    seccompProfile:         # в—Җв”Җв”Җ Required by restricted
      type: RuntimeDefault
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: gcr.io/distroless/static:nonroot
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false  # в—Җв”Җв”Җ Required by restricted
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true     # в—Җв”Җв”Җ Best practice
      capabilities:
        drop: ["ALL"]                  # в—Җв”Җв”Җ Required by restricted
    resources:
      limits:
        memory: "128Mi"
        cpu: "250m"
    volumeMounts:
    - name: tmp
      mountPath: /tmp       # в—Җв”Җв”Җ writable dir via emptyDir
  volumes:
  - name: tmp
    emptyDir: {}            # use emptyDir for writable paths
Pod-Level vs Container-Level SecurityContext
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ   POD-LEVEL securityContext          applies to ALL containers  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  runAsUser: 1000                                       в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  runAsGroup: 3000                                      в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  fsGroup: 2000                                         в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  runAsNonRoot: true                                    в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  seccompProfile: RuntimeDefault                        в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ                       в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ               в–ј                       в–ј                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ CONTAINER-LEVEL   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ CONTAINER-LEVEL   в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ (overrides pod)   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ (inherits pod)    в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ runAsUser: 2000   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ (uses 1000 from   в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ вҶҗ overrides 1000  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  pod-level)       в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ capabilities:     в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ capabilities:     в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   drop: ["ALL"]   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   drop: ["ALL"]   в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ allowPrivilegeEsc в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ readOnlyRootFS:   в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ : false           в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ true              в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     container: app          container: sidecar                   в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
рҹ’Ў CKA Pattern: Restricted-Compliant Pod

To make ANY pod pass the "restricted" standard, add these 4 things: runAsNonRoot: true, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, capabilities.drop: ["ALL"], and seccompProfile.type: RuntimeDefault. Missing any one вһ” rejected.

27.4 Admission Controllers

Admission Controllers are plugins that intercept API requests after authentication and authorization but before the object is persisted to etcd. They can validate, mutate, or reject requests.

Request Flow Through Admission Controllers
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                API REQUEST LIFECYCLE                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   kubectl apply в”Җв”Җв–¶ API Server                                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                        в”Ӯ                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ  AuthN  в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җ Who are you? (certs, tokens)                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ  AuthZ  в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җ Are you allowed? (RBAC)                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ  ADMISSION CONTROLLERS                в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ                                       в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  MUTATING    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  VALIDATING  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў Defaults   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў Policy     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў Inject     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў Rules      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   sidecars   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў Quotas     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў Labels     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў PSA        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў Webhooks   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ вҖў Webhooks   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ modify           в”Ӯ check    в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                             в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                             в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                      в”Ӯ if ALL pass                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                 в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                 в”Ӯ  etcd   в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җ Object persisted              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                 в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
Admission Controller Type Purpose
NamespaceLifecycleValidatingPrevents creating resources in terminating/non-existent namespaces
LimitRangerMutatingApplies default resource limits from LimitRange objects
ResourceQuotaValidatingEnforces resource quotas per namespace
ServiceAccountMutatingAuto-mounts SA tokens onto pods
PodSecurityValidatingEnforces Pod Security Standards (PSA)
DefaultStorageClassMutatingAssigns default StorageClass to PVCs without one
MutatingAdmissionWebhookMutatingCalls external webhooks to modify objects
ValidatingAdmissionWebhookValidatingCalls external webhooks to validate objects
admission-controllers.sh 
# в”Җв”Җ Check which admission controllers are enabled в”Җв”Җ
# Look at API server manifest on control plane node
cat /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml \
  | grep enable-admission-plugins
# --enable-admission-plugins=NodeRestriction,...

# в”Җв”Җ Enable/disable admission controllers в”Җв”Җ
# Edit the API server static pod manifest:
vi /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml
# Add to the command args:
# --enable-admission-plugins=NodeRestriction,PodSecurity,ResourceQuota
# --disable-admission-plugins=DefaultStorageClass

# в”Җв”Җ After editing, API server auto-restarts (static pod) в”Җв”Җ

# в”Җв”Җ List default enabled controllers в”Җв”Җ
kube-apiserver -h | grep enable-admission-plugins
# Default: CertificateApproval,CertificateSigning,
# CertificateSubjectRestriction,DefaultIngressClass,
# DefaultStorageClass,DefaultTolerationSeconds,LimitRanger,
# MutatingAdmissionWebhook,NamespaceLifecycle,PodSecurity,
# ResourceQuota,ServiceAccount,StorageObjectInUseProtection,
# ValidatingAdmissionPolicy,ValidatingAdmissionWebhook
вҡ пёҸ CKA: Editing API Server Manifest

On the exam, you may need to enable an admission controller by editing /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml. After saving, the kubelet auto-restarts the API server pod. Wait ~30 seconds and verify with kubectl get pods -n kube-system. If the API server doesn't come back, check your YAML syntax.

вң… DO
  • Use PSA labels for namespace-level enforcement
  • Start with warn mode before switching to enforce
  • Set runAsNonRoot: true for all application pods
  • Drop ALL capabilities, add only what's needed
  • Use readOnlyRootFilesystem + emptyDir for writable paths
вқҢ DON'T
  • Run containers as root without a compelling reason
  • Use privileged: true for application workloads
  • Skip seccomp profiles in restricted namespaces
  • Forget to restart API server after editing admission plugins
  • Confuse PodSecurityPolicy (removed) with Pod Security Standards
Security Context Quick Reference вҖ” CKA Cheat Sheet
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ        SECURITY CONTEXT CHEAT SHEET вҖ” WHAT TO SET WHERE                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  POD-LEVEL                       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  CONTAINER-LEVEL                 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  spec.securityContext            в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  spec.containers[].securityCtx   в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вң… runAsUser: 1000              в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вң… allowPrivilegeEscalation     в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вң… runAsGroup: 3000             в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вң… readOnlyRootFilesystem       в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вң… runAsNonRoot: true           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вң… capabilities.drop/add       в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вң… fsGroup: 2000                в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вң… privileged (avoid!)         в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вң… seccompProfile               в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вң… runAsUser (overrides pod)   в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вң… supplementalGroups           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вң… runAsNonRoot (overrides)    в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вң… seccompProfile (overrides)  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вқҢ NOT available:               в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ     capabilities                 в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  вқҢ NOT available:               в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ     readOnlyRootFilesystem       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ     fsGroup                      в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ     allowPrivilegeEscalation     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ     supplementalGroups           в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ’Ў Rule: Container-level OVERRIDES pod-level for shared fields             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     Pod sets runAsUser: 1000, Container sets runAsUser: 2000                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     вҶ’ Container runs as UID 2000                                            в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Pod Security Verification Checklist

  • kubectl get ns NAME --show-labels вҶ’ verify PSA labels are applied
  • kubectl label ns NAME pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted --dry-run=server вҶ’ preview label effect
  • kubectl apply --dry-run=server -f pod.yaml вҶ’ test pod against PSA before deploying
  • kubectl exec POD -- id вҶ’ confirm runAsUser/runAsGroup
  • kubectl exec POD -- touch /test вҶ’ verify readOnlyRootFilesystem (should fail)
  • kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep apiserver вҶ’ verify apiserver restarted after admission controller changes
  • cat /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml | grep enable-admission вҶ’ check enabled admission controllers

рҹҸӢпёҸ Chapter 27 Practice Exercises

These exercises cover the most common CKA pod security patterns.

вҸұпёҸ Target: 4 minutes
Exercise 27.1 вҖ” Apply PSA to a Namespace

Configure Pod Security Admission to enforce baseline and warn for restricted on a namespace.

  1. Create namespace secure-ns
  2. Label it to enforce the baseline standard and warn for restricted
  3. Try deploying a pod with hostNetwork: true вҶ’ should be rejected
  4. Deploy a normal nginx pod вҶ’ should succeed with a warning about restricted
рҹ’Ў Hint: PSA labels follow the format pod-security.kubernetes.io/MODE=LEVEL. You can set multiple modes on one namespace.
рҹ“қ View Solution 
# Step 1: Create namespace
kubectl create namespace secure-ns

# Step 2: Apply PSA labels
kubectl label ns secure-ns \
  pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=baseline \
  pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn=restricted

# Step 3: Try deploying a pod that violates baseline
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: bad-pod
  namespace: secure-ns
spec:
  hostNetwork: true    # violates baseline!
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: nginx
EOF
# Error: pods "bad-pod" is forbidden: violates PodSecurity "baseline:latest"

# Step 4: Deploy a normal pod (passes baseline, warns restricted)
kubectl run nginx --image=nginx -n secure-ns
# Warning: would violate PodSecurity "restricted:latest": ...
# pod/nginx created  вҶҗ allowed!
вҸұпёҸ Target: 6 minutes
Exercise 27.2 вҖ” Create Restricted-Compliant Pod

Create a pod that passes the strictest PSS level вҖ” restricted.

  1. Label namespace secure-ns to enforce restricted
  2. Create a pod with all required fields: runAsNonRoot, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, capabilities.drop: ["ALL"], seccompProfile.type: RuntimeDefault
  3. Use a non-root image (e.g., gcr.io/distroless/static:nonroot)
  4. Verify the pod runs successfully in the restricted namespace
рҹ’Ў Hint: The restricted standard requires ALL 4 of these: runAsNonRoot, no privilege escalation, drop ALL capabilities, and a seccomp profile. Missing any one causes rejection.
рҹ“қ View Solution 
# Step 1: Upgrade PSA to enforce restricted
kubectl label ns secure-ns \
  pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted \
  --overwrite

# Step 2: Create restricted-compliant pod
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: secure-pod
  namespace: secure-ns
spec:
  securityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    runAsUser: 1000
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: gcr.io/distroless/static:nonroot
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
      capabilities:
        drop: ["ALL"]
    resources:
      limits:
        memory: "64Mi"
        cpu: "100m"
EOF

# Step 3: Verify pod is running
kubectl get pod secure-pod -n secure-ns
# NAME         READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
# secure-pod   1/1     Running   0          5s
вҸұпёҸ Target: 5 minutes
Exercise 27.3 вҖ” SecurityContext Fields

Practice setting security context fields and verifying their effects inside the container.

  1. Create a pod with runAsUser: 1000 and readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
  2. Exec into the pod: kubectl exec -it pod-name -- id вҶ’ should show uid=1000
  3. Try writing a file: touch /test вҶ’ should fail (read-only filesystem)
  4. Mount an emptyDir at /tmp and verify writing works there
рҹ’Ў Hint: readOnlyRootFilesystem is a container-level field. Use emptyDir volumes for any writable paths (logs, temp files, caches).
рҹ“қ View Solution 
# Create pod with security context + writable emptyDir
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: sec-ctx-pod
spec:
  securityContext:
    runAsUser: 1000
    runAsGroup: 3000
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: busybox
    command: ["sleep", "3600"]
    securityContext:
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
    volumeMounts:
    - name: tmp
      mountPath: /tmp
  volumes:
  - name: tmp
    emptyDir: {}
EOF

# Wait for pod
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod/sec-ctx-pod

# Verify UID
kubectl exec sec-ctx-pod -- id
# uid=1000 gid=3000

# Try writing to root filesystem (fails)
kubectl exec sec-ctx-pod -- touch /test
# touch: /test: Read-only file system

# Write to emptyDir (succeeds)
kubectl exec sec-ctx-pod -- touch /tmp/test
kubectl exec sec-ctx-pod -- ls /tmp/test
# /tmp/test  вҶҗ success!
вҸұпёҸ Target: 5 minutes
Exercise 27.4 вҖ” Enable Admission Controller

Edit the API server manifest to enable an additional admission controller plugin.

  1. SSH to control plane and check current admission plugins: cat /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml | grep enable-admission
  2. Add NodeRestriction to the list (if not present)
  3. Save the file вҖ” kubelet auto-restarts the API server
  4. Wait 30 seconds and verify: kubectl get pods -n kube-system
рҹ’Ў Hint: Static pod manifests auto-restart when saved. If API server doesn't come back, you made a YAML syntax error вҖ” check with crictl ps and crictl logs.
рҹ“қ View Solution 
# Step 1: SSH to control plane
ssh controlplane

# Step 2: Check current admission plugins
cat /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml \
  | grep enable-admission-plugins
# --enable-admission-plugins=NodeRestriction

# Step 3: Edit the manifest
vi /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml
# Find the --enable-admission-plugins line and add to the list:
# --enable-admission-plugins=NodeRestriction,PodSecurity,ResourceQuota
# IMPORTANT: comma-separated, no spaces!

# Step 4: Wait for API server to restart (auto by kubelet)
sleep 30

# Step 5: Verify API server is running
kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep apiserver
# kube-apiserver-controlplane   1/1   Running   0   25s

# Step 6: Verify the admission plugin is active
kubectl get pod kube-apiserver-controlplane \
  -n kube-system -o yaml | grep enable-admission
# --enable-admission-plugins=NodeRestriction,PodSecurity,ResourceQuota
вҸұпёҸ CKA Speed Tip: PSA Labels

The fastest way to apply PSA is a single kubectl label command. No YAML needed!
kubectl label ns my-ns pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-version=latest

вҡ пёҸ CKA Gotcha: Pod Security & Admission Control Traps

  • 3 PSA modes вҖ” enforce (reject violations), warn (allow but show warning), audit (log only). The exam usually asks for enforce. Apply via namespace labels, not cluster config
  • 3 PSS levels вҖ” privileged (no restrictions), baseline (blocks known escalations), restricted (hardened). Restricted requires runAsNonRoot: true, drop ALL capabilities, and seccompProfile
  • SecurityContext at pod vs container level вҖ” spec.securityContext sets defaults for ALL containers. spec.containers[].securityContext overrides per container. Container-level wins
  • runAsNonRoot: true fails if image uses root вҖ” The container must have a non-root USER in Dockerfile, or set runAsUser: 1000 explicitly. Otherwise the pod is rejected at startup
  • Admission controllers are API server flags вҖ” --enable-admission-plugins=NodeRestriction,PodSecurity. To add one, edit /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml. The API server auto-restarts

CKA Speed Tip вҖ” SecurityContext skeleton (memorize):
securityContext:
  runAsUser: 1000
  runAsNonRoot: true
  readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
  allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
  capabilities: { drop: ["ALL"] }
This covers 90% of SecurityContext questions on the exam.

вҡЎ Pod Security commands: kubectl label ns <ns> pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted вҖ” apply PSA
kubectl label ns <ns> pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn=baseline вҖ” warning mode
kubectl get ns <ns> --show-labels вҖ” verify PSA labels
kubectl get pod <name> -o jsonpath='{.spec.securityContext}' вҖ” pod security context
kubectl get pod <name> -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].securityContext}' вҖ” container security context
рҹ“қ Chapter 27 Practice Drill

Timed challenge (target: 7 minutes):

  • 1. Create namespace hardened with PSA enforce=restricted
  • 2. Try to deploy a pod with default settings вҖ” it should be REJECTED
  • 3. Fix the pod: add runAsNonRoot: true, runAsUser: 1000, seccompProfile: {type: RuntimeDefault}, drop ALL capabilities
  • 4. Deploy the fixed pod вҖ” should succeed
  • 5. Verify the API server has PodSecurity admission plugin enabled

рҹ”ҙ Part 8: Cluster Maintenance Chapters 28-30

Chapter 28: Cluster Upgrades with kubeadm

Kubernetes releases a new minor version roughly every 4 months. As a cluster administrator, you must upgrade clusters regularly to stay within the supported version window, receive security patches, and access new features. For your anihpj/jobpost production cluster, a botched upgrade means downtime for your job board. The CKA exam consistently tests the upgrade procedure вҖ” expect to perform a full control plane and worker node upgrade hands-on. This chapter gives you a battle-tested, step-by-step workflow.

рҹ”ҙ Advanced вҸұпёҸ ~2 hours в¬ҶпёҸ Upgrades рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

рҹҺҜ What You'll Learn

  • вң… Kubernetes release cycle вҖ” minor vs patch versions, support window
  • вң… Component version skew policy вҖ” what can differ, what can't
  • вң… Upgrade strategy вҖ” control plane first, then workers
  • вң… Hands-on control plane upgrade вҖ” kubeadm, kubelet, kubectl
  • вң… Hands-on worker node upgrade вҖ” drain, upgrade, uncordon
рҹҺ“ CKA Relevance
Cluster Architecture, Installation & Configuration 25%
Cluster Upgrades on the CKA Exam

Expect a question like: "Upgrade the control plane node from v1.30.0 to v1.31.0 using kubeadm." You must:

  • Know the exact sequence: upgrade kubeadm вҶ’ plan вҶ’ apply вҶ’ drain вҶ’ upgrade kubelet/kubectl вҶ’ uncordon
  • Use apt-mark unhold/hold to manage package versions
  • Remember systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl restart kubelet after upgrading kubelet
  • Never skip minor versions вҖ” 1.30 вҶ’ 1.31 is OK, 1.30 вҶ’ 1.32 is NOT

Cluster Upgrade Commands

ActionCommand
Check current versionkubectl get nodes
Unhold kubeadmapt-mark unhold kubeadm
Install specific kubeadmapt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-1.1
Hold kubeadmapt-mark hold kubeadm
Check upgrade plankubeadm upgrade plan
Apply upgrade (control plane)kubeadm upgrade apply v1.31.0
Upgrade worker node configkubeadm upgrade node
Drain nodekubectl drain NODE --ignore-daemonsets
Upgrade kubelet + kubectlapt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-1.1 kubectl=1.31.0-1.1
Restart kubeletsystemctl daemon-reload && systemctl restart kubelet
Uncordon nodekubectl uncordon NODE

Upgrade Exam Traps

  • вқҢ Skip from 1.30 to 1.32 directly
  • вң… Upgrade one minor version at a time: 1.30 вҶ’ 1.31 вҶ’ 1.32
  • вқҢ Upgrade workers before control plane
  • вң… Always upgrade control plane FIRST, then workers
  • вқҢ Forget systemctl daemon-reload after upgrading kubelet
  • вң… Always run systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl restart kubelet
  • On workers use kubeadm upgrade node, NOT kubeadm upgrade apply
  • --ignore-daemonsets is REQUIRED on drain вҖ” DaemonSet pods can't be evicted
  • Check the package version format вҖ” some providers use =1.31.0-1.1 not =1.31.0-00

Find Upgrade Info Fast

kubectl get nodes                          # current versions
kubeadm version                             # kubeadm version
kubelet --version                           # kubelet version
kubeadm upgrade plan                        # available upgrades
apt-cache madison kubeadm                   # available package versions
~4 mo Minor Release Cycle
3 Supported Minor Versions
Вұ1 Version Skew (kubelet)
7 Steps per Upgrade

28.1 Kubernetes Release Cycle

Understanding the release cycle helps you plan when and how to upgrade your cluster. Kubernetes follows a predictable release pattern.

Kubernetes Version Numbering
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                    KUBERNETES VERSION FORMAT                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                         v1.31.2                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җ PATCH version (bug fixes, security)       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ MINOR version (new features)               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                          в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ MAJOR version (always 1)                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  RELEASE TIMELINE вҖ” Kubernetes Support Window                   в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Time в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶           в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  v1.29  в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘              в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ         в—Җв”Җв”Җ supported в”Җв–¶ в—Җв”Җв”Җ end of life в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶               в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  v1.30  в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘              в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ              в—Җв”Җв”Җ supported в”Җв–¶                                   в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  v1.31  в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘              в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                  в—Җв”Җв”Җ supported в”Җв–¶  в—Җв”Җв”Җ current                  в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  v1.32  в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ                           в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                      в—Җв”Җв”Җ latest в”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶                            в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ң Only the latest 3 minor versions receive patches           в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ң Each minor version supported for ~14 months                в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ         в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
Aspect Minor Release Patch Release
FrequencyEvery ~4 monthsMonthly (or as needed)
What changesNew features, API changesBug fixes, security patches
Example1.30 вҶ’ 1.311.31.0 вҶ’ 1.31.1
Can you skip?вқҢ No вҖ” upgrade one at a timeвң… Yes вҖ” jump from .0 to .3
Downtime riskHigher вҖ” requires drain/restartLower вҖ” kubelet restart only
Planning neededRead release notes, test firstApply when available
Component Version Skew Policy
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ              COMPONENT VERSION SKEW RULES                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   The API server is the "anchor" вҖ” everything else is relative to it        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  kube-apiserver  в”Ӯ  в—Җв”Җв”Җ THIS sets the version baseline                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ    v1.31         в”Ӯ                                                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ  ALLOWED VERSION RANGES                      в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ                                              в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  kubelet:         v1.30 or v1.31  (N-1 to N)         в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  kube-proxy:      v1.30 or v1.31  (N-1 to N)         в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  controller-mgr:  v1.31 only      (must match)       в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  scheduler:       v1.31 only      (must match)       в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  kubectl:         v1.30 to v1.32  (N-1 to N+1)      в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                                                       в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  вҡ пёҸ kubelet CANNOT be newer than API server!          в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  вҡ пёҸ This is why you upgrade control plane FIRST!      в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   EXAMPLE: API server at v1.31                                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”ӮComponent в”Ӯ  OK Versions  в”Ӯ  NOT OK      в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯkubelet   в”Ӯ 1.30, 1.31    в”Ӯ 1.29, 1.32   в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯkubectl   в”Ӯ 1.30,1.31,1.32в”Ӯ 1.29         в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯscheduler в”Ӯ 1.31          в”Ӯ 1.30, 1.32   в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                               в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
вҡ пёҸ Why Control Plane First?

kubelet can be one minor version behind the API server, but never ahead. If you upgrade workers first, the kubelet would be v1.31 while the API server is still v1.30 вҖ” this violates the skew policy and causes unpredictable behavior. Always: control plane вҶ’ workers.

28.2 Upgrade Strategy

A cluster upgrade follows a strict sequence. Think of it as a conveyor belt вҖ” each step must complete before the next begins.

Complete Cluster Upgrade Workflow вҖ” 10 Steps
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ         CLUSTER UPGRADE вҖ” COMPLETE WORKFLOW                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  PHASE 1: CONTROL PLANE UPGRADE                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ STEP 1: Upgrade kubeadm             в”Ӯ                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ apt-mark unhold kubeadm             в”Ӯ                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ apt-get install kubeadm=1.31.0-1.1  в”Ӯ                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ apt-mark hold kubeadm               в”Ӯ                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                 в–ј                                                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ STEP 2: Check upgrade plan          в”Ӯ                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ kubeadm upgrade plan                в”Ӯ                                    в”Ӯ
