в”Ӯ  в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Deploy:   $ kubectl apply -k overlays/production/                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Preview:  $ kubectl kustomize overlays/production/                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Result: base/deployment.yaml MERGED with production patches                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  вҶ’ replicas: 5, cpu: 500m, memory: 512Mi                                    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Kustomize Files for anihpj

base/kustomization.yaml & overlays/production/kustomization.yaml 
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
# base/kustomization.yaml
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization
resources:
- deployment.yaml
- service.yaml
- configmap.yaml
commonLabels:
  app: anihpj

---
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
# overlays/production/kustomization.yaml
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization
resources:
- ../../base
namePrefix: prod-              # All resources get prod- prefix
namespace: anihpj-production
patches:
- path: patch-replicas.yaml
  target:
    kind: Deployment
    name: anihpj-web
images:
- name: anihpj/web
  newTag: v2.1-stable          # Override image tag for prod

---
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
# overlays/production/patch-replicas.yaml
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-web
spec:
  replicas: 5                  # Production: 5 replicas
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: anihpj
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "500m"
            memory: "512Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "1000m"
            memory: "1Gi"

Kustomize Power Features вҖ” Generators & Transformers

kustomization.yaml вҖ” Generators, labels, and config management 
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization
resources:
- ../../base

namespace: anihpj-production

# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
# CONFIGMAP GENERATOR вҖ” auto-creates ConfigMaps from files/literals
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
configMapGenerator:
- name: anihpj-config
  literals:
  - DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.production
  - ALLOWED_HOSTS=anihpj.com
  - DEBUG=false

# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
# SECRET GENERATOR вҖ” auto-creates Secrets
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
secretGenerator:
- name: anihpj-db-creds
  literals:
  - DB_PASSWORD=supersecret   # In real life: use sealed-secrets or external-secrets
  type: Opaque

# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
# COMMON LABELS вҖ” applied to ALL resources
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
commonLabels:
  app: anihpj
  env: production
  team: backend

# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
# COMMON ANNOTATIONS
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
commonAnnotations:
  managed-by: kustomize

# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
# IMAGE TRANSFORMER вҖ” override image tags
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
images:
- name: anihpj/web
  newName: ghcr.io/youruser/anihpj-web
  newTag: v2.1-stable

# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
# PATCHES вҖ” strategic merge or JSON patches
# в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ
patches:
- path: patch-replicas.yaml
  target:
    kind: Deployment
    name: anihpj-web
рҹҸӢпёҸ Practice Drill вҖ” Kustomize Multi-Environment Deploy

Scenario: Deploy anihpj to staging and production using Kustomize overlays with different configurations.

  1. Preview what Kustomize will generate for production:
    kubectl kustomize overlays/production/
  2. Apply the production overlay:
    kubectl apply -k overlays/production/
  3. Verify resources were created in the correct namespace:
    kubectl get all -n anihpj-production
  4. Check that labels were applied correctly:
    kubectl get deploy -n anihpj-production --show-labels
  5. Verify the image tag matches the overlay:
    kubectl get deploy anihpj-web -n anihpj-production -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}'
  6. Compare with staging config:
    diff <(kubectl kustomize overlays/staging/) <(kubectl kustomize overlays/production/)

Key point: Same base YAML, but -k flag applies environment-specific patches. This is DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) for Kubernetes!

рҹҺҜ CKA Exam вҖ” Kustomize

The CKA exam may reference Kustomize in the exam environment. Know these two commands cold: kubectl apply -k <directory> applies Kustomize configuration, and kubectl kustomize <directory> previews without applying. The -k flag is your friend!

37.6 Helm vs Kustomize вҖ” When to Use Each

This is one of the most common questions in Kubernetes. The short answer: they serve different purposes and complement each other.

Feature Helm Kustomize
Approach Templates + values Patches on base YAML
Learning Curve Moderate (Go templates) Low (plain YAML patches)
Package Sharing вң… Chart repos (Artifact Hub) вқҢ Not designed for sharing
Complexity Go template syntax Strategic merge patches
Built into kubectl No (separate binary) Yes (kubectl apply -k)
Versioning Chart versions + release history Git-based versioning
Rollback helm rollback git revert
Best For Shared / public apps (nginx, Redis) Internal config management
anihpj Use Case Package for distribution Multi-env deployments

Decision Tree вҖ” Helm, Kustomize, or Both?

в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  DECISION TREE                                                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Need to share charts publicly?                                              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ YES вҶ’ Use Helm (chart repos)                                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җ NO                                                                      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ                                                                       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      Need complex templating (conditionals, loops)?                         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      в”ңв”Җв”Җ YES вҶ’ Use Helm (Go templates are powerful)                         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      в””в”Җв”Җ NO                                                                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ                                                                   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ          Just need different configs per environment?                        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ          в”ңв”Җв”Җ YES вҶ’ Use Kustomize (simplest solution)                        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ          в””в”Җв”Җ BOTH                                                            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ                                                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ              Use Helm to render, Kustomize to patch!                        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ              $ helm template ./chart | kubectl apply -k -                   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  рҹ”‘ PRO TIP: Many teams use BOTH together:                                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Helm    в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ Rendered в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”ӮKustomize в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ Applied  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Chart   в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  YAML    в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  Patches в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ to K8s   в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  ArgoCD natively supports both Helm and Kustomize as sources!               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                              в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

вң… Chapter 37 Competency Checklist

  • Can you explain the stages of a CI/CD pipeline for Kubernetes?
  • Can you write a GitHub Actions workflow that builds, tests, and pushes a container image?
  • Can you articulate why GitOps (pull-based) is preferred over push-based CD?
  • Can you set up an ArgoCD Application pointing at a Git repo?
  • Can you troubleshoot ArgoCD sync/health status issues?
  • Can you create a Helm chart with Go templates and values files?
  • Can you read and write Helm Go template syntax (conditionals, loops, defaults)?
  • Can you use kubectl apply -k with a base + overlay Kustomize structure?
  • Can you use Kustomize generators (configMapGenerator, secretGenerator)?
  • Can you explain when to choose Helm vs Kustomize vs both?
рҹ’Ў Real-World Stack for anihpj вҖ” Putting It All Together

Here's how a production team would combine everything from this chapter:

  • GitHub Actions вҖ” CI: run Django tests, build Docker image, push to GHCR
  • Kustomize вҖ” Manage dev/staging/prod overlays for anihpj manifests
  • ArgoCD вҖ” Watch the manifest repo, auto-sync to cluster, detect drift
  • Helm вҖ” Install third-party tools (nginx-ingress, cert-manager, Prometheus)
  • HPA + Cluster Autoscaler вҖ” Scale pods and nodes based on traffic

This is the modern Kubernetes deployment stack used by most teams. You now understand every layer.

вҢЁпёҸ Appendix A: kubectl Admin Command Reference

APPENDIX рҹ“Ӣ REFERENCE

Complete kubectl admin command reference with CKA speed aliases вҖ” sections A.1 through A.7. Every command uses the anihpj project context. Bookmark this page for the exam.

Exam Day Setup вҖ” Run these aliases first when your exam terminal opens:

~/.bashrc вҖ” exam aliases 
alias k=kubectl
alias kgp='kubectl get pods'
alias kgs='kubectl get svc'
alias kgn='kubectl get nodes'
alias kd='kubectl describe'
alias kaf='kubectl apply -f'
alias kdel='kubectl delete'
export do='--dry-run=client -o yaml'
export now='--force --grace-period=0'

# Apply immediately:
source ~/.bashrc
вҡ пёҸ Context Switching вҖ” #1 Exam Killer

The CKA exam uses multiple clusters. Each question tells you which cluster to use. If you forget to switch, you'll modify the wrong cluster and lose points with no way to undo. Run the context command first for every question:

terminal вҖ” context switching (EVERY question!) 
# The exam tells you: "Use context: k8s-cluster-1"
kubectl config use-context k8s-cluster-1

# Verify you're on the right cluster
kubectl config current-context
# k8s-cluster-1

# List all available contexts
kubectl config get-contexts
# CURRENT   NAME              CLUSTER           AUTHINFO
# *         k8s-cluster-1     k8s-cluster-1     admin
#           k8s-cluster-2     k8s-cluster-2     admin

# Set default namespace (saves typing -n every time)
kubectl config set-context --current --namespace=anihpj

kubectl Command Flow вҖ” What Happens When You Run a Command

 в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
 в”Ӯ  YOU type:   в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  ~/.kube/    в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  API Server  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ    etcd      в”Ӯ
 в”Ӯ  kubectl ... в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  config      в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  :6443       в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  (store)     в”Ӯ
 в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  (auth+ctx)  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  (AuthN/Z)   в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
 в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                                               в”Ӯ
                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                    в–ј                          в–ј                      в–ј
             в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ       в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
             в”Ӯ  Scheduler  в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ  Controller  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   kubelet    в”Ӯ
             в”Ӯ  (assigns   в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ  Manager     в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ  (runs pods  в”Ӯ
             в”Ӯ   nodes)    в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ  (reconcile) в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   on nodes)  в”Ӯ
             в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ       в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

A.1 Cluster Management Commands

COMMANDS рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

These commands help you inspect the overall health and capabilities of your cluster. Use them first when troubleshooting any cluster issue.

Cluster Health Check Decision Tree

                        рҹҸҘ Cluster Health Check
                        в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
                                в”Ӯ
                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                    в–ј           в–ј           в–ј
              kubectl       kubectl     kubectl
             cluster-info  get nodes   get cs
                    в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ
                    в–ј           в–ј           в–ј
              вң… API server  вң… Nodes    вҡ пёҸ Deprecated
              reachable?     Ready?      in v1.19+
                    в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ
                    в–ј           в–ј           в–ј
               If вқҢ:       If NotReady:  Use instead:
              Check         SSH to node,  kubectl get
              kubeconfig    check kubelet componentstatuses
              + API pod     + containerd  (still works)

Cluster Inspection Commands

CommandWhat It ShowsWhen to Use
kubectl cluster-info API server URL + CoreDNS URL First check вҖ” is the API reachable?
kubectl get componentstatuses scheduler, controller-manager, etcd health Control plane component health check
kubectl top nodes CPU/memory usage per node Resource pressure diagnosis
kubectl top pods --all-namespaces CPU/memory usage per pod Find resource-hungry pods
kubectl get events --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp Recent cluster events (newest last) Troubleshooting вҖ” what just happened?
kubectl api-resources All resource types + short names + API group Discover resource types and their short names
kubectl api-versions All API group/versions available Check if an API version exists
terminal вҖ” cluster inspection 
# Check cluster is reachable
kubectl cluster-info
# Kubernetes control plane is running at https://10.0.0.10:6443
# CoreDNS is running at https://10.0.0.10:6443/api/v1/namespaces/kube-system/...

# Quick health check
kubectl get componentstatuses
# NAME                 STATUS    MESSAGE
# scheduler            Healthy   ok
# controller-manager   Healthy   ok
# etcd-0               Healthy   ok

# Resource usage (requires metrics-server)
kubectl top nodes
# NAME     CPU(cores)   CPU%   MEMORY(bytes)   MEMORY%
# cp-1     213m         10%    1024Mi          25%
# worker-1 156m         7%     768Mi           19%

# Find the most recent events
kubectl get events -A --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp | tail -20

# Discover all resource types with short names
kubectl api-resources --sort-by=name | head -20
# NAME          SHORTNAMES   APIVERSION   NAMESPACED   KIND
# configmaps    cm           v1           true         ConfigMap
# deployments   deploy       apps/v1      true         Deployment
# pods          po           v1           true         Pod
# services      svc          v1           true         Service
рҹ’Ў Short Names Save Time

Use short names on the exam: po (pods), svc (services), deploy (deployments), cm (configmaps), ns (namespaces), no (nodes), pv (persistentvolumes), pvc (persistentvolumeclaims), sa (serviceaccounts), netpol (networkpolicies), ing (ingresses).

A.2 Node Management

COMMANDS рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

Node management is a core CKA skill вҖ” you'll cordon, drain, and uncordon nodes during upgrades and maintenance.

Node Maintenance Lifecycle

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                    NODE MAINTENANCE WORKFLOW                            в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
  в”Ӯ                                                                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  NORMAL  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ CORDON   в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ    DRAIN     в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ UNCORDON в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ          в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ          в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ              в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ Schedul- в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ No new   в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ Evict all    в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ Allow    в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ able вң…  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ pods вӣ”  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ pods рҹҡҖ      в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ pods вң…  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                          в”Ӯ                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                   в”Ӯ  PERFORM    в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                   в”Ӯ  MAINTENANCEв”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                   в”Ӯ  (upgrade,  в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                   в”Ӯ   reboot)   в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                      в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Node Management Commands

CommandEffectCKA Context
kubectl get nodes -o wide List nodes with IP, OS, kernel, container runtime First check during troubleshooting
kubectl describe node <name> Full node details: conditions, capacity, pods, taints Diagnose NotReady, resource pressure
kubectl cordon <node> Mark unschedulable (existing pods stay) Pre-maintenance вҖ” stop new pods
kubectl drain <node> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data Cordon + evict all pods (except DaemonSets) Safely evacuate before maintenance
kubectl uncordon <node> Mark schedulable again After maintenance вҖ” resume scheduling
kubectl taint nodes <node> key=value:NoSchedule Add taint вҖ” only pods with matching toleration can schedule Isolate nodes for specific workloads
kubectl label nodes <node> disk=ssd Add label вҖ” used with nodeSelector for scheduling Target pods to specific nodes
terminal вҖ” node maintenance for anihpj 
# Step 1: Check current node status
kubectl get nodes -o wide
# NAME       STATUS   ROLES           VERSION   INTERNAL-IP   OS-IMAGE
# cp-1       Ready    control-plane   v1.31.0   10.0.0.10     Ubuntu 22.04
# worker-1   Ready    <none>          v1.31.0   10.0.0.11     Ubuntu 22.04
# worker-2   Ready    <none>          v1.31.0   10.0.0.12     Ubuntu 22.04

# Step 2: Prepare worker-1 for maintenance
kubectl cordon worker-1
# node/worker-1 cordoned

# Step 3: Drain it (move anihpj pods to worker-2)
kubectl drain worker-1 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data
# evicting pod anihpj/anihpj-web-7d4b8c6f9-xk2vn
# pod/anihpj-web-7d4b8c6f9-xk2vn evicted

# Step 4: Verify pods moved
kubectl get pods -n anihpj -o wide
# NAME                          READY   NODE
# anihpj-web-7d4b8c6f9-m8p3q   1/1     worker-2  вҶҗ moved here

# Step 5: Perform maintenance (upgrade kubelet, etc.)

# Step 6: Bring node back
kubectl uncordon worker-1
# node/worker-1 uncordoned

вҡ пёҸ Drain Fails? Common Causes

  • Unmanaged pods вҖ” Pods not managed by a controller (bare pods). Fix: add --force flag (pods will be deleted, not rescheduled!)
  • PodDisruptionBudget violated вҖ” PDB prevents eviction. Fix: check PDB with kubectl get pdb -A, adjust minAvailable
  • emptyDir data loss вҖ” Need --delete-emptydir-data flag to acknowledge data loss
  • DaemonSet pods вҖ” Always need --ignore-daemonsets (they're present on every node)

A.3 Workload Commands

COMMANDS рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

Deployment lifecycle commands вҖ” create, scale, update, rollback. These are your bread and butter on the CKA exam.

Deployment Rollout Lifecycle

  CREATE в”Җв”Җв–¶ SCALE в”Җв”Җв–¶ UPDATE в”Җв”Җв–¶ ROLLBACK
    в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
    в–ј          в–ј         в–ј          в–ј
  kubectl    kubectl   kubectl    kubectl
  create     scale     set image  rollout undo
  deploy     deploy    deploy/    deploy/
  --image    --rep=5   name       name
    в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
    в–ј          в–ј         в–ј          в–ј
  1 replica   5 reps   Rolling    Previous
  created     running  update     revision
                       started    restored

  рҹ“Ҡ MONITOR: kubectl rollout status deploy/name
  рҹ“ң HISTORY: kubectl rollout history deploy/name

Deployment & Workload Commands

CommandEffectCKA Speed Tip
kubectl create deployment anihpj-web --image=anihpj:v1 --replicas=3 Create deployment with 3 replicas Fastest way вҖ” no YAML needed
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-web --replicas=5 Scale to 5 replicas Instant вҖ” no rolling update
kubectl set image deploy/anihpj-web web=anihpj:v2 Update container image (triggers rolling update) Container name must match spec
kubectl rollout status deploy/anihpj-web Watch rollout progress Blocks until complete or failed
kubectl rollout history deploy/anihpj-web Show revision history See all past versions
kubectl rollout undo deploy/anihpj-web Rollback to previous revision Instant rollback вҖ” saves exam time
kubectl rollout undo deploy/anihpj-web --to-revision=2 Rollback to specific revision When previous isn't enough
terminal вҖ” anihpj deployment lifecycle 
# Create deployment imperatively
kubectl create deployment anihpj-web --image=anihpj:v1 --replicas=3 -n anihpj

# Generate YAML without creating (exam trick!)
kubectl create deployment anihpj-web --image=anihpj:v1 --replicas=3 \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml > deploy.yaml

# Scale up for traffic
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-web --replicas=5 -n anihpj

# Update to new version (triggers rolling update)
kubectl set image deployment/anihpj-web web=anihpj:v2 -n anihpj

# Watch the rollout
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-web -n anihpj
# Waiting for deployment "anihpj-web" rollout to finish: 2 of 5 updated...
# deployment "anihpj-web" successfully rolled out

# Something wrong? Rollback!
kubectl rollout undo deployment/anihpj-web -n anihpj
# deployment.apps/anihpj-web rolled back

The --dry-run=client -o yaml combo is your best friend. Use it to generate YAML for any resource, then edit as needed. With the $do alias: k create deploy anihpj-web --image=anihpj:v1 $do > deploy.yaml

A.4 Debugging Commands

COMMANDS рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

Debugging is 30% of the CKA exam. Master these commands вҖ” they're your diagnostic toolkit.

Pod Debugging Decision Tree

                        рҹ”Қ Pod Not Working?
                        в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
                                в”Ӯ
               в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
               в–ј                в–ј                в–ј
          Pod Status?      Container        Can't reach
               в”Ӯ           crashing?        the service?
               в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ        в–ј                в–ј
    в–ј          в–ј      в–ј   kubectl logs     kubectl get
  Pending  CrashLoop  Image  pod -c ctr    endpoints svc
    в”Ӯ      BackOff    Pull     в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
    в–ј        в–ј       BackOff   в–ј                в–ј
  describe  logs     describe  Fix app      Empty? вҶ’
  pod вҶ’     pod вҶ’    pod вҶ’     crash or     Labels don't
  check     check    check     missing      match between
  events    app      image/    config       pod & service
            error    secret

Debug & Inspect Commands

CommandWhat It ShowsDebug Scenario
kubectl describe pod <name> -n anihpj Full pod details, events, conditions First stop for ANY pod issue
kubectl logs <pod> -n anihpj Container stdout/stderr App crash, startup failure
kubectl logs <pod> --previous Logs from previous (crashed) container CrashLoopBackOff вҖ” see WHY it crashed
kubectl logs <pod> -c <container> Logs from specific container in multi-container pod Sidecar / init container issues
kubectl exec -it <pod> -- /bin/sh Open shell inside container Check files, env vars, network from inside
kubectl port-forward pod/<pod> 8080:8000 Forward local 8080 вҶ’ pod 8000 Test pod directly without Service
kubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.name=<pod> Events for specific pod only Targeted event search
kubectl auth can-i <verb> <resource> --as=<user> Check RBAC permissions Forbidden errors вҖ” who can do what?
terminal вҖ” debugging anihpj pods 
# Pod stuck in CrashLoopBackOff вҖ” investigate
kubectl describe pod anihpj-web-7d4b8c6f9-crash -n anihpj
# Events:
#   Warning  BackOff  kubelet  Back-off restarting failed container

# Get logs from the crashed container
kubectl logs anihpj-web-7d4b8c6f9-crash -n anihpj --previous
# ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'anihpj.settings'

# Shell into a running pod to investigate
kubectl exec -it anihpj-web-7d4b8c6f9-m8p3q -n anihpj -- /bin/sh
# $ env | grep DB          вҶҗ check environment variables
# $ cat /app/manage.py     вҶҗ check files exist
# $ python manage.py check вҶҗ test Django
# $ exit

# Test pod connectivity directly
kubectl port-forward pod/anihpj-web-7d4b8c6f9-m8p3q 8080:8000 -n anihpj
# Then: curl http://localhost:8080/jobs/

# Check who can create pods in anihpj namespace
kubectl auth can-i create pods -n anihpj --as=system:serviceaccount:anihpj:anihpj-sa
# yes

вҡ пёҸ Log Gotcha: Multi-Container Pods

If a pod has multiple containers (sidecar pattern), kubectl logs <pod> will fail with "a container name must be specified". Always use -c <container-name>. List containers with: kubectl get pod <name> -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[*].name}'

A.5 RBAC Commands

COMMANDS рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) commands for managing who can do what in your cluster. These are frequently tested on the CKA exam.

RBAC Components вҖ” The Permission Chain

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                    RBAC PERMISSION MODEL                           в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
  в”Ӯ                                                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   WHO (Subject)         BINDING              WHAT (Role)           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   рҹ‘Ө User            RoleBinding           Role                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   рҹ‘Ҙ Group           (namespace-scoped)    (namespace-scoped)      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   рҹӨ– ServiceAccount  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                      ClusterRoleBinding    ClusterRole              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                      (cluster-wide)        (cluster-wide)          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   Example for anihpj:                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ anihpj-sa  в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Ӯ anihpj-binding  в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Ӯ anihpj-role      в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ (SA)       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ (RoleBinding)   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ get,list pods    в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ in ns: anihpj   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ       в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

RBAC Imperative Commands

CommandCreatesScope
kubectl create role anihpj-role --verb=get,list --resource=pods -n anihpj Namespace-scoped Role anihpj namespace only
kubectl create clusterrole node-reader --verb=get,list,watch --resource=nodes Cluster-wide ClusterRole All namespaces
kubectl create rolebinding anihpj-binding --role=anihpj-role --serviceaccount=anihpj:anihpj-sa -n anihpj RoleBinding (links SA вҶ’ Role) anihpj namespace
kubectl create clusterrolebinding admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=admin ClusterRoleBinding Cluster-wide
kubectl auth can-i --list --as=system:serviceaccount:anihpj:anihpj-sa Lists all permissions for that SA Debug RBAC issues
terminal вҖ” RBAC setup for anihpj 
# Create ServiceAccount for anihpj app
kubectl create serviceaccount anihpj-sa -n anihpj

# Create Role: can read pods and services
kubectl create role anihpj-role \
  --verb=get,list,watch \
  --resource=pods,services \
  -n anihpj

# Bind the role to the ServiceAccount
kubectl create rolebinding anihpj-binding \
  --role=anihpj-role \
  --serviceaccount=anihpj:anihpj-sa \
  -n anihpj

# Verify permissions
kubectl auth can-i list pods -n anihpj \
  --as=system:serviceaccount:anihpj:anihpj-sa
# yes

kubectl auth can-i delete pods -n anihpj \
  --as=system:serviceaccount:anihpj:anihpj-sa
# no  вҶҗ correct! We only granted get,list,watch

# List ALL permissions for the SA
kubectl auth can-i --list \
  --as=system:serviceaccount:anihpj:anihpj-sa \
  -n anihpj

A.6 Certificate Commands

COMMANDS рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

Kubernetes uses TLS certificates extensively. Certificate management вҖ” checking expiry, renewing, and inspecting certs вҖ” is a core CKA topic.

Kubernetes Certificate Architecture

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ              KUBERNETES PKI вҖ” /etc/kubernetes/pki/                  в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
  в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“Ғ /etc/kubernetes/pki/                                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ ca.crt + ca.key               вҶҗ Cluster CA (root of trust)    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ apiserver.crt + apiserver.key  вҶҗ API Server cert               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ apiserver-kubelet-client.crt   вҶҗ API вҶ’ kubelet auth           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ front-proxy-ca.crt            вҶҗ Aggregation layer CA          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җ sa.key + sa.pub               вҶҗ ServiceAccount signing        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җ etcd/                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      в”ңв”Җв”Җ ca.crt + ca.key           вҶҗ etcd CA (separate!)           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      в”ңв”Җв”Җ server.crt + server.key   вҶҗ etcd server cert              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      в”ңв”Җв”Җ peer.crt + peer.key       вҶҗ etcd peer-to-peer             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      в””в”Җв”Җ healthcheck-client.crt    вҶҗ etcd health check             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вҸ° Default validity: 1 year (except CA: 10 years)                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ”„ kubeadm certs renew all вҶҗ renews all 1-year certs              в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Certificate Management Commands

CommandWhat It DoesWhen to Use
kubeadm certs check-expiration Show expiry dates for all certs Regular health check / before renewal
kubeadm certs renew all Renew all kubeadm-managed certificates Before expiry or after upgrade
openssl x509 -in <cert> -text -noout Inspect certificate details (CN, SANs, dates) Verify specific cert properties
terminal вҖ” certificate management 
# Check expiration dates for ALL certs
sudo kubeadm certs check-expiration
# CERTIFICATE                EXPIRES                  RESIDUAL TIME
# admin.conf                 Dec 15, 2026 10:30 UTC   364d
# apiserver                  Dec 15, 2026 10:30 UTC   364d
# apiserver-etcd-client      Dec 15, 2026 10:30 UTC   364d
# apiserver-kubelet-client   Dec 15, 2026 10:30 UTC   364d
# controller-manager.conf    Dec 15, 2026 10:30 UTC   364d
# etcd-healthcheck-client    Dec 15, 2026 10:30 UTC   364d
# etcd-peer                  Dec 15, 2026 10:30 UTC   364d
# etcd-server                Dec 15, 2026 10:30 UTC   364d
# front-proxy-client         Dec 15, 2026 10:30 UTC   364d
# scheduler.conf             Dec 15, 2026 10:30 UTC   364d
# CA                         Dec 12, 2035 10:30 UTC   10y  вҶҗ CA lasts 10 years

# Renew all certificates
sudo kubeadm certs renew all
# [renew] Reading configuration from the cluster...
# certificate apiserver renewed
# certificate apiserver-kubelet-client renewed
# ...

# IMPORTANT: Restart control plane after renewal!
sudo systemctl restart kubelet

# Inspect a specific certificate
openssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt -text -noout | grep -A2 "Validity"
#     Validity
#         Not Before: Dec 15 10:30:00 2025 GMT
#         Not After : Dec 15 10:30:00 2026 GMT

# Check Subject Alternative Names (SANs)
openssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt -text -noout | grep -A1 "Subject Alternative"
#     X509v3 Subject Alternative Name:
#         DNS:cp-1, DNS:kubernetes, DNS:kubernetes.default, IP:10.96.0.1, IP:10.0.0.10

вҡ пёҸ After Renewing Certs

You must restart the control plane components after renewing certificates. With kubeadm, simply restart kubelet: sudo systemctl restart kubelet. The static pods (API server, controller-manager, scheduler) will be recreated automatically. If you forget this step, the old (expired) certs remain loaded in memory!

A.7 etcd Commands

COMMANDS рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

etcd backup and restore is guaranteed on the CKA exam. Every command requires TLS flags вҖ” memorize the cert paths.

etcd Backup & Restore Flow

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                 etcd BACKUP & RESTORE WORKFLOW                     в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
  в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ“ё BACKUP                          рҹ”„ RESTORE                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ                         в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  etcdctl snapshot save              etcdctl snapshot restore        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ                                   в”Ӯ                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в–ј                                   в–ј                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  /opt/backup/                        /var/lib/etcd-restored/        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  etcd-snapshot.db                    (new data dir)                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ                                   в”Ӯ                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ       в–ј                                   в–ј                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  etcdctl snapshot                    Update etcd pod manifest:      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  status (verify)                     --data-dir=/var/lib/           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                      etcd-restored                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                           в”Ӯ                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                           в–ј                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вҡ пёҸ ALL COMMANDS REQUIRE:            Restart: etcd auto-restarts    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  --cacert, --cert, --key             from static pod manifest       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  + ETCDCTL_API=3                                                    в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

etcd Admin Commands

CommandPurposeCKA Exam Note
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot save <file> Create backup snapshot Always specify full path: /opt/backup/snapshot.db
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot restore <file> Restore from snapshot Use --data-dir to set new directory
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot status <file> --write-table Verify backup integrity Shows hash, revision, total keys
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl member list List etcd cluster members Verify HA etcd cluster health
terminal вҖ” etcd backup & restore (CKA exam) 
# в•җв•җв•җ STEP 1: BACKUP в•җв•җв•җ
# The TLS flags are MANDATORY вҖ” memorize these paths!
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot save /opt/backup/etcd-snapshot.db \
  --cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt \
  --cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt \
  --key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key \
  --endpoints=https://127.0.0.1:2379
# Snapshot saved at /opt/backup/etcd-snapshot.db

# в•җв•җв•җ STEP 2: VERIFY BACKUP в•җв•җв•җ
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot status /opt/backup/etcd-snapshot.db \
  --write-table
# +----------+----------+------------+------------+
# |   HASH   | REVISION | TOTAL KEYS | TOTAL SIZE |
# +----------+----------+------------+------------+
# | 3c5d8a2f |    48291 |       1043 |     4.1 MB |
# +----------+----------+------------+------------+

# в•җв•җв•җ STEP 3: RESTORE в•җв•җв•җ
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot restore /opt/backup/etcd-snapshot.db \
  --data-dir=/var/lib/etcd-restored

# в•җв•җв•җ STEP 4: UPDATE ETCD MANIFEST в•җв•җв•җ
# Edit the static pod manifest:
sudo vi /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml
# Change: --data-dir=/var/lib/etcd
# To:     --data-dir=/var/lib/etcd-restored
#
# ALSO update the hostPath volume:
# path: /var/lib/etcd вҶ’ path: /var/lib/etcd-restored

# etcd will auto-restart with restored data

# в•җв•җв•җ VERIFY в•җв•җв•җ
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl member list \
  --cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt \
  --cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt \
  --key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key
# Shows: member ID, name, peer URLs, client URLs

вҡ пёҸ The 3 Cert Flags вҖ” Don't Forget Any!

  • --cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt вҖ” etcd CA certificate
  • --cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt вҖ” etcd server certificate
  • --key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key вҖ” etcd server private key

Also: ETCDCTL_API=3 is required. Without it, etcdctl defaults to API v2 which uses completely different commands. If your command returns an error about unknown flags, check this first!

вҡ пёҸ Restore Creates a NEW Data Directory

Never restore into the existing /var/lib/etcd directory. Always use a new path like /var/lib/etcd-restored, then update the etcd static pod manifest to point to the new directory. This prevents data corruption and allows you to keep the old data as a safety net.

рҹ“Ӣ Appendix A вҖ” Quick Reference Card

  • вң… A.1 вҖ” Cluster health: cluster-info, top nodes, get events, api-resources
  • вң… A.2 вҖ” Node lifecycle: cordon вҶ’ drain вҶ’ maintain вҶ’ uncordon
  • вң… A.3 вҖ” Workloads: create deploy, scale, set image, rollout undo
  • вң… A.4 вҖ” Debug: describe, logs --previous, exec -it, port-forward
  • вң… A.5 вҖ” RBAC: create role, create rolebinding, auth can-i
  • вң… A.6 вҖ” Certs: kubeadm certs check-expiration, renew all, openssl x509
  • вң… A.7 вҖ” etcd: snapshot save, snapshot restore, always use TLS flags + ETCDCTL_API=3

рҹ“қ Appendix B: CKA YAML Templates

APPENDIX рҹ“Ӣ REFERENCE

12 fully annotated YAML templates covering all CKA exam resources вҖ” sections B.1 through B.12. Every template uses the anihpj project with the jobpost app. Copy, adapt, deploy.

YAML Template Map вҖ” Which Template for Which Task?

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ            CKA YAML TEMPLATE QUICK-FIND MAP                            в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
  в”Ӯ                                                                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹҸ—пёҸ WORKLOADS               рҹҢҗ NETWORKING          рҹ”’ SECURITY         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ              в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ          в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  B.1  Pod                   B.3  Service (3 types) B.6  Role+Binding    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  B.2  Deployment            B.8  NetworkPolicy     B.7  ClusterRole     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  B.10 PDB                   B.11 Ingress           B.9  ServiceAccount  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ’ҫ STORAGE                 вҡҷпёҸ CONFIGURATION                            в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ              в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  B.4  PV + PVC             B.12 ConfigMap                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  B.5  StorageClass         B.12 Secret                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  рҹ’Ў Exam tip: Generate base YAML with --dry-run=client -o yaml          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     then ADD fields like resources, probes, volumes from memory         в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

YAML Generation Shortcut: Never write YAML from scratch on the exam. Generate it first, then add what's missing:

  • kubectl run anihpj-web --image=anihpj:v1 --dry-run=client -o yaml > pod.yaml
  • kubectl create deploy anihpj-web --image=anihpj:v1 --dry-run=client -o yaml > deploy.yaml
  • kubectl expose deploy anihpj-web --port=80 --target-port=8000 --dry-run=client -o yaml > svc.yaml

B.1 Pod Template (with all common fields)

рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

The Pod is the atomic unit in Kubernetes. This template shows every field you'll encounter on the CKA exam.

Pod вҖ” Complete Exam Template

Fields marked with вҶҗ are most commonly tested on the CKA exam

apiVersion: v1                          # Always v1 for pods
kind: Pod                               # Object type
metadata:
  name: anihpj-web                      # вҶҗ Required: unique within namespace
  namespace: anihpj                     # вҶҗ Omit for default namespace
  labels:                               # вҶҗ Key-value pairs for selection
    app: anihpj
    tier: frontend
    version: v1
spec:
  containers:                           # вҶҗ List of containers (at least 1)
  - name: web                           # Container name
    image: anihpj:v1                    # вҶҗ Image[:tag]
    ports:
    - containerPort: 8000               # Informational only вҖ” does NOT expose
    env:                                # вҶҗ Environment variables
    - name: DEBUG
      value: "False"
    - name: DB_HOST
      valueFrom:
        configMapKeyRef:                # вҶҗ From ConfigMap
          name: anihpj-config
          key: DB_HOST
    - name: SECRET_KEY
      valueFrom:
        secretKeyRef:                   # вҶҗ From Secret
          name: anihpj-secret
          key: SECRET_KEY
    resources:                          # вҶҗ ALWAYS set in production
      requests:                         # Scheduling guarantee
        memory: "128Mi"
        cpu: "250m"
      limits:                           # Hard ceiling
        memory: "256Mi"
        cpu: "500m"
    readinessProbe:                     # вҶҗ When pod is ready for traffic
      httpGet:
        path: /health
        port: 8000
      initialDelaySeconds: 5
      periodSeconds: 10
    livenessProbe:                      # вҶҗ When to restart container
      httpGet:
        path: /health
        port: 8000
      initialDelaySeconds: 15
      periodSeconds: 20
    volumeMounts:                       # вҶҗ Mount volumes into container
    - name: app-data
      mountPath: /app/data
  volumes:                              # вҶҗ Define volumes at pod level
  - name: app-data
    persistentVolumeClaim:
      claimName: anihpj-pvc
  restartPolicy: Always                 # Always | OnFailure | Never
  serviceAccountName: anihpj-sa         # вҶҗ Explicit SA (security best practice)

Pod Spec Structure вҖ” Visual Map

  Pod
   в”ңв”Җв”Җ metadata
   в”Ӯ    в”ңв”Җв”Җ name: anihpj-web
   в”Ӯ    в”ңв”Җв”Җ namespace: anihpj
   в”Ӯ    в””в”Җв”Җ labels: {app: anihpj}
   в”Ӯ
   в””в”Җв”Җ spec
        в”ңв”Җв”Җ serviceAccountName: anihpj-sa
        в”ңв”Җв”Җ restartPolicy: Always
        в”Ӯ
        в”ңв”Җв”Җ containers[]
        в”Ӯ    в””в”Җв”Җ [0] web
        в”Ӯ         в”ңв”Җв”Җ image: anihpj:v1
        в”Ӯ         в”ңв”Җв”Җ ports: [{containerPort: 8000}]
        в”Ӯ         в”ңв”Җв”Җ env: [{name, value/valueFrom}]
        в”Ӯ         в”ңв”Җв”Җ resources: {requests, limits}
        в”Ӯ         в”ңв”Җв”Җ readinessProbe: {httpGet}
        в”Ӯ         в”ңв”Җв”Җ livenessProbe: {httpGet}
        в”Ӯ         в””в”Җв”Җ volumeMounts: [{name, mountPath}]
        в”Ӯ
        в””в”Җв”Җ volumes[]
             в””в”Җв”Җ [0] {name: app-data, pvc: anihpj-pvc}

Multi-Container Pod (Sidecar Pattern) вҖ” Exam Favourite

The CKA frequently asks you to add a sidecar container to an existing pod

apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: anihpj-web-with-logger
  namespace: anihpj
spec:
  containers:
  - name: web                           # Main application container
    image: anihpj:v1
    ports:
    - containerPort: 8000
    volumeMounts:
    - name: shared-logs
      mountPath: /var/log/app
  - name: log-sidecar                   # вҶҗ Sidecar: ships logs
    image: busybox:1.36
    command: ["sh", "-c", "tail -f /var/log/app/access.log"]
    volumeMounts:
    - name: shared-logs                 # вҶҗ Same volume = shared filesystem
      mountPath: /var/log/app
  initContainers:                       # вҶҗ Runs BEFORE main containers
  - name: init-db-check
    image: busybox:1.36
    command: ["sh", "-c", "until nslookup anihpj-db-svc; do echo waiting; sleep 2; done"]
  volumes:
  - name: shared-logs
    emptyDir: {}                        # вҶҗ Temporary shared storage

Multi-Container Pod Patterns

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  Pod: anihpj-web-with-logger                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җ INIT CONTAINER в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  Runs FIRST, must complete   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  init-db-check            в”Ӯ  before main containers start в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  (waits for DB DNS)       в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ вң… done                                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в–ј                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җ MAIN CONTAINERS (run in parallel) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                                        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    shared     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ web         в”Ӯ    volume     в”Ӯ log-sidecar     в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ anihpj:v1   в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ busybox:1.36    в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ :8000       в”Ӯ  /var/log/app в”Ӯ tail -f logs    в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ               в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                                                        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

B.2 Deployment Template (with strategy)

рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

Deployments manage ReplicaSets which manage Pods. The strategy field controls how updates are rolled out.

Deployment вҖ” Rolling Update Strategy

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-web
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app: anihpj
spec:
  replicas: 3                           # вҶҗ Desired pod count
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate                 # вҶҗ RollingUpdate | Recreate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1                       # Extra pods during update (can be % or int)
      maxUnavailable: 0                 # вҶҗ Zero-downtime: never go below replicas
  selector:
    matchLabels:                        # вҶҗ MUST match template.metadata.labels
      app: anihpj
  template:                             # вҶҗ Pod template (same fields as B.1)
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj                     # вҶҗ MUST match selector.matchLabels
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: anihpj-sa
      containers:
      - name: web
        image: anihpj:v1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "128Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
          limits:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
StrategyBehaviorWhen to UseDowntime?
RollingUpdate Gradually replaces pods one by one Production вҖ” zero-downtime deploys вқҢ No
Recreate Kills ALL old pods, then creates new ones Dev/test, or when old+new can't coexist вң… Yes

вҡ пёҸ Label Mismatch = Deployment Stuck

If spec.selector.matchLabels doesn't match spec.template.metadata.labels, the Deployment can't find its pods. Kubernetes will reject the manifest outright. This is the #1 YAML mistake вҖ” always ensure these labels are identical.

B.3 Service Templates (ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer)

рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

Services expose pods to the network. Three types, each for different access patterns.

Service Types вҖ” Access Levels

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  вҳҒпёҸ INTERNET                                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ                                                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     в–ј                                                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  LoadBalancer    в”Ӯ вҶҗ Cloud provider provisions external LB       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  (external IP)  в”Ӯ   Port: 80 вҶ’ targetPort: 8000                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ                                                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     в–ј           в–ј                                                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”ӮNodePortв”Ӯ  в”ӮNodePortв”Ӯ вҶҗ Every node opens port 30000-32767        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ :30080 в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ :30080 в”Ӯ   Access: <nodeIP>:30080                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ            в–ј                                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  ClusterIP   в”Ӯ вҶҗ Internal only (default)                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  10.96.0.100 в”Ӯ   Access: anihpj-web-svc.anihpj.svc:80       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ                                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ     в–ј             в–ј                                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Pod  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ Pod  в”Ӯ вҶҗ selector: app=anihpj matches these        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ:8000 в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ:8000 в”Ӯ                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                              в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

ClusterIP Service (internal only вҖ” default)

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-web-svc
  namespace: anihpj
spec:
  type: ClusterIP                       # Default вҖ” can omit
  selector:
    app: anihpj                         # вҶҗ MUST match pod labels
  ports:
  - port: 80                            # Service port (what clients connect to)
    targetPort: 8000                    # Container port (where app listens)
    protocol: TCP

NodePort Service (external via node IP:port)

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-web-nodeport
  namespace: anihpj
spec:
  type: NodePort
  selector:
    app: anihpj
  ports:
  - port: 80                            # Service port
    targetPort: 8000                    # Container port
    nodePort: 30080                     # вҶҗ 30000-32767 range (omit for auto)

LoadBalancer Service (cloud provider LB)

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-web-lb
  namespace: anihpj
spec:
  type: LoadBalancer
  selector:
    app: anihpj
  ports:
  - port: 80                            # External port
    targetPort: 8000                    # Container port
  # Cloud provider assigns EXTERNAL-IP automatically
TypeAccessUse CaseImperative
ClusterIP Internal only DB, internal APIs kubectl expose deploy anihpj-web --port=80 --target-port=8000
NodePort External via nodeIP:port Dev/test, on-prem kubectl expose deploy anihpj-web --type=NodePort --port=80
LoadBalancer External via cloud LB Production, cloud kubectl expose deploy anihpj-web --type=LoadBalancer --port=80

B.4 PersistentVolume + PVC Template

рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

PVs are cluster-level storage resources; PVCs are namespace-scoped claims that bind to PVs. Admin creates PV, developer creates PVC.

PV вҶ” PVC Binding Flow

  ADMIN creates:                  DEVELOPER creates:          Pod uses:
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ               в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ        в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ PersistentVolв”Ӯ               в”Ӯ PersistentVol  в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ   Pod    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ    BINDING    в”Ӯ Claim          в”Ӯ MOUNT  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ capacity:10Giв”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ request: 10Gi  в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ volumes: в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ accessMode:  в”Ӯ  (automatic) в”Ӯ accessMode:    в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ  - pvc:  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  RWO         в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ  RWO           в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ  anihpj- в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ storageClass:в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ storageClass:  в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ  pvc     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  manual      в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ  manual        в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ               в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
     вҶ‘                              вҶ‘
     Must match:                    Must match:
     - accessModes                  - storageClassName
     - storageClassName             - capacity вүӨ PV capacity

PersistentVolume (admin creates)

apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolume
metadata:
  name: anihpj-pv                       # PVs are cluster-scoped (no namespace)
spec:
  capacity:
    storage: 10Gi                       # вҶҗ Storage amount
  accessModes:
  - ReadWriteOnce                       # вҶҗ RWO | ROX | RWX
  persistentVolumeReclaimPolicy: Retain # вҶҗ Retain | Delete | Recycle
  storageClassName: manual              # вҶҗ MUST match PVC
  hostPath:                             # вҶҗ Dev only! Use cloud storage in prod
    path: /data/anihpj-db

PersistentVolumeClaim (developer creates)

apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: anihpj-pvc
  namespace: anihpj
spec:
  accessModes:
  - ReadWriteOnce                       # вҶҗ Must match PV
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 10Gi                     # вҶҗ Must be вүӨ PV capacity
  storageClassName: manual              # вҶҗ Must match PV
Access ModeAbbreviationDescriptionUse Case
ReadWriteOnceRWORead-write by a single nodeDatabase (PostgreSQL)
ReadOnlyManyROXRead-only by many nodesShared config/assets
ReadWriteManyRWXRead-write by many nodesShared uploads (NFS)

B.5 StorageClass Template

рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

StorageClasses enable dynamic provisioning вҖ” no pre-created PVs needed. When a PVC references a StorageClass, Kubernetes automatically creates the PV.

Static vs Dynamic Provisioning

  STATIC Provisioning (B.4)              DYNAMIC Provisioning (B.5)
  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ             в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

  1. Admin creates PV manually           1. Admin creates StorageClass
     apiVersion: v1                          apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
     kind: PersistentVolume                  kind: StorageClass
     capacity: 10Gi                          provisioner: ebs.csi.aws.com
           в”Ӯ                                       в”Ӯ
  2. Dev creates PVC                     2. Dev creates PVC (same)
     storageClassName: manual                storageClassName: fast-storage
           в”Ӯ                                       в”Ӯ
  3. K8s binds PVC вҶ’ PV                  3. K8s AUTO-CREATES PV + binds!
     (must find matching PV)                 (provisioner handles it)
           в”Ӯ                                       в”Ӯ
           в–ј                                       в–ј
  вҡ пёҸ If no PV matches вҶ’ Pending          вң… PV created on-demand = no Pending

  рҹ’Ў Exam: Dynamic is preferred in cloud. Static for hostPath/local dev.

StorageClass вҖ” Dynamic Provisioning

apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: fast-storage
provisioner: kubernetes.io/no-provisioner  # вҶҗ Or: ebs.csi.aws.com, pd.csi.storage.gke.io
volumeBindingMode: WaitForFirstConsumer    # вҶҗ Delay until pod schedules (topology-aware)
reclaimPolicy: Delete                      # вҶҗ Delete PV when PVC is deleted
allowVolumeExpansion: true                 # вҶҗ Allow PVC resize
Binding ModeBehaviorBest For
ImmediatePV created as soon as PVC is createdSingle-zone clusters
WaitForFirstConsumerPV created when first pod using PVC is scheduledMulti-zone clusters (prevents zone mismatch)

B.6 Role + RoleBinding Template

рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

Namespace-scoped RBAC вҖ” grants permissions within a specific namespace.

Role (namespace-scoped permissions)

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: anihpj                     # вҶҗ Scoped to this namespace
  name: anihpj-role
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]                       # вҶҗ "" = core API group (pods, services, etc.)
  resources: ["pods", "services"]       # вҶҗ What resources
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]       # вҶҗ What actions allowed
- apiGroups: ["apps"]                   # вҶҗ apps group (deployments, replicasets)
  resources: ["deployments"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "update"]

RoleBinding (assigns role to subject)

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  namespace: anihpj                     # вҶҗ Same namespace as the Role
  name: anihpj-binding
subjects:                               # вҶҗ WHO gets the permissions
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: anihpj-sa
  namespace: anihpj
roleRef:                                # вҶҗ WHICH role to assign
  kind: Role                            # Role or ClusterRole
  name: anihpj-role
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
рҹ’Ў Common API Groups

"" (core): pods, services, secrets, configmaps, PVCs, namespaces, nodes, events. "apps": deployments, replicasets, statefulsets, daemonsets. "rbac.authorization.k8s.io": roles, rolebindings. "networking.k8s.io": networkpolicies, ingresses.

B.7 ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding Template

рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

Cluster-wide RBAC вҖ” grants permissions across all namespaces or on cluster-scoped resources (nodes, PVs).

ClusterRole (cluster-wide permissions)

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: node-reader                     # вҶҗ No namespace (cluster-scoped)
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["nodes"]                  # вҶҗ Cluster-scoped resource
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["persistentvolumes"]      # вҶҗ Also cluster-scoped
  verbs: ["get", "list"]

ClusterRoleBinding

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: node-reader-binding             # вҶҗ No namespace
subjects:
- kind: User
  name: jane
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: node-reader
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
Role + RoleBindingClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding
ScopeSingle namespaceAll namespaces / cluster resources
Use forApp-level access (anihpj pods)Admin access (nodes, PVs)
Has namespace?Yes (in metadata)No
Exampleanihpj-sa can read pods in anihpj nsjane can read nodes cluster-wide

B.8 NetworkPolicy Template

рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

NetworkPolicies control pod-to-pod traffic. By default, all pods can talk to all pods. A NetworkPolicy restricts that вҖ” deny by default, allow explicitly.

NetworkPolicy вҖ” anihpj Traffic Flow

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  namespace: anihpj                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     вң… port 5432    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  anihpj-web  в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶ в”Ӯ  anihpj-db       в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  app: anihpj в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ  app: anihpj-db  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                               в–І                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     вқҢ BLOCKED              в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  other-pod   в”Ӯ в”Җ в”Җ в”Җ в”Җ в”Җ в”Җ в”Җ в”Җ в”Җ в”Җ в”Җ в”Җ в”Җ в”ҳ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  app: hacker в”Ӯ    NetworkPolicy denies                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    non-matching pods                          в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

NetworkPolicy вҖ” Allow Only anihpj-web вҶ’ anihpj-db

apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: db-restrict
  namespace: anihpj
spec:
  podSelector:                          # вҶҗ Apply to these pods
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-db
  policyTypes:
  - Ingress                             # вҶҗ Control incoming traffic
  ingress:
  - from:
    - podSelector:                      # вҶҗ Only allow FROM these pods
        matchLabels:
          app: anihpj
    ports:
    - port: 5432                        # вҶҗ Only on PostgreSQL port
      protocol: TCP

вҡ пёҸ NetworkPolicy Requires a CNI That Supports It

NetworkPolicies only work if your CNI plugin enforces them. Calico, Cilium, Weave Net support them. Flannel does NOT. On the CKA exam, the cluster uses a supporting CNI. If you apply a NetworkPolicy and traffic still flows unrestricted, check your CNI plugin.

Deny-All Default Policy (Common Exam Question!)

Apply this FIRST, then allow specific traffic with additional policies

apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: deny-all-ingress
  namespace: anihpj
spec:
  podSelector: {}                       # вҶҗ Empty = applies to ALL pods in namespace
  policyTypes:
  - Ingress                             # вҶҗ Block all incoming traffic
  # No ingress rules = nothing allowed
рҹ’Ў NetworkPolicy Logic

No policy = all traffic allowed. Policy with empty podSelector = applies to all pods. Policy with no ingress rules = deny all ingress. Combine deny-all + specific allow policies for a "zero trust" network model. The CKA exam loves this pattern.

B.9 ServiceAccount Template

рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

ServiceAccounts provide identity for pods. Each pod runs as a ServiceAccount вҖ” always create a dedicated one instead of using default.

ServiceAccount

apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: anihpj-sa
  namespace: anihpj
automountServiceAccountToken: false     # вҶҗ Security: don't auto-mount token
                                        #   Only set true if pod needs API access
вҡ пёҸ Security Best Practice

Set automountServiceAccountToken: false unless your pod actually needs to talk to the Kubernetes API. The default SA token mounted at /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token gives the pod API access. If compromised, attackers could query the API. The CKA exam may ask you to configure this.

terminal вҖ” ServiceAccount lifecycle 
# Create SA imperatively
kubectl create serviceaccount anihpj-sa -n anihpj

# Assign to a pod (in pod spec)
# spec.serviceAccountName: anihpj-sa

# Verify which SA a pod uses
kubectl get pod anihpj-web -n anihpj -o jsonpath='{.spec.serviceAccountName}'
# anihpj-sa

# Check if token is mounted
kubectl exec anihpj-web -n anihpj -- ls /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/
# ca.crt  namespace  token

# Full RBAC chain: create SA вҶ’ create Role вҶ’ bind them
kubectl create sa anihpj-sa -n anihpj
kubectl create role anihpj-role --verb=get,list --resource=pods -n anihpj
kubectl create rolebinding anihpj-binding --role=anihpj-role \
  --serviceaccount=anihpj:anihpj-sa -n anihpj

B.10 PodDisruptionBudget Template

рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

PDBs protect your app during voluntary disruptions (node drain, cluster upgrade). They guarantee minimum availability.

PodDisruptionBudget

apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: anihpj-pdb
  namespace: anihpj
spec:
  minAvailable: 2                       # вҶҗ At least 2 pods must stay running
  # OR: maxUnavailable: 1              # вҶҗ At most 1 pod can be down
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj                       # вҶҗ Protects pods with this label
FieldMeaningExample (3 replicas)
minAvailable: 2Keep at least 2 runningCan evict max 1 at a time
maxUnavailable: 1At most 1 can be downSame effect for 3 replicas
minAvailable: "50%"Keep at least 50% runningCan evict 1 of 3

вҡ пёҸ PDB Blocks kubectl drain!

If a PDB prevents eviction, kubectl drain will hang indefinitely. Common CKA trap: you try to drain a node but it just waits. Check PDBs: kubectl get pdb -A. If minAvailable equals the current replica count and there's only one node, the drain can never succeed. You must either scale up first or temporarily lower the PDB's minAvailable.

B.11 Ingress Template

рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

Ingress provides HTTP/HTTPS routing from external traffic to internal Services. Requires an Ingress Controller (e.g., nginx) to be installed in the cluster.

Ingress Routing вҖ” anihpj Example

  Internet
     в”Ӯ
     в–ј
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ              Ingress Controller (nginx)                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Rule 1: anihpj.example.com/                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в””в”Җв”Җв–¶ Service: anihpj-web-svc:80                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Rule 2: anihpj.example.com/api/                         в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в””в”Җв”Җв–¶ Service: anihpj-api-svc:80                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Rule 3: admin.example.com/                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ           в””в”Җв”Җв–¶ Service: admin-svc:80                     в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
     в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ
     в–ј                    в–ј                    в–ј
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯanihpj  в”Ӯ         в”Ӯanihpj  в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ admin  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯweb podsв”Ӯ         в”Ӯapi podsв”Ӯ         в”Ӯ pods   в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Ingress вҖ” Host & Path-Based Routing

apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: anihpj-ingress
  namespace: anihpj
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx               # вҶҗ Which Ingress Controller to use
  rules:
  - host: anihpj.example.com            # вҶҗ Domain name routing
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /                         # вҶҗ URL path
        pathType: Prefix                # вҶҗ Prefix | Exact | ImplementationSpecific
        backend:
          service:
            name: anihpj-web-svc        # вҶҗ Target Service name
            port:
              number: 80                # вҶҗ Target Service port
      - path: /api
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: anihpj-api-svc
            port:
              number: 80
pathTypeMatchesExample
PrefixURL prefix match/jobs matches /jobs, /jobs/123
ExactExact URL match only/jobs matches only /jobs

Ingress with TLS (HTTPS)

The CKA may ask you to configure TLS termination вҖ” create the Secret first

# Step 1: Create TLS secret
# kubectl create secret tls anihpj-tls \
#   --cert=tls.crt --key=tls.key -n anihpj

# Step 2: Reference in Ingress
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: anihpj-ingress-tls
  namespace: anihpj
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:                                  # вҶҗ TLS termination
  - hosts:
    - anihpj.example.com
    secretName: anihpj-tls              # вҶҗ Secret with tls.crt + tls.key
  rules:
  - host: anihpj.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: anihpj-web-svc
            port:
              number: 80

B.12 ConfigMap + Secret Template

рҹ“қ CKA EXAM

ConfigMaps store non-sensitive config; Secrets store sensitive data (base64-encoded, not encrypted by default).

ConfigMap

apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: anihpj-config
  namespace: anihpj
data:                                   # вҶҗ Key-value pairs (plain text)
  DEBUG: "False"
  DB_HOST: "anihpj-db-svc"
  DB_PORT: "5432"
  ALLOWED_HOSTS: "*"
  DB_NAME: "anihpj_db"

Secret (base64-encoded values)

apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: anihpj-secret
  namespace: anihpj
type: Opaque                            # вҶҗ Most common type
data:                                   # вҶҗ Values MUST be base64-encoded
  SECRET_KEY: c2VjcmV0MTIz              # echo -n 'secret123' | base64
  DB_PASSWORD: cGFzc3dvcmQ=             # echo -n 'password' | base64
  DB_USER: YW5paHBq                     # echo -n 'anihpj' | base64
terminal вҖ” imperative ConfigMap/Secret creation 
# Create ConfigMap imperatively
kubectl create configmap anihpj-config \
  --from-literal=DEBUG=False \
  --from-literal=DB_HOST=anihpj-db-svc \
  --from-literal=ALLOWED_HOSTS='*' \
  -n anihpj

# Create Secret imperatively (auto-encodes to base64!)
kubectl create secret generic anihpj-secret \
  --from-literal=SECRET_KEY=secret123 \
  --from-literal=DB_PASSWORD=password \
  -n anihpj

# Decode a secret value
kubectl get secret anihpj-secret -n anihpj -o jsonpath='{.data.DB_PASSWORD}' | base64 -d
# password

ConfigMap/Secret вҶ’ Pod вҖ” Three Injection Methods

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  METHOD 1: Environment Variables (most common)                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ                 в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  env:                                                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  - name: DB_HOST                                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    valueFrom:                                                   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      configMapKeyRef:                                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ        name: anihpj-config                                      в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ        key: DB_HOST                                             в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
  в”Ӯ  METHOD 2: envFrom (all keys at once)                           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ                          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  envFrom:                                                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  - configMapRef:                                                в”Ӯ
