  в”Ӯ      name: anihpj-config                                        в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
  в”Ӯ  METHOD 3: Volume Mount (as files)                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  volumes:                                                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  - name: config-vol                                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    configMap:                                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ      name: anihpj-config                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  volumeMounts:                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  - name: config-vol                                             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ    mountPath: /etc/config     вҶҗ Each key becomes a file         в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

вҡ пёҸ Secrets Aren't Encrypted by Default!

Kubernetes Secrets are only base64-encoded, not encrypted. Anyone with API access can decode them. For real security: enable encryption at rest (EncryptionConfiguration), use RBAC to restrict Secret access, and consider external tools like HashiCorp Vault or Sealed Secrets.

рҹ“Ӣ Appendix B вҖ” Template Quick Reference

  • вң… B.1 Pod вҖ” containers, env, resources, probes, volumes
  • вң… B.2 Deployment вҖ” replicas, strategy (RollingUpdate/Recreate), selector must match labels
  • вң… B.3 Service вҖ” ClusterIP (internal), NodePort (external:30000-32767), LoadBalancer (cloud)
  • вң… B.4 PV+PVC вҖ” capacity, accessModes, storageClassName must match
  • вң… B.5 StorageClass вҖ” dynamic provisioning, WaitForFirstConsumer
  • вң… B.6 Role+RoleBinding вҖ” namespace-scoped permissions
  • вң… B.7 ClusterRole+ClusterRoleBinding вҖ” cluster-wide permissions
  • вң… B.8 NetworkPolicy вҖ” pod-level firewall, requires supporting CNI
  • вң… B.9 ServiceAccount вҖ” pod identity, disable auto-mount token
  • вң… B.10 PDB вҖ” minAvailable or maxUnavailable during disruptions
  • вң… B.11 Ingress вҖ” HTTP routing by host/path, needs Ingress Controller
  • вң… B.12 ConfigMap+Secret вҖ” env vars, envFrom, volume mount

рҹҡЁ Appendix C: Common Errors and Solutions

APPENDIX рҹ“Ӣ REFERENCE

Quick reference table вҖ” Error вҶ’ Cause вҶ’ Fix for the most common Kubernetes issues you'll encounter on the CKA exam and in production.

Troubleshooting Decision Tree вҖ” Where to Start

  Something is wrong!
        в”Ӯ
        в–ј
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     YES     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ Pod running?       в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ Check: logs, exec, describe       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ kubectl get pods   в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ вҶ’ CrashLoopBackOff (app crash)    в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в”Ӯ вҶ’ OOMKilled (memory limit)        в”Ӯ
           в”Ӯ NO                     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
           в–ј
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     YES     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ Pod Pending?       в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ Check: describe pod events        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ вҶ’ Insufficient resources          в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в”Ӯ вҶ’ No matching PV                  в”Ӯ
           в”Ӯ NO                     в”Ӯ вҶ’ Taints/tolerations mismatch     в”Ӯ
           в–ј                        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     YES     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ ImagePullBackOff?  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ Check: image name, tag, registry  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ вҶ’ Wrong image name/tag            в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в”Ӯ вҶ’ Missing imagePullSecrets        в”Ӯ
           в”Ӯ NO                     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
           в–ј
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     YES     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ Node NotReady?     в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ SSH to node, check kubelet        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ kubectl get nodes  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ вҶ’ systemctl status kubelet        в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ            в”Ӯ вҶ’ journalctl -u kubelet           в”Ӯ
           в”Ӯ NO                     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
           в–ј
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ API/Network issue? в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶ Check kubeconfig, API server, certs
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Exam Debugging Shortcuts:

  • kubectl describe pod <name> вҖ” Always start here; the Events section at the bottom shows why things failed
  • kubectl logs <pod> --previous вҖ” See logs from the LAST crashed container
  • kubectl get events --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' вҖ” Cluster-wide timeline of issues
Error / Status Category Common Cause How to Diagnose Fix
CrashLoopBackOff рҹҸ—пёҸ Workload App crashes on startup вҖ” missing env var, wrong command, config error kubectl logs <pod> --previous
kubectl describe pod <pod>		 Fix app config/code. Check command, args, env vars. Ensure ConfigMap/Secret exists.
ImagePullBackOff рҹҸ—пёҸ Workload Wrong image name/tag, private registry without credentials kubectl describe pod <pod> вҶ’ Events section Fix image name. For private registries: create imagePullSecrets and reference in pod spec.
Pending (Pod) рҹҸ—пёҸ Workload Insufficient CPU/memory, no node matches nodeSelector/affinity, all nodes tainted kubectl describe pod <pod> вҶ’ Events
kubectl describe node <node>		 Reduce resource requests, add nodes, remove taints, or add tolerations.
Pending (PVC) рҹ’ҫ Storage No PV matches accessMode/capacity/storageClass kubectl describe pvc <name>
kubectl get pv		 Create matching PV or fix StorageClass name. Ensure accessModes and storageClassName match.
NodeNotReady рҹ–ҘпёҸ Node kubelet stopped, resource pressure (disk/memory), network partition ssh <node>
systemctl status kubelet
journalctl -u kubelet		 Restart kubelet: systemctl restart kubelet. Free disk/memory. Check network connectivity.
FailedScheduling рҹ–ҘпёҸ Node All nodes tainted (no matching tolerations), nodeAffinity mismatch kubectl describe pod <pod> вҶ’ Events
kubectl describe node <node> вҶ’ Taints		 Add tolerations to pod spec, or remove taint: kubectl taint node <n> key:NoSchedule-
Forbidden рҹ”’ RBAC ServiceAccount/User lacks permissions kubectl auth can-i <verb> <resource> --as=system:serviceaccount:ns:sa Create or update Role/ClusterRole + Binding to grant required permissions.
connection refused рҹҢҗ Network Target pod/service not running, wrong port, NetworkPolicy blocking kubectl get svc
kubectl get endpoints
kubectl get netpol		 Ensure target pod is running and healthy. Verify Service selector matches pod labels. Check NetworkPolicy rules.
certificate has expired рҹ”җ Certs Kubernetes PKI cert past its validity period (typically 1 year) kubeadm certs check-expiration
openssl x509 -in /etc/kubernetes/pki/apiserver.crt -noout -dates		 kubeadm certs renew all then restart control plane:
systemctl restart kubelet
etcd cluster is unavailable рҹ—„пёҸ etcd etcd pod down, cert expired, disk full kubectl -n kube-system get pods -l component=etcd
crictl ps | grep etcd		 Check etcd logs. Verify certs in /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/. Restore from backup if data corrupt.
unable to connect to server вҡҷпёҸ API Wrong kubeconfig, API server pod down, firewall blocking port 6443 kubectl cluster-info
cat ~/.kube/config вҖ” check server URL
crictl ps | grep kube-api		 Fix kubeconfig server URL. Restart API server. Check firewall rules for port 6443.
OOMKilled рҹҸ—пёҸ Workload Container exceeded its memory limit kubectl describe pod <pod> вҶ’ Last State: OOMKilled Increase resources.limits.memory, or fix memory leak in application.

Top 5 CKA Exam Errors вҖ” Detailed Fix Walkthroughs

рҹ’Ў CKA Debug Pattern (memorize this!)

describe вҶ’ logs вҶ’ exec вҶ’ events. Start with describe for events, check logs for app errors, exec into the pod to test connectivity, and get events for cluster-wide issues.

terminal вҖ” CrashLoopBackOff deep debug 
# 1. Check pod status and restart count
kubectl get pod anihpj-web -n anihpj
# NAME         READY   STATUS             RESTARTS   AGE
# anihpj-web   0/1     CrashLoopBackOff   5          2m

# 2. See why it crashed (logs from PREVIOUS container)
kubectl logs anihpj-web -n anihpj --previous
# ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'django'
# вҶ’ Fix: wrong image or missing dependency

# 3. Check events for scheduling/pull issues
kubectl describe pod anihpj-web -n anihpj | tail -20
# Events:
#   Warning  BackOff  kubelet  Back-off restarting failed container

# 4. If env var issue вҖ” check ConfigMap exists
kubectl get configmap anihpj-config -n anihpj
kubectl get secret anihpj-secret -n anihpj
terminal вҖ” Pending Pod / FailedScheduling debug 
# 1. Check WHY it's pending
kubectl describe pod anihpj-web -n anihpj | grep -A5 Events
# 0/3 nodes are available: 3 Insufficient memory

# 2. Check node resources
kubectl top nodes
kubectl describe node worker-1 | grep -A5 "Allocated resources"

# 3. If taint issue вҖ” check node taints
kubectl describe node worker-1 | grep Taints
# Taints: node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane:NoSchedule

# 4. Fix: add toleration to pod OR remove taint
kubectl taint node worker-1 node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane:NoSchedule-
terminal вҖ” RBAC Forbidden debug 
# 1. Test what the SA can do
kubectl auth can-i get pods --as=system:serviceaccount:anihpj:anihpj-sa -n anihpj
# no

# 2. Check existing roles/bindings
kubectl get roles,rolebindings -n anihpj
kubectl get clusterroles,clusterrolebindings | grep anihpj

# 3. Fix: create the missing Role + RoleBinding
kubectl create role anihpj-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=pods -n anihpj
kubectl create rolebinding anihpj-binding --role=anihpj-role \
  --serviceaccount=anihpj:anihpj-sa -n anihpj

# 4. Verify
kubectl auth can-i get pods --as=system:serviceaccount:anihpj:anihpj-sa -n anihpj
# yes
terminal вҖ” NodeNotReady recovery 
# 1. Identify the problem node
kubectl get nodes
# NAME       STATUS     ROLES           AGE    VERSION
# cp-1       Ready      control-plane   30d    v1.31.0
# worker-1   NotReady   <none>          30d    v1.31.0  вҶҗ PROBLEM

# 2. SSH to the affected node
ssh worker-1

# 3. Check kubelet status
systemctl status kubelet
# Active: inactive (dead)  вҶҗ kubelet stopped!

# 4. Check kubelet logs for the root cause
journalctl -u kubelet --no-pager | tail -30
# "failed to run Kubelet: unable to load bootstrap kubeconfig"
# вҶ’ OR: "eviction threshold met: memory.available"

# 5. Fix and restart
sudo systemctl restart kubelet
sudo systemctl enable kubelet

# 6. Back on control plane вҖ” verify recovery
kubectl get nodes
# worker-1   Ready   <none>   30d   v1.31.0  вҶҗ RECOVERED

# 7. If disk pressure вҖ” free space on the node
ssh worker-1
df -h                                   # Check disk usage
sudo crictl rmi --prune                 # Remove unused images
sudo journalctl --vacuum-size=100M      # Trim system logs
terminal вҖ” certificate expired recovery 
# Symptom: kubectl commands return "certificate has expired"
kubectl get nodes
# Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate has expired

# 1. SSH to the control plane node (kubectl won't work!)
ssh cp-1

# 2. Check which certs are expired
sudo kubeadm certs check-expiration
# CERTIFICATE                EXPIRES                  RESIDUAL TIME
# apiserver                  Jan 15, 2026 10:30 UTC   EXPIRED  вҶҗ !
# apiserver-kubelet-client   Jan 15, 2026 10:30 UTC   EXPIRED

# 3. Renew all certificates
sudo kubeadm certs renew all
# certificate apiserver renewed
# certificate apiserver-kubelet-client renewed
# ...
# Done renewing certificates.

# 4. Restart kubelet (restarts static pods with new certs)
sudo systemctl restart kubelet

# 5. Update admin kubeconfig (your kubectl config)
sudo cp /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf ~/.kube/config
sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) ~/.kube/config

# 6. Verify everything works
kubectl get nodes
# NAME   STATUS   ROLES           AGE   VERSION
# cp-1   Ready    control-plane   30d   v1.31.0  вҶҗ WORKING!

Debug Command Quick Reference Card

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ              WHICH COMMAND FOR WHICH ERROR?                          в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
  в”Ӯ  ERROR                в”Ӯ  FIRST COMMAND TO RUN                       в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
  в”Ӯ  CrashLoopBackOff     в”Ӯ  kubectl logs <pod> --previous             в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  ImagePullBackOff     в”Ӯ  kubectl describe pod <pod>                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Pending (pod)        в”Ӯ  kubectl describe pod <pod>                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Pending (PVC)        в”Ӯ  kubectl describe pvc <name>               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  NodeNotReady         в”Ӯ  ssh node вҶ’ systemctl status kubelet       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  FailedScheduling     в”Ӯ  kubectl describe pod вҶ’ check taints       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Forbidden            в”Ӯ  kubectl auth can-i <verb> <res> --as=SA  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  connection refused   в”Ӯ  kubectl get endpoints <svc>               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  cert expired         в”Ӯ  ssh cp вҶ’ kubeadm certs check-expiration   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  etcd unavailable     в”Ӯ  crictl ps | grep etcd                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  can't connect to API в”Ӯ  cat ~/.kube/config (check server URL)     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  OOMKilled            в”Ӯ  kubectl describe pod вҶ’ Last State          в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

рҹҡЁ Appendix C вҖ” Quick Debug Cheat Sheet

  • вң… CrashLoopBackOff вҶ’ logs --previous вҶ’ fix config/code
  • вң… ImagePullBackOff вҶ’ check image name, add imagePullSecrets
  • вң… Pending Pod вҶ’ describe pod вҶ’ resources, taints, affinity
  • вң… Pending PVC вҶ’ match PV accessMode + storageClassName + capacity
  • вң… NodeNotReady вҶ’ SSH, systemctl restart kubelet
  • вң… Forbidden вҶ’ auth can-i вҶ’ create Role + RoleBinding
  • вң… connection refused вҶ’ verify pod running + Service selector + NetworkPolicy
  • вң… cert expired вҶ’ kubeadm certs renew all
  • вң… etcd unavailable вҶ’ check etcd pod/certs, restore from backup
  • вң… unable to connect вҶ’ fix kubeconfig, check API server

рҹ“– Appendix D: Kubernetes Terminology Glossary

APPENDIX рҹ“Ӣ REFERENCE

110+ Kubernetes terms organized alphabetically across 22 letter sections (AвҖ“W) with CKA-relevant definitions, object hierarchy diagrams, and resource scope reference. Bookmark this glossary вҖ” if you see an unfamiliar term on the CKA exam, this is your quick-reference.

рҹ”Ө Jump to Letter

A B C D E F G H I J K L M N O P R S T U V W

Where These Terms Fit вҖ” Kubernetes Architecture Map

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ CONTROL PLANE в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                                                                       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ API Server в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Scheduler   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Controller  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    etcd    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Manager    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  (key-val  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ AuthN/AuthZв”Ӯ  в”Ӯ NodeSelector в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ ReplicaSet  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   store)   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Admission  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Affinity     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Deployment  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ RBAC       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Taints       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ DaemonSet   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Backup/    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Tolerations  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ StatefulSet в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Restore    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
           в”Ӯ
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ WORKER NODES в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ        в–ј                                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ kubelet  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ kube-proxy в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ           PODS                  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ (agent)  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ (network   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  rules)    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”ӮContainer в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Sidecar    в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Probes   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Services   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Image    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Init Ctr   в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ cgroups  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ iptables   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Volumes  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  emptyDir   в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Env/CM/  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ             в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Secret  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ             в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Container Runtime        в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ (containerd / CRI-O)     в”Ӯ                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ OCI images, CNI plugins  в”Ӯ                                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                        в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Exam Tip: You won't be asked definitions directly, but understanding these terms helps you parse question wording correctly. The CKA uses precise terminology вҖ” knowing the difference between cordon vs drain, or Role vs ClusterRole saves critical seconds.

Kubernetes Object Ownership Hierarchy

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                        DEPLOYMENT                                в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ          (Desired state: replicas, image, strategy)              в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
              в”Ӯ creates & manages            в”Ӯ creates on update
              в–ј                              в–ј
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ      в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ   ReplicaSet (v2)   в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ   ReplicaSet (v1)       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   replicas: 3        в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ   replicas: 0 (scaled   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   вң… current         в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ   down after rollout)   в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ      в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ creates & maintains desired count
          в–ј  в–ј  в–ј
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”ӮPod-0в”Ӯ в”ӮPod-1в”Ӯ в”ӮPod-2в”Ӯ    Each pod gets:
  в””в”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”ҳ    вҖў Unique IP
     в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ        вҖў Shared network namespace
     в–ј       в–ј       в–ј        вҖў ownerReference вҶ’ ReplicaSet
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җв”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җв”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ Ctr  в”Ӯв”Ӯ Ctr  в”Ӯв”Ӯ Ctr  в”Ӯ   Container = running image
  в”Ӯnginx в”Ӯв”Ӯnginx в”Ӯв”Ӯnginx в”Ӯ   (the actual process)
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳв””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳв””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  рҹ”‘ Ownership chain: Deployment вҶ’ ReplicaSet вҶ’ Pod вҶ’ Container
  рҹ—‘пёҸ Delete Deployment = cascading delete of ALL children
рҹ”Қ Resource Scope Quick Reference

Every K8s resource is either cluster-scoped or namespace-scoped. This determines where you can create it and how RBAC applies.

рҹҢҗ Cluster-Scoped (no namespace) рҹ“Ғ Namespace-Scoped
Node, PersistentVolume, StorageClassPod, Deployment, ReplicaSet, StatefulSet
Namespace (yes, it's cluster-scoped!)Service, Endpoints, Ingress
ClusterRole, ClusterRoleBindingRole, RoleBinding, ServiceAccount
CustomResourceDefinition (CRD)ConfigMap, Secret, PVC
PriorityClass, RuntimeClassJob, CronJob, DaemonSet, HPA
CSIDriver, CSINodeNetworkPolicy, PodDisruptionBudget
IngressClassLimitRange, ResourceQuota

вҡ пёҸ CKA Trap: Forgetting -n namespace

If you create a Role but forget -n <namespace>, it goes to default. Always specify namespace explicitly on the exam: kubectl create role myrole --verb=get --resource=pods -n target-ns

A

TermCKA?Definition
Admission Controllerрҹ“қPlugin that intercepts API requests after authentication/authorization but before persistence. Examples: LimitRanger, PodSecurity, MutatingWebhook. Can mutate or validate objects.
Affinityрҹ“қRules controlling pod placement. nodeAffinity targets specific nodes; podAffinity/podAntiAffinity attracts/repels pods relative to other pods. Softer than nodeSelector.
Annotationрҹ“қKey-value metadata attached to objects. Unlike labels, annotations are NOT used for selection. Used for: build info, config hints (nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target), tool metadata.
API Groupрҹ“қLogical grouping of API resources. Core group: "" (pods, services). Named groups: apps (deployments), batch (jobs), networking.k8s.io (networkpolicies), rbac.authorization.k8s.io.
API Serverрҹ“қCentral management hub of Kubernetes (kube-apiserver). All kubectl commands and internal components communicate through it. Runs on port 6443. Handles AuthN вҶ’ AuthZ вҶ’ Admission вҶ’ etcd persistence.

B

TermCKA?Definition
Base64рҹ“қEncoding (NOT encryption) used by Secrets. echo -n "password" | base64 to encode; echo "cGFzc3dvcmQ=" | base64 -d to decode. Anyone with RBAC access to the Secret can decode it.
BindingAPI object that ties a pod to a node. The Scheduler creates a Binding when it assigns a pod. Rarely used directly вҖ” understanding helps when debugging Pending pods.
Blue-Green DeploymentDeployment strategy: run two identical environments (blue=current, green=new), switch traffic instantly via Service selector change. Not built-in вҖ” implemented using label swaps on Services.

C

TermCKA?Definition
Calicoрҹ“қCNI plugin providing pod networking and NetworkPolicy enforcement. Most popular choice for CKA-style clusters. Supports BGP routing and IPIP tunneling.
CertificateSigningRequestрҹ“қAPI object to request a certificate signed by the cluster CA. Used for user authentication: create CSR вҶ’ approve вҶ’ extract cert. kubectl certificate approve <name>.
cgroupрҹ“қLinux kernel feature that limits, accounts for, and isolates resource usage (CPU, memory, I/O) of process groups. Kubernetes uses cgroups to enforce resources.limits.
CIDRрҹ“қClassless Inter-Domain Routing вҖ” notation for IP address ranges. Example: 10.244.0.0/16 = pod network, 10.96.0.0/12 = service network. Used in --pod-network-cidr and NetworkPolicy.
ClusterIPрҹ“қDefault Service type вҖ” accessible only within the cluster. Gets a virtual IP from the service CIDR. Access via <svc-name>.<namespace>.svc.cluster.local.
ClusterRoleрҹ“қCluster-wide permission set (no namespace). Used for cluster-scoped resources (nodes, PVs) or cross-namespace access. Bound via ClusterRoleBinding.
CNIрҹ“қContainer Network Interface вҖ” plugin standard for pod networking. Provides IP allocation and inter-pod connectivity. Examples: Calico, Flannel, Cilium, Weave Net.
ConfigMapрҹ“қStores non-sensitive configuration as key-value pairs. Injected into pods via: environment variables (envFrom), individual env vars (configMapKeyRef), or volume mounts (files).
Container Runtimeрҹ“қSoftware that actually runs containers. Kubernetes supports any CRI-compatible runtime. Standard: containerd. Alternative: CRI-O. Docker was removed in v1.24.
containerdрҹ“қIndustry-standard container runtime. Manages the complete container lifecycle: pull images, create containers, manage storage, networking. Default runtime for most K8s distributions.
Control Planeрҹ“қThe "brain" of the cluster running on master/control-plane nodes. Components: API Server, etcd, Scheduler, Controller Manager. Manages cluster state and makes scheduling decisions.
CoreDNSрҹ“қDNS server built into Kubernetes (runs as a Deployment in kube-system). Resolves service names: <svc>.<ns>.svc.cluster.local. Debug: kubectl run tmp --rm -it --image=busybox -- nslookup <svc>.
Cordonрҹ“қMark a node as unschedulable вҖ” no new pods will be placed, but existing pods remain. kubectl cordon <node>. First step before maintenance. Reverse: uncordon.
CRIContainer Runtime Interface вҖ” protocol between kubelet and container runtime. Replaced Docker-specific Dockershim in K8s v1.24.
CronJobрҹ“қRuns Jobs on a schedule (cron syntax). Example: schedule: "0 2 * * *" = daily at 2 AM. Creates Job objects which create Pods. Good for backups, report generation.
CSIContainer Storage Interface вҖ” standard for exposing storage systems to Kubernetes. Drivers: ebs.csi.aws.com, pd.csi.storage.gke.io, disk.csi.azure.com.
CRD (CustomResourceDefinition)Extend the Kubernetes API with your own resource types. Combined with a custom controller creates an Operator. Example: kind: Certificate (cert-manager).

D

TermCKA?Definition
DaemonSetрҹ“қEnsures exactly one pod per node (or per matching node). Used for: log collectors (Fluentd), monitoring agents (node-exporter), CNI plugins, kube-proxy. Pods survive kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets.
Deploymentрҹ“қManages ReplicaSets for declarative updates. Supports rolling updates, rollbacks, and scaling. The most common workload controller: kubectl create deployment anihpj-web --image=anihpj:v1.
Drainрҹ“қEvict all pods from a node for maintenance. Cordons the node first, then evicts pods. Flags: --ignore-daemonsets (required), --delete-emptydir-data. Managed pods are rescheduled elsewhere.

E

TermCKA?Definition
emptyDirрҹ“қTemporary volume created when a pod starts, deleted when pod is removed. Shared between containers in the same pod (sidecar pattern). volumes: [{name: shared, emptyDir: {}}].
Endpointрҹ“қIP:port combination backing a Service. When a Service selector matches pods, Kubernetes auto-creates Endpoints. Debug: kubectl get endpoints <svc> вҖ” if empty, selector/labels mismatch.
etcdрҹ“қDistributed key-value store holding all cluster state. Runs on control plane (port 2379). Backup: etcdctl snapshot save. Restore: etcdctl snapshot restore. Always requires TLS flags.

F

TermCKA?Definition
Finalizerрҹ“қMetadata key that prevents object deletion until cleanup is done. When you delete an object with finalizers, it enters Terminating state. The controller removes the finalizer after cleanup, then K8s completes deletion.
FlannelSimple CNI plugin using overlay networking (VXLAN). Easy to set up but does NOT support NetworkPolicies. Use Calico or Cilium instead for production.
FQDNрҹ“қFully Qualified Domain Name for Services: <svc>.<ns>.svc.cluster.local. Same-namespace pods can use just <svc>. Cross-namespace requires at least <svc>.<ns>.

G

TermCKA?Definition
Garbage CollectionAutomatic cleanup of orphaned objects. When a Deployment is deleted, its ReplicaSets and Pods are garbage collected via ownerReferences. Cascade modes: Foreground, Background, Orphan.
Gateway APINext-gen routing API (successor to Ingress). Resources: GatewayClass, Gateway, HTTPRoute. More expressive than Ingress вҖ” supports header-based routing, traffic splitting, and cross-namespace references.
GracePeriodрҹ“қTime Kubernetes waits for a pod to shut down gracefully (default: 30 seconds). Override: kubectl delete pod <name> --grace-period=0 --force. During this time, SIGTERM is sent, then SIGKILL after timeout.

H

TermCKA?Definition
HelmKubernetes package manager. Bundles YAML manifests into Charts. Install: helm install my-release chart-name. Not on CKA exam directly, but widely used in practice.
hostPathрҹ“қVolume that mounts a file/directory from the host node's filesystem into a pod. Used for: dev/test, static pods reading node configs. Not safe for production вҖ” data only on one node.
HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler)рҹ“қAutomatically scales pod replicas based on CPU/memory metrics (requires metrics-server). kubectl autoscale deploy anihpj-web --min=2 --max=10 --cpu-percent=70.

I

TermCKA?Definition
Ingressрҹ“қAPI object defining HTTP/HTTPS routing rules. Routes external traffic to Services based on host/path. Requires an Ingress Controller (nginx, traefik). Supports TLS termination.
Ingress Controllerрҹ“қComponent that implements Ingress rules. It's a pod running a reverse proxy (nginx, HAProxy). Without it, Ingress resources have no effect. Install: kubectl apply -f nginx-ingress.yaml.
Init Containerрҹ“қRuns before main containers. Must complete successfully before app containers start. Used for: waiting for dependencies, database migrations, config generation. Defined in spec.initContainers[].

J

TermCKA?Definition
Jobрҹ“қRun-to-completion workload. Pod runs once (or N times), then stops. restartPolicy: Never or OnFailure. Used for: backups, data processing, one-time migrations.
JSONPathрҹ“қQuery language for extracting data from JSON output. kubectl get pods -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'. Powerful for scripting and CKA exam output formatting.

K

TermCKA?Definition
kubeadmрҹ“қOfficial tool to bootstrap Kubernetes clusters. kubeadm init (control plane), kubeadm join (workers), kubeadm upgrade (version upgrades), kubeadm certs (certificate management). Core CKA tool.
kubectlрҹ“қCLI for communicating with the Kubernetes API server. Every CKA task uses it. Config: ~/.kube/config. Structure: kubectl [verb] [resource] [name] [flags].
kubeletрҹ“қAgent running on every node. Receives pod specs from API server, ensures containers are running and healthy. Manages probes, reports node status. Not a pod вҖ” it's a systemd service: systemctl status kubelet.
kube-proxyрҹ“қNetwork proxy on each node maintaining network rules (iptables/IPVS). Implements Service abstraction вҖ” routes traffic to pod endpoints. Runs as a DaemonSet in kube-system.
kube-schedulerрҹ“қControl plane component that assigns pods to nodes. Considers: resource requests, nodeSelector, affinity, taints/tolerations, PV topology. If it can't find a node вҶ’ pod stays Pending.
KustomizeKubernetes-native config management (built into kubectl apply -k). Overlays patches on base YAML without modifying originals. Alternative to Helm for simpler use cases.

L

TermCKA?Definition
Labelрҹ“қKey-value pair attached to objects for identification and selection. Used by selectors, services, deployments: app: anihpj. Filter: kubectl get pods -l app=anihpj.
LeaseLightweight object for leader election and node heartbeats. kubelet creates a Lease in kube-node-lease namespace вҖ” if it stops updating, node is marked NotReady.
LimitRangeрҹ“қSets default/min/max resource constraints per pod/container in a namespace. If a pod doesn't specify resources, LimitRange can inject defaults. Prevents runaway resource consumption.
Liveness Probeрҹ“қHealth check: "is the container alive?" If it fails, kubelet restarts the container. Types: httpGet, tcpSocket, exec. Configure initialDelaySeconds to avoid false kills on startup.

M

TermCKA?Definition
Manifestрҹ“қYAML or JSON file declaring the desired state of Kubernetes objects. Applied with kubectl apply -f manifest.yaml. The declarative approach вҖ” Kubernetes reconciles actual state to match.
Metrics Serverрҹ“қCluster add-on that collects CPU/memory usage from kubelets. Required for kubectl top nodes, kubectl top pods, and HPA autoscaling. Install: kubectl apply -f components.yaml from GitHub.
Multi-container Podрҹ“қPod with multiple containers sharing network and volumes. Patterns: Sidecar (helper alongside main), Ambassador (proxy), Adapter (transform output). Init containers run before app containers.

N

TermCKA?Definition
Namespaceрҹ“қVirtual cluster within a cluster. Provides resource isolation, RBAC boundaries, and resource quotas. Default namespaces: default, kube-system, kube-public, kube-node-lease.
NetworkPolicyрҹ“қFirewall rules for pod traffic. Controls ingress (incoming) and egress (outgoing) at L3/L4. Requires supporting CNI (Calico, Cilium). No policy = all traffic allowed. Empty podSelector = all pods.
Nodeрҹ“қA worker machine (VM or physical) in the cluster. Runs kubelet, kube-proxy, and container runtime. Control plane nodes also run API server, etcd, scheduler, controller-manager.
NodePortрҹ“қService type that opens a port (30000-32767) on every node's IP. External access via <nodeIP>:<nodePort>. Automatically creates a ClusterIP too. Good for dev/on-prem.

O

TermCKA?Definition
OCIOpen Container Initiative вҖ” industry standard for container image format and runtime specification. All K8s container images follow OCI spec.
OperatorPattern: custom controller + CRD that automates complex application lifecycle management. Examples: Prometheus Operator, cert-manager, PostgreSQL Operator. Extends Kubernetes capabilities.

P

TermCKA?Definition
PersistentVolume (PV)рҹ“қCluster-level storage resource provisioned by admin (or dynamically by StorageClass). Cluster-scoped вҖ” no namespace. Bound to PVCs. Reclaim policies: Retain, Delete, Recycle.
PersistentVolumeClaim (PVC)рҹ“қUser's request for storage. Namespace-scoped. Binds to a PV matching accessMode, capacity, and storageClass. Pod references PVC in volumes[].persistentVolumeClaim.claimName.
Podрҹ“қSmallest deployable unit вҖ” one or more containers sharing network namespace (same IP) and volumes. Usually one container per pod. Created by Deployments, not directly. kubectl run nginx --image=nginx.
PodDisruptionBudget (PDB)рҹ“қGuarantees minimum availability during voluntary disruptions (node drain, upgrades). minAvailable: 2 or maxUnavailable: 1. Can block kubectl drain if violated.
PodSecurityAdmissionрҹ“қBuilt-in admission controller enforcing Pod Security Standards at namespace level. Modes: enforce, audit, warn. Label: pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: restricted.
PodSecurityStandardрҹ“қThree profiles: Privileged (unrestricted), Baseline (sensible defaults), Restricted (hardened). Applied via PodSecurityAdmission labels on namespaces.
Probeрҹ“қHealth check mechanism. Three types: Liveness (restart if fail), Readiness (remove from service if fail), Startup (disable other probes until success). Methods: httpGet, tcpSocket, exec.

R

TermCKA?Definition
RBACрҹ“қRole-Based Access Control вҖ” who can do what. Four objects: Role (ns-scoped permissions), ClusterRole (cluster-wide), RoleBinding (links subjectвҶ’Role), ClusterRoleBinding.
Readiness Probeрҹ“қHealth check: "is the pod ready for traffic?" If it fails, pod is removed from Service endpoints (no restart). Critical for zero-downtime deployments during rolling updates.
ReplicaSetрҹ“қEnsures a specified number of pod replicas are running. Managed by Deployments вҖ” rarely created directly. kubectl get rs shows ReplicaSets behind a Deployment.
ResourceQuotaрҹ“қLimits total resource consumption per namespace (cpu, memory, pods count, PVCs). Prevents one team from consuming all cluster resources. Applied at namespace level.
RoleBindingрҹ“қBinds a Role (or ClusterRole) to subjects (users, groups, ServiceAccounts) within a single namespace. kubectl create rolebinding name --role=role --serviceaccount=ns:sa -n ns.
Rolling Updateрҹ“қDefault Deployment strategy: gradually replace old pods with new ones. Controlled by maxSurge and maxUnavailable. Zero-downtime when combined with readiness probes.

S

TermCKA?Definition
Secretрҹ“қStores sensitive data (base64-encoded, not encrypted by default). Types: Opaque (generic), kubernetes.io/tls (certs), kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson (registry auth). Protect with RBAC + encryption at rest.
SecurityContextрҹ“қSecurity settings at pod or container level. Controls: runAsUser, runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, capabilities, allowPrivilegeEscalation. CKA tests pod-level and container-level contexts.
Selectorрҹ“қLabel-based mechanism to identify a set of objects. Used by Services (spec.selector), Deployments (spec.selector.matchLabels), and NetworkPolicies (spec.podSelector).
Serviceрҹ“қStable network endpoint for a set of pods. Types: ClusterIP (internal), NodePort (external:30000+), LoadBalancer (cloud LB), ExternalName (DNS alias). Selector matches pod labels.
ServiceAccountрҹ“қIdentity for pods to authenticate to the API server. Every namespace has a default SA. Best practice: create dedicated SA with automountServiceAccountToken: false unless needed.
Sidecarрҹ“қAdditional container in a pod running alongside the main container. Pattern: log shippers, proxies (Envoy/Istio), config reloaders. Shares volumes and network with main container.
Static Podрҹ“қPod managed directly by kubelet (not API server). Defined in /etc/kubernetes/manifests/. Used for control plane components: API server, scheduler, controller-manager, etcd. Mirror pod appears in API.
StatefulSetрҹ“қManages stateful applications with stable identity: ordered pod names (db-0, db-1), persistent storage per pod, ordered deployment/scaling. Used for: databases, ZooKeeper, Kafka.
StorageClassрҹ“қDefines types of storage for dynamic provisioning. When a PVC references a StorageClass, K8s auto-creates the PV. Key fields: provisioner, volumeBindingMode, reclaimPolicy.

T

TermCKA?Definition
Taintрҹ“қMark on a node that repels pods unless they have a matching toleration. Effects: NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule, NoExecute. Control plane has default taint. kubectl taint nodes node key=val:NoSchedule.
Tolerationрҹ“қPod spec field allowing scheduling on tainted nodes. Must match taint key, value, and effect. spec.tolerations: [{key: "key", operator: "Equal", value: "val", effect: "NoSchedule"}].

U

TermCKA?Definition
Uncordonрҹ“қMake a cordoned node schedulable again. kubectl uncordon <node>. Final step after maintenance. Existing pods won't move back automatically вҖ” new pods may be scheduled there.
Upgrade (kubeadm)рҹ“қCKA-critical procedure: kubeadm upgrade plan вҶ’ kubeadm upgrade apply vX.Y.Z on control plane, then upgrade kubelet/kubectl on each node. Always upgrade one minor version at a time (e.g., 1.29вҶ’1.30).

V

TermCKA?Definition
Volumeрҹ“қStorage attached to a pod. Types: emptyDir (temp), hostPath (node fs), persistentVolumeClaim (PVC), configMap, secret, projected. Defined in spec.volumes[], mounted via volumeMounts[].
VolumeMountрҹ“қHow a volume is mounted inside a container. Specifies: name (matches volume name), mountPath (path in container), optional readOnly and subPath.

W

TermCKA?Definition
WebhookHTTP callback for admission control. MutatingAdmissionWebhook can modify objects; ValidatingAdmissionWebhook can accept/reject. Used by tools like cert-manager, OPA/Gatekeeper.
Worker Nodeрҹ“қNode that runs application workloads (pods). Contains kubelet, kube-proxy, and container runtime. Joined to cluster via kubeadm join. Managed from control plane.

вҡ пёҸ Frequently Confused Term Pairs

  • Label vs Annotation вҖ” Labels are for selection (selectors use them); Annotations are metadata only
  • Cordon vs Drain вҖ” Cordon stops new pods; Drain evicts existing pods too
  • Role vs ClusterRole вҖ” Role is namespace-scoped; ClusterRole is cluster-wide
  • PV vs PVC вҖ” PV is the actual storage (admin); PVC is the request (developer)
  • Liveness vs Readiness вҖ” Liveness restarts container; Readiness removes from service
  • DaemonSet vs Deployment вҖ” DaemonSet: one pod per node; Deployment: specified replica count
  • ConfigMap vs Secret вҖ” ConfigMap: plain text config; Secret: base64 sensitive data
  • Taint vs Toleration вҖ” Taint is on nodes (repels); Toleration is on pods (allows)
  • NodePort vs LoadBalancer вҖ” NodePort: nodeIP:port; LoadBalancer: cloud-provisioned external IP

рҹ“– Appendix D вҖ” Glossary Quick Stats

  • вң… 110+ terms across 22 letter sections (AвҖ“W) covering all CKA exam domains
  • вң… рҹ“қ = CKA-relevant вҖ” terms you may encounter on the exam
  • вң… Object hierarchy diagram вҖ” Deployment вҶ’ ReplicaSet вҶ’ Pod вҶ’ Container ownership chain
  • вң… Resource scope reference вҖ” cluster-scoped vs namespace-scoped at a glance
  • вң… Definitions include commands, examples, and cross-references
  • вң… Confused pairs вҖ” 9 most commonly mixed-up term pairs clarified

рҹҗҚ Appendix E: Complete anihpj Kubernetes Manifests

APPENDIX рҹ“Ӣ REFERENCE

Production-ready YAML manifests for the complete anihpj Django/jobpost application вҖ” E.1 through E.12. Each manifest follows CKA best practices: resource limits, security contexts, labels, and health checks.

anihpj Application вҖ” Complete Kubernetes Architecture

                          в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                INTERNET в”Җв”Ӯ   Ingress (NGINX) в”Ӯ
                          в”Ӯ  anihpj.local     в”Ӯ
                          в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                                   в”Ӯ  :80/:443
                          в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                          в”Ӯ  Django Service   в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ NetworkPolicy в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                          в”Ӯ  (ClusterIP)      в”Ӯ       (restricts traffic)  в”Ӯ
                          в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                             в”Ӯ
                  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                      в”Ӯ
                  в–ј                в–ј                 в–ј                      в”Ӯ
          в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ               в”Ӯ
          в”Ӯ Django Pod-0 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ Django Pod-1 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ Django Pod-N в”Ӯ  в—„в”Җв”Җ HPA      в”Ӯ
          в”Ӯ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ  (2-10 pods)  в”Ӯ
          в”Ӯ в”ӮContainer в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  Deployment  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  Deployment  в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ
          в”Ӯ в”Ӯanihpj:v1 в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  replicas=3  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  replicas=3  в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ
          в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ               в”Ӯ
          в”Ӯ в”ӮConfigMap в”Ӯ в”Ӯ                                                  в”Ӯ
          в”Ӯ в”Ӯ DB_HOST  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ    ServiceAccount: anihpj-web-sa                в”Ӯ
          в”Ӯ в”Ӯ DB_NAME  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ    PDB: minAvailable=2                          в”Ӯ
          в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө в”Ӯ                                                  в”Ӯ
          в”Ӯ в”Ӯ Secret   в”Ӯ в”Ӯ                                                  в”Ӯ
          в”Ӯ в”Ӯ DB_PASS  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ                                                  в”Ӯ
          в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ                                                  в”Ӯ
          в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                                  в”Ӯ
                 в”Ӯ                                                          в”Ӯ
          в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                                               в”Ӯ
          в”Ӯ  PostgreSQL Svc в”Ӯ  в—„в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
          в”Ӯ  (ClusterIP)    в”Ӯ
          в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                 в”Ӯ
          в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
          в”Ӯ PostgreSQL       в”Ӯ   StatefulSet (replicas=1)
          в”Ӯ StatefulSet-0    в”Ӯ   volumeClaimTemplates вҶ’ PVC
          в”Ӯ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ
          в”Ӯ в”Ӯ pgdata volumeв”Ӯ в”Ӯ   StorageClass: anihpj-storage
          в”Ӯ в”Ӯ (PVC 5Gi)    в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   PersistentVolume: 10Gi
          в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ
          в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ NAMESPACE: anihpj в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ  RBAC: Role (anihpj-role) + RoleBinding (anihpj-web-sa)          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  CronJob: db-backup (daily at 2 AM)                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Job: db-migrate (one-time schema migration)                     в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

How to Use These Manifests: Apply in order (E.1вҶ’E.12). Each manifest depends on the previous ones. Or apply all at once: kubectl apply -f anihpj/ (place all YAML in one directory).

E.1 Namespace

Why First

Every other manifest references namespace: anihpj. Create this before anything else.

01-namespace.yaml 
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: anihpj
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: anihpj-platform
    environment: production
  annotations:
    description: "anihpj Django/jobpost application namespace"
вҡЎ Imperative equivalent: kubectl create namespace anihpj

E.2 ConfigMap

Purpose

Non-sensitive configuration вҖ” database host, port, app settings. Referenced by Django Deployment pods via envFrom.

02-configmap.yaml 
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: anihpj-config
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/component: config
data:
  # Database connection (non-sensitive)
  DB_HOST: "anihpj-postgres"
  DB_PORT: "5432"
  DB_NAME: "anihpj_db"

  # Django settings
  DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: "anihpj.settings"
  DJANGO_ALLOWED_HOSTS: "*"
  DJANGO_DEBUG: "False"

  # Application
  APP_NAME: "anihpj-jobpost"
  LOG_LEVEL: "INFO"
вҡЎ Imperative equivalent: kubectl create configmap anihpj-config --from-literal=DB_HOST=anihpj-postgres --from-literal=DB_PORT=5432 --from-literal=DB_NAME=anihpj_db -n anihpj

E.3 Secret

вҡ пёҸ Secrets are base64-encoded, NOT encrypted

  • Anyone with RBAC access to Secrets can decode them: echo <value> | base64 -d
  • Enable encryption at rest in production (EncryptionConfiguration)
  • Use stringData (plain text, auto-encoded) instead of data (pre-encoded) for readability
03-secret.yaml 
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: anihpj-secrets
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/component: secrets
type: Opaque
stringData:
  # Using stringData вҖ” Kubernetes auto-encodes to base64
  DB_PASSWORD: "your-strong-password-here"
  DJANGO_SECRET_KEY: "your-django-secret-key-here"
  POSTGRES_PASSWORD: "your-strong-password-here"
вҡЎ Imperative equivalent: kubectl create secret generic anihpj-secrets --from-literal=DB_PASSWORD=your-strong-password-here --from-literal=DJANGO_SECRET_KEY=your-django-secret-key-here -n anihpj

E.4 StorageClass & PersistentVolumeClaim

Storage Flow

StorageClass defines how storage is provisioned вҶ’ PVC requests storage вҶ’ StatefulSet (E.5) uses the PVC via volumeClaimTemplates.

Storage Binding Flow

  StorageClass                PVC                     StatefulSet
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ anihpj-      в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ pgdata-claim в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ postgres-0 Pod     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ storage      в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ 5Gi RWO      в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ mountPath: /data   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ provisioner: в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ storageClass в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ volumeClaimTemplatesв”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  hostPath    в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  =anihpj-    в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  uses this class   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ (dev/test)   в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ   storage    в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ                    в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
04a-storageclass.yaml 
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: anihpj-storage
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/component: storage
provisioner: docker.io/hostpath       # Dev/test only
reclaimPolicy: Retain                 # Keep data after PVC deletion
volumeBindingMode: WaitForFirstConsumer  # Bind when pod is scheduled
allowVolumeExpansion: true
04b-pvc.yaml 
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pgdata-claim
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/component: database
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce              # Single node read-write
  storageClassName: anihpj-storage
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 5Gi

CKA Tip: For the exam, remember the three access modes: ReadWriteOnce (RWO), ReadOnlyMany (ROX), ReadWriteMany (RWX). Databases almost always use RWO.

E.5 PostgreSQL StatefulSet & Service

Why StatefulSet?

Databases need: stable network identity (anihpj-postgres-0), persistent storage per pod, ordered deployment. A Deployment would lose data on pod reschedule.

05a-postgres-statefulset.yaml 
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-postgres
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/component: database
spec:
  serviceName: anihpj-postgres    # Headless service name
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-postgres
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-postgres
        app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
        app.kubernetes.io/component: database
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: postgres
          image: postgres:15-alpine
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
              name: postgres
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              valueFrom:
                configMapKeyRef:
                  name: anihpj-config
                  key: DB_NAME
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: anihpj-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: PGDATA
              value: /var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
          volumeMounts:
            - name: pgdata
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 256Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 512Mi
          livenessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["pg_isready", "-U", "postgres"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["pg_isready", "-U", "postgres"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 5
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: pgdata
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        storageClassName: anihpj-storage
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 5Gi
05b-postgres-service.yaml 
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-postgres
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/component: database
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  clusterIP: None                 # Headless service for StatefulSet
  selector:
    app: anihpj-postgres
  ports:
    - port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
      name: postgres

вҡ пёҸ StatefulSet Gotchas

  • clusterIP: None makes a headless service вҖ” required for StatefulSet DNS (anihpj-postgres-0.anihpj-postgres.anihpj.svc.cluster.local)
  • serviceName in StatefulSet must match the headless service name
  • PVCs from volumeClaimTemplates are NOT deleted on StatefulSet deletion вҖ” delete manually if needed

E.6 Django Deployment & Service

Core Workload

This is the main anihpj application. Uses Deployment (stateless) with ConfigMap/Secret injection, resource limits, probes, and security context.

06a-django-deployment.yaml 
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-web
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/component: web
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-web
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1
      maxUnavailable: 0           # Zero-downtime deployments
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-web
        app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
        app.kubernetes.io/component: web
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: anihpj-web-sa
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      containers:
        - name: django
          image: anihpj/jobpost:v1.0.0
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8000
              name: http
          envFrom:
            - configMapRef:
                name: anihpj-config
            - secretRef:
                name: anihpj-secrets
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 100m
              memory: 128Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 512Mi
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /health/
              port: 8000
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 20
            failureThreshold: 3
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /ready/
              port: 8000
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
            capabilities:
              drop: ["ALL"]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: tmp
              mountPath: /tmp
      volumes:
        - name: tmp
          emptyDir: {}            # Writable /tmp for Django
06b-django-service.yaml 
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-web
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/component: web
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: anihpj-web
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 8000
      protocol: TCP
      name: http

CKA Pattern: maxUnavailable: 0 + readiness probe = zero-downtime rolling update. Old pods stay in the Service until new pods pass readiness. This is a common exam scenario.

E.7 Ingress

07-ingress.yaml 
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: anihpj-ingress
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/component: networking
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect: "false"
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  rules:
    - host: anihpj.local
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: anihpj-web
                port:
                  number: 80
          - path: /api
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: anihpj-web
                port:
                  number: 80

Ingress Traffic Flow

  Client Request                 Ingress Controller          Backend Pods
  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ                   в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ          в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
  GET anihpj.local/      в”Җв”Җв–¶   nginx pod (DaemonSet)  в”Җв”Җв–¶  anihpj-web-xxx
  GET anihpj.local/api   в”Җв”Җв–¶   matches path rules     в”Җв”Җв–¶  anihpj-web-yyy
  GET other.local/       в”Җв”Җв–¶   404 (no matching rule)

вҡ пёҸ Ingress Gotchas

  • Ingress resource alone does nothing вҖ” you need an Ingress Controller installed (nginx, traefik)
  • ingressClassName replaced the deprecated kubernetes.io/ingress.class annotation in v1.19+
  • pathType: Prefix matches /api and /api/jobs; Exact matches only /api

E.8 NetworkPolicy

вҡ пёҸ Prerequisite

NetworkPolicies require a CNI that supports them (Calico, Cilium). Flannel does NOT enforce network policies.

08-networkpolicy.yaml 
# Default deny all ingress in namespace
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny-ingress
  namespace: anihpj
spec:
  podSelector: {}                 # All pods in namespace
  policyTypes:
    - Ingress
---
# Allow Django to receive traffic from Ingress
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-web-ingress
  namespace: anihpj
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-web
  policyTypes:
    - Ingress
  ingress:
    - from:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              kubernetes.io/metadata.name: ingress-nginx
      ports:
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 8000
---
# Allow Django вҶ’ PostgreSQL traffic only
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-postgres-from-web
  namespace: anihpj
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-postgres
  policyTypes:
    - Ingress
  ingress:
    - from:
        - podSelector:
            matchLabels:
              app: anihpj-web
      ports:
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 5432

Network Policy Effect вҖ” What's Allowed & What's Blocked

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җ ingress-nginx namespace в”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ anihpj namespace в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ         в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Ingress         в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җ вң… в”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ anihpj-web   в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Controller      в”Ӯ  :8000  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ (Django)     в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ         в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ вң… :5432                 в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в”Ӯ         в–ј                          в”Ӯ
                                        в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җ any other namespace в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ         в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ anihpj-      в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                            в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ postgres     в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ      в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Random Pod      в”Ӯв”Җв”Җ вқҢ в”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”ҖX   в”Ӯ                                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ      в”Ӯ        в”Ӯ  вқҢ All other ingress denied       в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

E.9 RBAC (Role & RoleBinding)

09a-serviceaccount.yaml 
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: anihpj-web-sa
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/component: rbac
automountServiceAccountToken: false   # Best practice: opt-in only
09b-role.yaml 
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: anihpj-role
  namespace: anihpj
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["configmaps", "secrets"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["pods"]
    verbs: ["get", "list"]
09c-rolebinding.yaml 
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: anihpj-web-binding
  namespace: anihpj
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: anihpj-web-sa
    namespace: anihpj
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: anihpj-role
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
вҡЎ Imperative equivalents: kubectl create sa anihpj-web-sa -n anihpj kubectl create role anihpj-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=configmaps,secrets -n anihpj kubectl create rolebinding anihpj-web-binding --role=anihpj-role --serviceaccount=anihpj:anihpj-web-sa -n anihpj

E.10 PodDisruptionBudget

Purpose

Guarantees at least 2 Django pods are always running during voluntary disruptions (node drain, cluster upgrades).

10-pdb.yaml 
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: anihpj-web-pdb
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/component: availability
spec:
  minAvailable: 2                 # Always keep 2+ pods running
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-web

CKA Tip: Use minAvailable when you know the minimum OR maxUnavailable when you want flexibility. With 3 replicas and minAvailable: 2, only 1 pod can be disrupted at a time.

E.11 Horizontal Pod Autoscaler

11-hpa.yaml 
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: anihpj-web-hpa
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/component: autoscaling
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: anihpj-web
  minReplicas: 2
  maxReplicas: 10
  metrics:
    - type: Resource
      resource:
        name: cpu
        target:
          type: Utilization
          averageUtilization: 70
    - type: Resource
      resource:
        name: memory
        target:
          type: Utilization
          averageUtilization: 80
  behavior:
    scaleDown:
      stabilizationWindowSeconds: 300   # Wait 5 min before scaling down
      policies:
        - type: Pods
          value: 1
          periodSeconds: 60             # Remove max 1 pod per minute

HPA Scaling Behavior

  CPU Load     Replicas     Action
  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ     в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ     в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
    20%          2          Minimum (idle)
    50%          2          Still within threshold
    70%          3          Scale up triggered (target: 70%)
    90%          5          Aggressive scale up
   100%         10          Maximum reached
    60%         10          Stabilization window (5 min wait)
    40%          9          Scale down: -1 pod/min
    20%          2          Back to minimum
вҡЎ Imperative equivalent: kubectl autoscale deployment anihpj-web --min=2 --max=10 --cpu-percent=70 -n anihpj

E.12 Jobs & CronJobs

Two Job Types

A one-time Job for database migration, and a CronJob for daily database backups.

12a-db-migrate-job.yaml 
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: anihpj-db-migrate
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/component: migration
spec:
  backoffLimit: 3                 # Retry up to 3 times
  activeDeadlineSeconds: 300      # Timeout after 5 minutes
  template:
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
        - name: migrate
          image: anihpj/jobpost:v1.0.0
          command: ["python", "manage.py", "migrate", "--noinput"]
          envFrom:
            - configMapRef:
                name: anihpj-config
            - secretRef:
                name: anihpj-secrets
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 100m
              memory: 128Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 256Mi
12b-db-backup-cronjob.yaml 
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: anihpj-db-backup
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: anihpj
    app.kubernetes.io/component: backup
spec:
  schedule: "0 2 * * *"          # Daily at 2:00 AM
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid       # Don't overlap backup runs
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 3
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      backoffLimit: 2
      template:
        spec:
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
          containers:
            - name: backup
              image: postgres:15-alpine
              command:
                - /bin/sh
                - -c
                - |
                  pg_dump -h $DB_HOST -U postgres -d $DB_NAME \
                    -Fc -f /backup/anihpj-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).dump
              envFrom:
                - configMapRef:
                    name: anihpj-config
              env:
                - name: PGPASSWORD
                  valueFrom:
                    secretKeyRef:
                      name: anihpj-secrets
                      key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              resources:
                requests:
                  cpu: 100m
                  memory: 128Mi
                limits:
                  cpu: 250m
                  memory: 256Mi

рҹҗҚ Appendix E вҖ” Manifest Deployment Order

  • Step 1: kubectl apply -f 01-namespace.yaml вҖ” Create namespace first
  • Step 2: kubectl apply -f 02-configmap.yaml -f 03-secret.yaml вҖ” Config & secrets
  • Step 3: kubectl apply -f 04a-storageclass.yaml -f 04b-pvc.yaml вҖ” Storage
  • Step 4: kubectl apply -f 05a-postgres-statefulset.yaml -f 05b-postgres-service.yaml вҖ” Database
  • Step 5: kubectl apply -f 12a-db-migrate-job.yaml вҖ” Run migrations
  • Step 6: kubectl apply -f 06a-django-deployment.yaml -f 06b-django-service.yaml вҖ” App
  • Step 7: kubectl apply -f 07-ingress.yaml вҖ” External access
  • Step 8: Apply remaining: NetworkPolicy, RBAC, PDB, HPA, CronJob
  • вң… All at once: kubectl apply -f anihpj/ --recursive
вң… Post-Deployment Verification Checklist

After applying all manifests, run these commands to verify everything is healthy:

verify-deployment.sh 
# 1. Check all pods are Running
kubectl get pods -n anihpj -o wide

# 2. Verify Services have endpoints
kubectl get svc -n anihpj
kubectl get endpoints -n anihpj

# 3. Check PVC is Bound
kubectl get pvc -n anihpj

# 4. Verify Ingress has an address
kubectl get ingress -n anihpj

# 5. Check HPA is reading metrics
kubectl get hpa -n anihpj

# 6. Verify NetworkPolicy is applied
kubectl get networkpolicy -n anihpj

# 7. Test DNS resolution
kubectl run tmp --rm -it --image=busybox -n anihpj --restart=Never \
  -- nslookup anihpj-django-service

# 8. Test application endpoint
kubectl run tmp --rm -it --image=curlimages/curl -n anihpj --restart=Never \
  -- curl -s http://anihpj-django-service:8000/health/

# 9. Check RBAC is working
kubectl auth can-i get pods -n anihpj --as=system:serviceaccount:anihpj:anihpj-web-sa

# 10. View recent events for issues
kubectl get events -n anihpj --sort-by=.lastTimestamp | tail -20

Pod Not Running? вҖ” Troubleshooting Decision Flow

  Pod Status?
  в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ Pending в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә kubectl describe pod <name> -n anihpj
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                   Look at Events section:
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ "Insufficient cpu/memory" в”Җв”Җв–ә Increase node resources or reduce requests
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ "no nodes match selector"  в”Җв”Җв–ә Check nodeSelector/affinity/taints
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ "PVC not bound"           в”Җв”Җв–ә Check StorageClass exists, PV available
  в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ CrashLoopBackOff в”Җв–ә kubectl logs <pod> -n anihpj --previous
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                   (--previous shows logs from crashed container)
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ "Connection refused DB" в”Җв”Җв–ә PostgreSQL not ready yet (check ordering)
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ "Permission denied"     в”Җв”Җв–ә SecurityContext too restrictive
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ "Module not found"      в”Җв”Җв–ә Wrong image or missing dependencies
  в”Ӯ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ ImagePullBackOff в”Җв”Җв–ә kubectl describe pod <name> -n anihpj
  в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ "repository not found"  в”Җв”Җв–ә Wrong image name/tag
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ "unauthorized"          в”Җв”Җв–ә Missing imagePullSecrets
  в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җ Running but в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә kubectl logs <pod> -n anihpj -f
      not working         kubectl exec -it <pod> -n anihpj -- /bin/sh
                          Check: env vars, mounted configs, network connectivity

вҡ пёҸ Common Manifest Mistakes on CKA

  • Wrong namespace вҖ” Forgetting namespace: anihpj in metadata, so object lands in default
  • Label mismatch вҖ” Service selector doesn't match Deployment's template.metadata.labels вҶ’ 0 endpoints
  • Port mismatch вҖ” Service targetPort doesn't match container containerPort вҶ’ connection refused
  • Secret key typo вҖ” secretKeyRef.key doesn't match actual Secret data key вҶ’ pod fails to start
  • Missing resources вҖ” No resources.requests means scheduler can't account for pod needs вҶ’ OOM kills
  • PVC accessMode вҖ” Using ReadWriteMany with a StorageClass that only supports ReadWriteOnce вҶ’ PVC stays Pending

рҹҺ“ Appendix F: CKA Exam Preparation Guide

APPENDIX рҹ“Ӣ REFERENCE

Exam format, domain weights, must-know tips, time management, practice labs, exam day checklist. Everything you need to pass the CKA on your first attempt.

F.1 Exam Format and Rules

CKA Exam Environment Overview

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ CKA EXAM в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   Duration:    2 hours                                              в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   Questions:   15вҖ“20 performance-based tasks                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   Pass Score:  66%                                                  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   Format:      Hands-on (real clusters, real kubectl)               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   Retake:      1 free retake included                               в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   Validity:    2 years (was 3 years before 2023)                    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   Proctored:   Online via PSI Secure Browser                        в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                                                     в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  YOUR SCREEN DURING EXAM                                в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Question Panel   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Terminal                    в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ $ kubectl ...               в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Task description в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                             в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Requirements     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ One or more pre-configured  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Context switch   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ cluster contexts available  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                             в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Built-in Browser Tab (allowed docs only)         в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ       в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
AspectDetails
Kubernetes VersionLatest stable (currently v1.31 вҖ” always check CNCF site before exam)
Number of ClustersMultiple pre-configured clusters. Each question specifies which context to use: kubectl config use-context <name>
Root AccessYou get sudo on nodes when needed (e.g., for static pods, kubelet troubleshooting)
ClipboardCopy/paste works within the exam terminal. Use Ctrl+Shift+C/V in the terminal
NotepadBuilt-in notepad provided вҖ” use it to track flagged questions
IDE/Editorvim, nano, and emacs available. Set export EDITOR=vim if needed

вң… Allowed Documentation During Exam

You may open ONE extra browser tab with these domains only:

  • https://kubernetes.io/docs/ вҖ” Official documentation
  • https://kubernetes.io/blog/ вҖ” Official blog
  • https://helm.sh/docs/ вҖ” Helm documentation (since CKA 2024)

NOT allowed: GitHub, Stack Overflow, any other website, personal notes, second monitor.

F.2 CKA Exam Domain Weights

CKA Domain Weight Distribution (2024/2025)

  Domain                               Weight    Visual
  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
  рҹ”§ Troubleshooting                    30%     в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ
  рҹҸ—пёҸ Cluster Architecture & Setup       25%     в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ
  рҹҢҗ Services & Networking              20%     в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ
  рҹ“Ұ Workloads & Scheduling             15%     в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ
  рҹ’ҫ Storage                             10%     в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ

                              Pass threshold: 66% в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶ в”ғ
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ғв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  0%                   33%                  66%   в”ғ      100%
  в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в– в–Ўв–Ўв–Ўв–Ўв–Ўв–Ўв–Ўв–Ўв–Ўв–Ўв–Ўв–Ў
  в—Җв”Җв”Җ FAIL zone в”Җв”Җв–¶                   в—Җв”Җв”Җ PASS в”Җв–¶
%DomainKey Topics to MasterChapters
30% рҹ”§ Troubleshooting Application failure, pod debugging, cluster component failure, node issues, networking issues. Highest weight вҖ” practice logs, events, describe. Ch 27вҖ“31
25% рҹҸ—пёҸ Cluster Architecture RBAC, kubeadm upgrades, etcd backup/restore, HA setup, node management (cordon/drain/uncordon). Ch 1вҖ“6, 22вҖ“26
20% рҹҢҗ Services & Networking Service types (ClusterIP, NodePort, LB), Ingress, NetworkPolicy, DNS resolution, CNI. Ch 16вҖ“21
15% рҹ“Ұ Workloads & Scheduling Deployments, DaemonSets, resource limits, scheduling (nodeSelector, affinity, taints), scaling. Ch 7вҖ“12
10% рҹ’ҫ Storage PV/PVC, StorageClass, volume types, accessModes, dynamic provisioning. Ch 13вҖ“15

Strategy: Troubleshooting (30%) + Cluster Architecture (25%) = 55% of the exam. Master these two domains first вҖ” they alone can nearly pass you.

F.3 Tips, Tricks & Time Management

вҸұпёҸ Time Management Strategy (120 Minutes)

With 15вҖ“20 questions in 2 hours, you have ~6вҖ“8 minutes per question. Not all questions are equal weight.

Recommended Time Allocation

  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ FIRST PASS (90 min) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ SECOND PASS (25 min) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җ REVIEW в”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ                                  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  (5 min)  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  Answer ALL questions you can    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Return to flagged/skipped    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Verify   в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  do quickly (<5 min each)       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  questions                    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  contexts в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Check    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  FLAG and SKIP hard ones         в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Spend remaining time on      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  answers  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ  (come back in pass 2)           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  highest-weight questions     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                                  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   0 min                         90 min                             115 min          120 min
вҡЎ First Thing вҖ” Set Up Your Aliases

Paste these at the exam terminal start. They save hundreds of keystrokes.

~/.bashrc exam setup 
# Paste at start of exam (takes 30 seconds, saves 10+ minutes)
alias k=kubectl
alias kgp='kubectl get pods'
alias kgs='kubectl get svc'
alias kgn='kubectl get nodes'
alias kaf='kubectl apply -f'
alias kdel='kubectl delete'
alias kd='kubectl describe'
