alias kl='kubectl logs'
alias kx='kubectl exec -it'

# Auto-complete
source <(kubectl completion bash)
complete -o default -F __start_kubectl k

# Default editor
export EDITOR=vim
export KUBE_EDITOR=vim

# Quick dry-run shortcut
export do="--dry-run=client -o yaml"

# Usage: k run nginx --image=nginx $do > pod.yaml
#TipDetails
1Read context switchEvery question starts with kubectl config use-context <name>. DO NOT SKIP THIS вҖ” wrong context = wrong cluster = zero points.
2Use imperative commandskubectl create deploy, kubectl run, kubectl expose are 5x faster than writing YAML from scratch.
3Generate YAML skeletonskubectl run nginx --image=nginx --dry-run=client -o yaml > pod.yaml вҖ” then edit the YAML.
4Use kubectl explainkubectl explain pod.spec.containers.resources вҖ” built-in docs when you forget field names.
5Bookmark docs pagesBefore exam, bookmark: cheat sheet, kubectl reference, API reference, tasks page.
6Don't get stuckIf a 4% question takes >10 min, flag it and move on. An 8% question waiting is worth more.
7Verify your workAfter creating a resource: kubectl get, kubectl describe, check events. Confirm it's in the right namespace.
8Know vim basicsi (insert), Esc (normal), :wq (save+quit), dd (delete line), /search (find), :set number (line numbers).

вҡ пёҸ Common Exam Mistakes

  • Wrong namespace: Always check -n <namespace> вҖ” many questions use non-default namespaces
  • Wrong context: Run the context switch command at the start of EVERY question
  • YAML indentation: Spaces only (no tabs). A single wrong indent breaks everything
  • Forgetting to save: In vim, :wq вҖ” many have lost work by exiting without saving
  • Over-engineering: The exam wants minimal correct answers, not production-grade YAML

рҹ”– Must-Bookmark kubernetes.io Pages (Before Exam Day)

Open these before the exam starts and bookmark them in your allowed browser tab:

  • kubectl Cheat Sheet вҖ” kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubectl/cheatsheet/
  • kubectl Reference вҖ” kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubectl/kubectl-commands
  • API Reference (v1.31) вҖ” kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubernetes-api/
  • Tasks Index вҖ” kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/ (copy-paste YAML snippets)
  • kubeadm upgrade вҖ” kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/kubeadm/kubeadm-upgrade/
  • etcd backup/restore вҖ” kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/configure-upgrade-etcd/
  • NetworkPolicy вҖ” kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/network-policies/
  • RBAC вҖ” kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/rbac/
рҹ“қ Vim Survival Guide for the CKA Exam

You will use vim on the exam. Master these 15 commands:

KeyActionWhen to Use
iEnter insert modeStart typing/editing text
EscReturn to normal modeStop editing, run commands
:wqSave and quitDone editing вҖ” save file
:q!Quit without savingMessed up вҖ” discard all changes
ddDelete entire lineRemove unwanted YAML lines
5ddDelete 5 linesRemove a block of YAML
yy / pCopy line / Paste belowDuplicate a YAML block
uUndo last changeRevert a mistake
/textSearch forwardFind a field in large YAML
nNext search matchJump to next occurrence
GGo to end of fileJump to bottom of file
ggGo to start of fileJump to top of file
:set numberShow line numbersDebug YAML indentation errors
:set pastePaste mode (no auto-indent)Before pasting YAML from docs
Shift+V вҶ’ select вҶ’ dVisual line deleteSelect and delete multiple lines
вҡЎ Essential kubectl Patterns You MUST Know

These patterns cover ~80% of CKA tasks. Practice until they're muscle memory.

essential-exam-patterns.sh 
# Generate YAML skeleton (most-used pattern)
k run nginx --image=nginx $do > pod.yaml
k create deploy web --image=nginx --replicas=3 $do > deploy.yaml
k expose deploy web --port=80 --target-port=8080 $do > svc.yaml

# Context switching (FIRST thing every question)
k config use-context <context-name>
k config get-contexts    # verify current

# Quick troubleshooting chain
k get pods -A            # all namespaces overview
k describe pod <name>   # events + conditions
k logs <pod> -c <ctr>   # container logs
k logs <pod> --previous  # crashed container logs
k exec -it <pod> -- sh  # shell into container

# RBAC creation chain
k create sa mysa -n <ns>
k create role myrole --verb=get,list --resource=pods -n <ns>
k create rolebinding myrb --role=myrole --serviceaccount=<ns>:mysa -n <ns>
k auth can-i get pods --as=system:serviceaccount:<ns>:mysa -n <ns>

# Node maintenance
k cordon <node>
k drain <node> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data
k uncordon <node>

# etcd backup (know this exact command)
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot save /tmp/backup.db \
  --endpoints=https://127.0.0.1:2379 \
  --cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt \
  --cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt \
  --key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key

F.4 Practice Lab Recommendations

PlatformCostCKA Sim?Notes
killer.shFree (2 sessions with exam purchase)вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗMost realistic. Included with CKA exam purchase. Harder than real exam. Use both sessions in final week.
KodeKloud CKA~$15-25/moвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗStructured course with hands-on labs. Great for beginners. Mock exams included. Lightning labs for speed practice.
KillercodaFreeвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗFree browser-based K8s scenarios. Good for daily 15-min practice. By the makers of killer.sh.
Kubernetes the Hard WayFreeвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗKelsey Hightower's manual cluster setup. Deepens understanding but NOT exam format. Do once for learning.
Play with KubernetesFreeвӯҗвӯҗ4-hour free browser clusters. Good for quick experiments when you don't have local setup.
Local (kubeadm/kind/minikube)FreeвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗPractice kubeadm installs, upgrades, etcd backup locally. Closest to exam for cluster-admin tasks.

Recommended Study Path: KodeKloud (learn concepts + labs) вҶ’ Killercoda (daily practice) вҶ’ killer.sh (final 2 sessions before exam). Practice until you can complete killer.sh with 20+ minutes to spare.

F.5 Exam Day Checklist

Exam Day Timeline

  в”Җв”Җв”Җ BEFORE EXAM (Day Before) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

  вҳҗ  Test PSI Secure Browser (download + system check)
  вҳҗ  Verify webcam + microphone work
  вҳҗ  Prepare government-issued photo ID
  вҳҗ  Clear desk вҖ” nothing except computer, monitor, keyboard, mouse
  вҳҗ  Close all applications, disconnect extra monitors
  вҳҗ  Review your bookmarked kubernetes.io pages

  в”Җв”Җв”Җ EXAM DAY (30 min before) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

  вҳҗ  Close ALL apps except PSI Secure Browser
  вҳҗ  Clear browser cache
  вҳҗ  Ensure stable internet (wired recommended)
  вҳҗ  Check-in opens 15 min before scheduled time
  вҳҗ  Show room to proctor via webcam (360В° sweep)
  вҳҗ  Show desk surface and under desk

  в”Җв”Җв”Җ DURING EXAM (first 2 minutes) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

  вҳҗ  Set up aliases (paste your prepared .bashrc snippet)
  вҳҗ  Test: k get nodes (verify cluster access)
  вҳҗ  Skim all questions вҖ” identify easy wins
  вҳҗ  Start with highest-confidence questions first

  в”Җв”Җв”Җ DURING EXAM (ongoing) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

  вҳҗ  READ context switch command for EVERY question
  вҳҗ  Flag difficult questions, move on after 8 min
  вҳҗ  Verify work: kubectl get / describe after each task
  вҳҗ  Use notepad to track flagged question numbers

  в”Җв”Җв”Җ LAST 10 MINUTES в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

  вҳҗ  Return to flagged questions (highest weight first)
  вҳҗ  Verify all resources are in correct namespaces
  вҳҗ  Don't make last-minute changes without checking

вҡ пёҸ Environment Requirements

  • Clean room: No papers, books, phones, smartwatches, or second screens
  • Alone: No other people in the room during the exam
  • No headphones/earbuds вҖ” even noise-canceling
  • Browser: Only PSI Secure Browser + one kubernetes.io tab allowed
  • Talking: Do not read questions aloud or mumble вҖ” proctor may flag it
Post-Exam

Results arrive within 24 hours via email. If you don't pass, you get one free retake (must use within 12 months). Review weak areas using the domain breakdown in your score report.

рҹҺ“ Appendix F вҖ” Exam Prep Quick Reference

  • вң… Format: 2 hours, 15-20 tasks, 66% to pass, 1 free retake
  • вң… Top domains: Troubleshooting (30%) + Cluster Architecture (25%) = 55%
  • вң… Must-do first: Set up aliases + completion (saves 10+ min)
  • вң… 8 must-bookmark kubernetes.io pages for instant reference
  • вң… 15 vim commands вҖ” the only ones you need on exam day
  • вң… Essential kubectl patterns covering ~80% of exam tasks
  • вң… Best practice platform: killer.sh (2 free sessions with exam)
  • вң… Key rule: Read context switch EVERY question, verify EVERY answer

рҹ“… Appendix G: 10-Week CKA Learning Path

APPENDIX рҹ“Ӣ REFERENCE

Week-by-week study roadmap from complete beginner to CKA-certified administrator. Designed for 8вҖ“12 hours of study per week alongside a full-time job.

10-Week CKA Learning Roadmap

  WEEK   PHASE            DIFFICULTY    FOCUS AREA
  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
   1     рҹҹў Foundation    вҳ…вҳҶвҳҶвҳҶвҳҶ        Docker basics, K8s concepts, setup
   2     рҹҹў Foundation    вҳ…вҳ…вҳҶвҳҶвҳҶ        kubectl mastery, pods, namespaces

   3     рҹҹЎ Intermediate  вҳ…вҳ…вҳҶвҳҶвҳҶ        Deployments, ReplicaSets, Services
   4     рҹҹЎ Intermediate  вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳҶвҳҶ        ConfigMaps, Secrets, Volumes, PV/PVC
   5     рҹҹЎ Intermediate  вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳҶвҳҶ        Scheduling: taints, affinity, DaemonSets
   6     рҹҹЎ Intermediate  вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳҶвҳҶ        Networking: Services, Ingress, DNS, NetworkPolicy

   7     рҹҹ  Advanced      вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳҶ        RBAC, Security Contexts, ServiceAccounts
   8     рҹҹ  Advanced      вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳҶ        Cluster admin: kubeadm, upgrades, etcd backup

   9     рҹ”ҙ Expert        вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…        Troubleshooting deep-dive (30% of exam!)

  10     рҹҺҜ EXAM READY    вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…вҳ…        Mock exams, killer.sh, review weak areas

                    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ TAKE THE CKA EXAM в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
                    66% to pass в”Ӯ 1 free retake included

рҹҹў Week 1 вҖ” Foundation: Containers & Kubernetes Concepts

DayTopicChaptersHands-On
Day 1вҖ“2Docker fundamentals: images, containers, DockerfilesCh 1Build anihpj Docker image
Day 3вҖ“4Kubernetes architecture: control plane, worker nodes, etcdCh 2вҖ“3Install minikube or kind
Day 5вҖ“6kubectl basics: get, describe, create, delete, applyCh 4Deploy first pod, explore cluster
Day 7Review + Lab: Deploy a multi-container podCh 1вҖ“4Killercoda beginner scenarios

Goal: Comfortable running kubectl commands, understand pods, can explain K8s architecture on a whiteboard.

рҹҹў Week 2 вҖ” Foundation: kubectl Mastery & Core Objects

DayTopicChaptersHands-On
Day 1вҖ“2Namespaces, labels, selectors, annotationsCh 5Organize resources by namespace
Day 3вҖ“4Pod lifecycle, restart policies, init containersCh 6Create pods with init containers
Day 5вҖ“6Imperative vs declarative, dry-run, YAML generationCh 4, App BGenerate all resource YAML imperatively
Day 7Speed drill: Create 10 different resources in 15 minutesReviewTime yourself with App B commands

Goal: Can create any basic resource imperatively in under 30 seconds. Comfortable editing YAML.

рҹҹЎ Week 3 вҖ” Workloads: Deployments, Scaling & Updates

DayTopicChaptersHands-On
Day 1вҖ“2Deployments, ReplicaSets, rolling updates, rollbacksCh 7вҖ“8Deploy anihpj, perform rolling update
Day 3вҖ“4Services: ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer, ExternalNameCh 16Expose anihpj via each service type
Day 5вҖ“6Probes: liveness, readiness, startupCh 9Add health checks to anihpj deployment
Day 7Lab: Rolling update with zero downtime using probesCh 7вҖ“9, 16Full deployment cycle end-to-end

Goal: Can deploy, scale, update, and rollback, with services and health checks. Covers 15% of exam (Workloads).

рҹҹЎ Week 4 вҖ” Configuration & Storage

DayTopicChaptersHands-On
Day 1вҖ“2ConfigMaps and Secrets: creation, injection, mountingCh 10вҖ“11Inject DB config into anihpj pods
Day 3вҖ“4Volumes: emptyDir, hostPath, PV, PVC, StorageClassCh 13вҖ“14Set up PostgreSQL with persistent storage
Day 5вҖ“6Resource requests/limits, LimitRange, ResourceQuotaCh 12Configure resource constraints for anihpj namespace
Day 7Lab: Complete anihpj storage setup (E.1вҖ“E.5)App EApply manifests E.1 through E.5

Goal: Storage (10% of exam) mastered. Can create PV/PVC, mount ConfigMaps, manage Secrets.

рҹҹЎ Week 5 вҖ” Scheduling & Workload Controllers

DayTopicChaptersHands-On
Day 1вҖ“2nodeSelector, node affinity, pod affinity/anti-affinityCh 15Schedule pods to specific nodes
Day 3вҖ“4Taints & tolerations, DaemonSets, static podsCh 15Create DaemonSet, taint/untaint nodes
Day 5вҖ“6Jobs, CronJobs, HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler)Ch 8, 12Run backup CronJob, set up HPA for anihpj
Day 7Lab: Multi-constraint scheduling scenarioCh 15KodeKloud scheduling labs

Goal: Scheduling domain complete. Can control pod placement with any constraint type.

рҹҹЎ Week 6 вҖ” Networking Deep Dive

DayTopicChaptersHands-On
Day 1вҖ“2Kubernetes networking model, CNI, pod-to-pod communicationCh 17Trace network traffic between pods
Day 3вҖ“4Ingress: controllers, rules, TLS terminationCh 18Install nginx ingress, route to anihpj
Day 5вҖ“6NetworkPolicies: ingress/egress rules, default denyCh 19Implement anihpj network policies (E.8)
Day 7Lab: DNS debugging + NetworkPolicy scenariosCh 17вҖ“19CoreDNS troubleshooting, nslookup tests

Goal: Networking (20% of exam) mastered. Can create Ingress, NetworkPolicies, debug DNS.

рҹҹ  Week 7 вҖ” Security: RBAC & Pod Security

DayTopicChaptersHands-On
Day 1вҖ“2RBAC: Roles, ClusterRoles, RoleBindings, ClusterRoleBindingsCh 22Create least-privilege RBAC for anihpj SA
Day 3вҖ“4ServiceAccounts, token mounting, user certificates (CSR)Ch 23Create user cert, approve CSR, test access
Day 5вҖ“6SecurityContexts, Pod Security Standards, admissionCh 24Enforce restricted PSS on anihpj namespace
Day 7Lab: Full RBAC scenario + security auditCh 22вҖ“24KodeKloud security labs

Goal: Can create RBAC policies, manage SAs, enforce pod security. Critical for Cluster Architecture domain (25%).

рҹҹ  Week 8 вҖ” Cluster Administration

DayTopicChaptersHands-On
Day 1вҖ“2kubeadm: cluster init, token management, node joinCh 25Build a 3-node cluster from scratch with kubeadm
Day 3вҖ“4Cluster upgrades: control plane + worker node upgradeCh 26Upgrade cluster from v1.30 вҶ’ v1.31
Day 5вҖ“6etcd backup & restore, node maintenance (cordon/drain/uncordon)Ch 26Practice etcd snapshot + restore cycle
Day 7Lab: Full cluster lifecycle: build вҶ’ upgrade вҶ’ backup вҶ’ restoreCh 25вҖ“26End-to-end cluster admin practice

Goal: Cluster Architecture (25%) mastered. Can init, upgrade, backup, and restore clusters.

рҹ”ҙ Week 9 вҖ” Troubleshooting (30% of Exam!)

DayTopicChaptersHands-On
Day 1вҖ“2Application failures: CrashLoopBackOff, ImagePullBackOff, OOMKilledCh 27вҖ“28Debug broken deployments (logs, events, describe)
Day 3вҖ“4Cluster component failures: API server, scheduler, etcd, kubeletCh 29Break and fix control plane components
Day 5вҖ“6Networking issues: Service not routing, DNS failure, NetworkPolicy blockingCh 30вҖ“31Debug service connectivity, trace traffic path
Day 7Lab: "Fix 10 broken clusters" challengeCh 27вҖ“31KodeKloud troubleshooting labs

Goal: Troubleshooting (30%!) mastered. This is the HIGHEST-WEIGHT domain. Can diagnose and fix any cluster issue systematically.

рҹҺҜ Week 10 вҖ” Mock Exams & Final Review

DayTopicResourceTarget
Day 1killer.sh Session 1 вҖ” Full mock examkiller.shComplete in <2 hours, score 70%+
Day 2Review killer.sh mistakes, study weak areasNotesZero knowledge gaps
Day 3вҖ“4Speed drills: imperative commands, YAML generationApp B, CAll common tasks in <2 min each
Day 5killer.sh Session 2 вҖ” Second mock examkiller.shScore 80%+ with time to spare
Day 6Light review: aliases, bookmarks, exam checklist (App F)App FFeel confident and prepared
Day 7рҹҺҜ TAKE THE CKA EXAMPSI Secure BrowserPass with 66%+!

Goal: Walk into the exam confident. You've built clusters, deployed apps, debugged failures, and aced mock exams.

Cumulative Exam Coverage by Week

  Week     Domains Covered                              Cumulative %
  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ            в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

     3     + Workloads & Scheduling (partial)                 10%
     4     + Storage (complete!)                               20%
     5     + Workloads & Scheduling (complete!)               25%
     6     + Services & Networking (complete!)                45%
     7     + Cluster Architecture (partial: RBAC)             55%
     8     + Cluster Architecture (complete!)                  70%  в—Җв”Җв”Җ PASS LINE
     9     + Troubleshooting (complete!)                      100%

  в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ
  0%              25%              50%            в–І75%           100%
                                                 в”Ӯ
                                           66% pass line

Pacing Tip: If you can only study 5 hrs/week instead of 10, extend to a 15-week plan вҖ” but don't rush Weeks 8-9 (Cluster Admin + Troubleshooting = 55% of the exam).

рҹҸҒ Milestone Checkpoints вҖ” "Am I On Track?"

At each milestone, you should be able to complete every item without looking at notes. If not, review before moving forward.

AfterYou Should Be Able To...Self-Test Command
Week 2Create any pod/deployment imperatively in <30 seck run test --image=nginx --port=80 $do
Week 4Create PV+PVC+Pod with mounted volume from memoryk explain pvc.spec вҶ’ write YAML without docs
Week 6Write a NetworkPolicy that allows only specific ingressk create -f netpol.yaml вҶ’ test with curl
Week 8Upgrade a cluster and backup/restore etcd blindfoldedFull kubeadm upgrade + etcdctl snapshot cycle
Week 9Diagnose a broken pod in <2 minutesk get/describe/logs вҶ’ identify root cause вҶ’ fix
Week 10Score 80%+ on killer.sh with 20+ min remainingComplete all killer.sh tasks under exam conditions

вҡ пёҸ Falling Behind? вҖ” Recovery Strategies

  • 1 week behind: Skip the review lab days вҖ” focus on core content only, do labs on weekends
  • 2+ weeks behind: Extend to a 12-week plan, but never skip Weeks 8 (Cluster Admin) or 9 (Troubleshooting)
  • Struggling with a topic: Watch a KodeKloud video on it + do 3 Killercoda scenarios. Don't spend more than 2 extra days per topic
  • Not confident at Week 10: Postpone the exam (you can reschedule). It's better to take an extra 2 weeks than to burn your free retake

рҹҺҜ CKA Readiness Self-Assessment (Score Yourself)

Rate each skill 0-3: 0=Can't do it, 1=Need docs, 2=Can do it slowly, 3=Muscle memory. Target: 45+ out of 60 (75%)

DomainWeightSkillScore /3
рҹ”§ Troubleshoot30%Debug CrashLoopBackOff / ImagePullBackOff pods___
Fix broken control plane (API server, scheduler, etcd)___
Debug Service connectivity (no endpoints, wrong selector)___
Read and interpret kubectl logs / events / describe___
рҹҸ—пёҸ Cluster25%Create RBAC (Role, RoleBinding, ClusterRole) from scratch___
Upgrade cluster with kubeadm (control plane + workers)___
Backup and restore etcd snapshot___
Cordon, drain, uncordon nodes for maintenance___
рҹҢҗ Network20%Create Services (ClusterIP, NodePort) and Ingress___
Write NetworkPolicy with specific ingress/egress rules___
Debug DNS with nslookup / dig from a pod___
Understand CoreDNS FQDN format for cross-namespace access___
рҹ“Ұ Workloads15%Create Deployments, scale, rolling update, rollback___
Schedule pods with nodeSelector, affinity, taints/tolerations___
Create DaemonSets, Jobs, CronJobs___
Configure resource requests/limits, LimitRange, ResourceQuota___
рҹ’ҫ Storage10%Create PV + PVC + Pod that mounts the volume___
Use StorageClass for dynamic provisioning___
Mount ConfigMap and Secret as volumes___
Understand accessModes (RWO, ROX, RWX) and reclaimPolicy___
рҹ“Ҡ How to Interpret Your Score

50-60: You're ready вҖ” schedule the exam with confidence.
40-49: Almost there вҖ” spend 1 more week on weak areas, then take it.
30-39: Need more practice вҖ” focus on the domain(s) where you scored lowest.
<30: Keep studying вҖ” you'll get there, but don't rush the exam.

рҹ“… Appendix G вҖ” Learning Path Summary

  • вң… Weeks 1-2: Foundation вҖ” Docker, architecture, kubectl basics (green)
  • вң… Weeks 3-6: Core domains вҖ” Workloads, Storage, Networking (yellow)
  • вң… Weeks 7-8: Advanced вҖ” RBAC, Security, Cluster Admin (orange)
  • вң… Week 9: Troubleshooting вҖ” highest exam weight at 30% (red)
  • вң… Week 10: Mock exams + CKA exam day! (purple)
  • вң… 6 milestone checkpoints with self-test commands to verify progress
  • вң… 20-skill readiness self-assessment mapped to exam domain weights
  • вң… Total effort: ~80-120 hours over 10 weeks

рҹҺҜ Appendix H: CKA Exam Simulator вҖ” 20 Performance-Based Tasks

APPENDIX рҹ“қ EXAM SIMULATOR

Simulate the real CKA exam: 20 performance-based tasks, weighted by domain, using the anihpj project. Each task matches the difficulty, format, and time pressure of the actual exam. Target: complete all 20 in under 120 minutes. Answers use <details> вҖ” try each task before revealing the solution.

CKA Exam Simulator вҖ” Domain Distribution

  Domain                                    Weight    Tasks    Questions
  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ    в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
  рҹ”§ Cluster Architecture, Install & Config  25%      5       H.1-H.5
  рҹ“Ұ Workloads & Scheduling                  15%      3       H.6-H.8
  рҹҢҗ Services & Networking                   20%      4       H.9-H.12
  рҹ’ҫ Storage                                  10%      2       H.13-H.14
  рҹ”Қ Troubleshooting                          30%      6       H.15-H.20

  Total: 20 tasks | Time: 120 minutes | Pass: 66% (вүҘ14 correct)

  вҸұпёҸ Suggested Pace:
  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
  в”Ӯ Easy (2-3 min each)  в”Ӯ Medium (5-7 min)  в”Ӯ Hard (8-10 min)    в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ H.1, H.6, H.9, H.13 в”Ӯ H.2-H.5, H.7-H.8 в”Ӯ H.15-H.20          в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ        ~12 min       в”Ӯ H.10-H.12, H.14   в”Ӯ (troubleshooting)  в”Ӯ
  в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ     ~56 min        в”Ӯ     ~52 min        в”Ӯ
  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Before You Start: Set up your aliases first (just like the real exam): alias k=kubectl, export do="--dry-run=client -o yaml", export now="--force --grace-period 0". Then set your context: kubectl config use-context k8s.

рҹ”§ Domain 1: Cluster Architecture, Installation & Configuration (25%)

Tasks H.1 through H.5 вҖ” RBAC, kubeadm upgrades, etcd backup/restore, cluster components.

H.1 RBAC вҖ” Create a Scoped ServiceAccount [5 min]

рҹ“Ӣ Task

Create a ServiceAccount deploy-sa in namespace anihpj. Create a Role named deploy-role that allows get, list, watch, create, update on deployments in the apps API group. Bind the Role to the ServiceAccount using a RoleBinding named deploy-binding.

рҹ’Ў Hints
Approach

Use kubectl create imperatively for speed. Remember: --resource=deployments needs --verb= and the API group is apps (not empty string).

вң… Solution
solution-h1.sh 
# Step 1: Create the ServiceAccount
kubectl create sa deploy-sa -n anihpj

# Step 2: Create the Role (note --resource and API group)
kubectl create role deploy-role \
  --verb=get,list,watch,create,update \
  --resource=deployments.apps \
  -n anihpj

# Step 3: Create the RoleBinding
kubectl create rolebinding deploy-binding \
  --role=deploy-role \
  --serviceaccount=anihpj:deploy-sa \
  -n anihpj

# Step 4: Verify
kubectl auth can-i create deployments --as=system:serviceaccount:anihpj:deploy-sa -n anihpj
# Expected: yes

kubectl auth can-i delete deployments --as=system:serviceaccount:anihpj:deploy-sa -n anihpj
# Expected: no (delete was not granted)

вҡ пёҸ Common Mistakes

  • Forgetting the .apps API group suffix вҖ” --resource=deployments alone targets the wrong API group
  • Using --serviceaccount=deploy-sa without the namespace prefix вҖ” must be anihpj:deploy-sa
  • Creating a ClusterRole instead of Role вҖ” the task says namespace-scoped

H.2 etcd Backup [7 min]

рҹ“Ӣ Task

Create a snapshot of the etcd database and save it to /opt/etcd-backup/snapshot.db. The etcd server is running on the control plane node with the following endpoints and certificates:

etcd connection details 
# Endpoint
--endpoints=https://127.0.0.1:2379

# Certificates (find these in /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/)
--cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt
--cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt
--key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key
рҹ’Ў Hints
Approach

Use etcdctl snapshot save. Set ETCDCTL_API=3 first. If you forget the cert paths, check the etcd static pod manifest: cat /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml.

вң… Solution
solution-h2.sh 
# Ensure etcdctl API version 3
export ETCDCTL_API=3

# Create backup directory
mkdir -p /opt/etcd-backup

# Take the snapshot
etcdctl snapshot save /opt/etcd-backup/snapshot.db \
  --endpoints=https://127.0.0.1:2379 \
  --cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt \
  --cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt \
  --key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key

# Verify the snapshot
etcdctl snapshot status /opt/etcd-backup/snapshot.db --write-table

вҡ пёҸ CKA Trap

  • Forgetting ETCDCTL_API=3 вҖ” version 2 uses different commands
  • Using wrong cert files вҖ” server.crt/server.key for etcdctl, NOT peer.crt
  • Not creating the output directory first вҖ” the command will fail silently

H.3 etcd Restore [8 min]

рҹ“Ӣ Task

A previous etcd snapshot exists at /opt/etcd-backup/snapshot.db. Restore etcd from this snapshot to a new data directory /var/lib/etcd-restored. Then update the etcd static pod to use the restored data directory.

рҹ’Ў Hints
Approach

Two steps: (1) etcdctl snapshot restore to a new directory, (2) edit /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml to point --data-dir and the hostPath volume to the new path. The kubelet will auto-restart etcd.

вң… Solution
solution-h3.sh 
# Step 1: Restore snapshot to new directory
export ETCDCTL_API=3
etcdctl snapshot restore /opt/etcd-backup/snapshot.db \
  --data-dir=/var/lib/etcd-restored

# Step 2: Edit the etcd static pod manifest
# Change --data-dir flag AND the hostPath volume
sudo vi /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml

# In the container spec, change:
#   - --data-dir=/var/lib/etcd
# To:
#   - --data-dir=/var/lib/etcd-restored

# In the volumes section, change:
#   hostPath:
#     path: /var/lib/etcd
# To:
#   hostPath:
#     path: /var/lib/etcd-restored

# Step 3: Wait for etcd to restart (kubelet detects manifest change)
# Verify:
kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep etcd
kubectl get nodes  # Should show Ready

вҡ пёҸ Critical: TWO Changes Required

  • You must change BOTH the --data-dir flag AND the hostPath.path in the volume mount
  • If you only change one, etcd will fail to start and the entire cluster goes down
  • Don't panic if kubectl stops working temporarily вҖ” the API server depends on etcd
  • Wait 1-2 minutes for the kubelet to detect the manifest change and restart the pod

H.4 Cluster Upgrade вҖ” Control Plane [10 min]

рҹ“Ӣ Task

Upgrade the control plane node controlplane from Kubernetes v1.30.0 to v1.31.0. Upgrade kubeadm first, then the control plane components, then kubelet and kubectl. Ensure the node is schedulable after the upgrade.

рҹ’Ў Hints
Approach

Order matters: (1) upgrade kubeadm, (2) kubeadm upgrade plan, (3) kubeadm upgrade apply, (4) drain node, (5) upgrade kubelet+kubectl, (6) restart kubelet, (7) uncordon. On Debian/Ubuntu, use apt-mark unhold вҶ’ install вҶ’ apt-mark hold.

вң… Solution
solution-h4.sh 
# Step 1: Upgrade kubeadm
sudo apt-mark unhold kubeadm
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-*
sudo apt-mark hold kubeadm

# Step 2: Verify upgrade plan
sudo kubeadm upgrade plan

# Step 3: Apply the upgrade
sudo kubeadm upgrade apply v1.31.0

# Step 4: Drain the control plane node
kubectl drain controlplane --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data

# Step 5: Upgrade kubelet and kubectl
sudo apt-mark unhold kubelet kubectl
sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-* kubectl=1.31.0-*
sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubectl

# Step 6: Restart kubelet
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl restart kubelet

# Step 7: Uncordon the node
kubectl uncordon controlplane

# Step 8: Verify
kubectl get nodes
# controlplane should show v1.31.0 and Ready

вҡ пёҸ Upgrade Order is Critical

  • Always upgrade kubeadm before running kubeadm upgrade apply
  • Always upgrade kubelet after the control plane components
  • Never skip minor versions вҖ” upgrade one version at a time (1.30вҶ’1.31, not 1.29вҶ’1.31)
  • Don't forget systemctl daemon-reload before restarting kubelet

H.5 Cluster Upgrade вҖ” Worker Node [7 min]

рҹ“Ӣ Task

Upgrade worker node worker-1 to Kubernetes v1.31.0. The control plane has already been upgraded. Ensure workloads are safely evicted during the upgrade and the node is schedulable afterwards.

рҹ’Ў Hints
Approach

From the control plane: drain the worker. SSH to the worker: upgrade kubeadm, run kubeadm upgrade node (NOT apply), upgrade kubelet+kubectl, restart. Back on control plane: uncordon.

вң… Solution
solution-h5.sh 
# FROM CONTROL PLANE:
# Step 1: Drain the worker node
kubectl drain worker-1 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data

# SSH TO WORKER NODE:
ssh worker-1

# Step 2: Upgrade kubeadm
sudo apt-mark unhold kubeadm
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-*
sudo apt-mark hold kubeadm

# Step 3: Upgrade node config (NOT "apply" вҖ” that's for control plane only)
sudo kubeadm upgrade node

# Step 4: Upgrade kubelet and kubectl
sudo apt-mark unhold kubelet kubectl
sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-* kubectl=1.31.0-*
sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubectl

# Step 5: Restart kubelet
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl restart kubelet

# Step 6: Exit back to control plane
exit

# FROM CONTROL PLANE:
# Step 7: Uncordon the worker
kubectl uncordon worker-1

# Verify
kubectl get nodes
# worker-1 should show v1.31.0 and Ready

вҡ пёҸ Worker vs Control Plane Difference

  • Workers use kubeadm upgrade node вҖ” NOT kubeadm upgrade apply
  • You must SSH to the worker to run upgrade commands вҖ” kubectl runs on control plane
  • Drain happens from control plane, upgrade happens on the worker node itself
